summary: Formation of hydroxyapatite; undesired on blood-contacting biomaterials due to thrombosis and device degradation.

label: apoptosis
summary: A process of programmed cell death that can be initiated by FE-DBD plasma treatment in melanoma cancer cells.

label: Arcing
summary: Electrical arcing during DC reactive sputtering due to insulating film deposition on chamber surfaces.

label: areal coverage ratio b/p
summary: Ratio b/p representing areal coverage of islands on the surface.

label: Argon plasma
summary: argon (Ar) plasma used to ionize evaporated metal during reactive evaporation and ARE processes.

label: Arrhenius behavior
summary: Temperature dependence of diffusion constants observed experimentally.

label: Arrhenius relation / Arrhenius plot
summary: Plot of ln(P) vs 1/T used to extract thermodynamic parameters; slope relates to ΔH°.

label: Arrival rate of atoms at a surface
summary: Rate R of atoms arriving at a surface in a vacuum chamber; R = n v̄ / 4 = p/(2π m k T)^(1/2).

label: Asaro-Tiller-Grinfeld instability
summary: Instability of a nominally flat, stressed solid surface under perturbations; described by Asaro–Tiller–Grinfeld framework.

label: Associated flow rule
summary: Plastic strain rate is normal to the yield surface, following the normality principle.

label: Associative Desorption
summary: The process where adsorbed species A:S desorb back into the gas phase as AS.

label: Associative Ionization
summary: A process where ionization occurs when heavy species collide and form a molecular ion, even if their total electronic excitation energy is insufficient.

label: Atom impingement rate (C)
summary: Rate at which atoms impinge onto nuclei per cm²·s.

label: Atom peening
summary: Surface damage mechanism contributing to compressive stresses during sputter deposition.

label: Atomic-peening mechanism
summary: Energetic particle bombardment causes compressive film stress (atomic peening).

label: Atomic units (a.u.)
summary: System of units used in atomic physics; includes Bohr radius and Hartree energy.

label: Attenuation length (AL)
summary: Attenuation length used in layer-growth analysis; shorter than IMFP due to elastic scattering.

label: Au–Co phase diagram
summary: Au–Co equilibrium phase diagram showing simple eutectic behavior with a eutectic composition around 23.5 at.% Co and eutectic temperature about 996°C.

label: Auger attenuation length
summary: Finite Auger attenuation length influences interpretation of Auger data at high spatial resolution.

label: Auger electron emission
summary: Auger electron emission observed in AES; spectrum often differentiated to sharpen peaks.

label: Auger electron spectroscopy
summary: AES; characterization method for core-electron binding energies in semiconductors.

label: Auger-electron tunneling transitions
summary: Electronic excitations during ion-solid interactions leading to Auger-electron tunneling.

label: Autoadhesion
summary: Adhesion phenomenon of elastomers; referenced in context of adhesive strategies.

label: Avalanche breakdown
summary: avalanche breakdown observed in the device at higher voltages, related to carrier multiplication.

label: Avogadro's number NA
summary: Avogadro's number NA = 6.0221367 (636) × 10^23 mol^-1.

label: Avrami equation
summary: Kinetic model f = 1 - exp(- (Kt)^n) used to analyze transformation data.

label: Azeotrope
summary: Composition at which liquid and solid phases have identical composition in a mixed-crystal system; may appear as a minimum/maximum in the liquidus.

label: Back-bond surface resonance
summary: Resonance localized between the first and second atomic layers; strengthens compensatory bonding at the surface.

label: Back-donation
summary: Chemisorption mechanism where π-character is transferred into the metal via covalent interactions.

label: Back-EMF
summary: Back-electromotive force proportional to rotor speed in motor-drive modeling.

label: Back-emf (e_A)
summary: Back electromotive force induced in the armature windings due to rotation in the stator field.

label: Back-emf in rotor (E_AG)
summary: Back-emf induced in the rotor due to relative motion with the stator field.

label: back ionization wave
summary: An ionization wave propagating from cathode to anode with velocities up to 10^9 cm/s, occurring during spark discharge.

label: Backscattered neutral bombardment
summary: Secondary role in generating compressive stress in sputtered films.

label: Backscattering
summary: Back-scattering variations affect Auger signal and image contrast (e.g., Ag islands vs. W substrate).

label: Backscattering by LO phonons
summary: Selection rules for backscattering by LO phonons for LO-phonon processes in GaAs/AlAs MQWs.

label: Backscattering factor
summary: Factor describing backscattered electrons in AES; influences signal proportionality.

label: α-BaCl2 ↔ β-BaCl2 phase transition
summary: First-order phase transition between α-BaCl2 and β-BaCl2 occurring around 925.3 °C.

label: Bactericidal Effect
summary: The strong effect observed during plasma treatment that leads to the inactivation of bacteria with minimal changes to biological tissues.

label: Baliga’s BFOM expression
summary: BFOM = ε_r ε_0 μ_n E_critical^3.

label: Baliga’s figure of merit (BFM)
summary: Figure of merit for power devices; BF M = BV^2 / RONS; also expressed as 1/(4 μp εr ε0 Em^3).

label: Baliga’s figure of merit (BFOM)
summary: BFOM = ε_r ε_0 μ_n E_critical^3.

label: Baliga’s high-frequency figure of merit (BHFFOM)
summary: BHFFOM = 1 / (R_ideal C_I) with C_I = (ε_r ε_0 q N_D / (2 V_G))^0.5.

label: Ballistic mixing
summary: Mixing driven by ballistic motion of defects during ion bombardment.

label: Bamboo grain structure
summary: Grain configuration with through-thickness boundary paths that impedes diffusion and increases electromigration life.

label: Band alignment types
summary: Classifications of valence/conduction band alignment: Type I (straddling), Type II (staggered), Type III (broken-gap).

label: band bending
summary: Variation of band edges near the surface to maintain constant Fermi level from bulk to surface, leading to depletion or enrichment layers.

label: Band bending at semiconductor surfaces
summary: Band bending due to surface states pinning the Fermi level; depletion region forms and depends on surface state distribution and doping type.

label: Band gap
summary: A characteristic of single-crystal diamond that divides it into conduction and valence bands.

label: Band offset
summary: Relative alignment of the band gaps at semiconductor heterojunctions; midgap alignment often drives zero net dipole conditions.

label: band offsets
summary: Discontinuities in energy bands at the GaAs/AlGaAs interface.

label: Band structure
summary: Electronic band structure of diamond- and zinc-blende-type crystals calculated via tight-binding / LCAO approach; 8x8 matrix for s and p bands.

label: Bandgap energy
summary: Bandgap energy of SiC; associated with junction drop in SiC pin diodes (~3 eV, ~3 V).

label: Barrier height (φ_B) at oxide/SiC interface
summary: Conduction-band offset between oxide and SiC, estimated from FN plots and XPS/IPE measurements; varies with surface orientation.

label: Barrier height (Φ_B0)
summary: Barrier height of the Schottky barrier; Ti diodes have lower Φ_B0 than Ni diodes, affecting reverse leakage.

label: Basal plane dislocation
summary: Edge dislocation lying in the basal plane [0001] in GaN/SiC crystals.

label: Basal plane dislocation (BPD)
summary: Major extended defect in SiC with Burgers vector <1120>/3; in {0001} plane (predominantly <1120>).

label: Basal plane dislocations
summary: Deformation-induced dislocations on the basal plane observed in 4H- and 6H-SiC.

label: Basal plane dislocations (BPD)
summary: Dislocations with Burgers vector [1120]/3; basal slip; replication in boule; conversion with TED.

label: Basal plane dislocations (BPDs)
summary: Dislocations in the basal plane of SiC; densities affected by growth temperature and doping and can be related to TSDs; observed as basal slip bands.

label: Base spreading resistance
summary: Resistance in the base causing a lateral voltage drop as gate current flows through the base during turn-off.

label: Base transport factor
summary: Fraction of electrons from the emitter that diffuse across the base to the collector; α_T = 1/ cosh(W/L NB).

label: Bauschinger effect
summary: Asymmetry of plastic yielding upon reversal of stress direction due to back stress.

label: BCF theory
summary: Burton, Cabrera, and Frank surface diffusion model referenced in the theoretical section.

label: Beam curling
summary: Electron-beam trajectory curling due to improper magnetic field; can cause shifting vapor deposition.

label: Bethe-Bloch formula
summary: A formula used to describe energy losses of charged particles as they pass through matter.

label: Bethe loss law
summary: Energy-loss dependence for inelastic electron scattering (dE/dx).

label: BHFFOM expression
summary: BHFFOM = 1 / (R_ideal C_I); related to mobility, E_critical, etc.

label: bi-Maxwellian distributions
summary: A distribution of electron energies indicating more efficient electron power absorption.

label: Bias Current
summary: The current emitted by the diamond nuclei towards the plasma, correlated with the voltage applied.

label: Bias Enhanced Nucleation on Ir (BEN)
summary: BEN on iridium leads to high nucleation density and well-aligned diamond nucleation.

label: Biaxial modulus of film (M_f)
summary: Biaxial modulus relating in-plane stress and strain in the film.

label: Biaxial modulus of substrate (M_s)
summary: Biaxial modulus relating in-plane stress and strain in the substrate.

label: Biaxial stress in thin films
summary: Biaxial tensile/compressive stress observed in thin films (balanced, unbalanced, or mixed components) as shown by interference fringe patterns.

label: Bifurcation of substrate curvature
summary: Transition in deformation state (bifurcation) of the substrate midplane as mismatch strain changes

label: Biharmonic equation
summary: Governs equilibrium and compatibility of the 2D elastic stress/strain field in the Airy formulation.

label: Binary phase diagram
summary: Phase diagram describing a two-component A–B system, showing solidus and liquidus boundaries and eutectic behavior.

label: Bipolar degradation
summary: Degradation in SiC bipolar devices caused by expansion of SSFs leading to reduced carrier lifetimes and increased forward voltage, with leakage current paths.

label: Bipolar-type defect (BPD)
summary: Extended defect involving partial dislocations and stacking faults that can expand under carrier injection.

label: Birefringence
summary: Optical anisotropy (birefringence) including strain-induced and intrinsic birefringence in cubic semiconductors.

label: Bloch function
summary: Psi_k(x) = e^{ikx} u_k(x) with u_k periodic; eigenfunction of translation and Hamiltonian in a periodic potential.

label: Bloch functions at Gamma
summary: Wave functions at Γ transform according to irreducible representations of Td.

label: Bloch oscillations
summary: Oscillatory electron motion in a periodic potential under an external field.

label: Bloch wave (surface state)
summary: Surface-state form described as a Bloch wave in the two dimensions of the surface.

label: Bloch waves
summary: Wave function form Psi(x) = e^{ikx}u(x) for periodic potentials.

label: blocking of a threading dislocation
summary: Retardation or blocking of threading dislocation by an interface misfit dislocation; involves a critical thickness criterion.

label: Blocking voltage
summary: Maximum reverse voltage before breakdown for a p+-n- junction; related to breakdown field.

label: blood coagulation
summary: The process of blood clotting, which can be stimulated by non-thermal plasma.

label: Bohm criterion
summary: Criterion related to sheath formation in plasma; referenced in context of plasma etching.

label: Bohr radius (a0)
summary: Atomic physics length scale; a0 = 0.529177249 Å.

label: Bohr–Sommerfeld quantization condition
summary: Quantum condition requiring areas enclosed by cyclotron orbits in k-space to be multiples of ħ; real-space orbits enclose integral number of flux quanta.

label: Boltzmann distribution
summary: Boltzmann distribution for nondegenerate electrons used in transport derivations.

label: Boltzmann equation
summary: Equation for the electron distribution function in the plasma used to model the discharge.

label: Boltzmann factor for thermal activation
summary: Probability factor for overcoming barriers by thermal fluctuations: exp(−ΔG*/kT).

label: Bond integral h
summary: Bond integral parameter in tight-binding models, related to coupling between orbitals.

label: Bond-order potentials
summary: Empirical potentials that incorporate bond-order effects to model covalent bonding.

label: Bond reconstruction in dislocation cores
summary: Computer simulations suggest core energy is reduced by reconstruction—dangling bonds rebonding along the core (e.g., CCCC rebonding).

label: Bonding and anti-bonding orbitals
summary: Orbitals formed from overlapping atomic wavefunctions; energy splitting into bonding and anti-bonding states.

label: Bonding and anti-bonding s-orbitals
summary: Bonding energy E_- = E_s - |V_ss||g_1| with bonding wavefunction ψ_B = (|S1⟩ - e^{-iπ/4}|S2⟩)/√2.

label: Bonding energy
summary: Energy released on bond formation between atoms; related to equilibrium spacing a0 and cohesive strength Eb.

label: bonding orbital
summary: Bonding molecular orbital formed from overlapping atomic orbitals.

label: σ bonds
summary: Pi-type bonds formed by side-to-side overlap of p orbitals.

label: Bone-bonding
summary: Sequence of events leading to bonding of bioactive materials to bone via adsorption and mineralization.

label: Born effective charge
summary: Dynamic transverse ionic charge e* associated with TO-phonon absorption.

label: Born-Oppenheimer approximation
summary: Approximation in molecular dynamics; its breakdown can lead to vibrational decay.

label: Born–von Kármán boundary conditions
summary: Period boundary conditions to model an infinite crystal by repeating finite crystal; equivalent to filling space with crystal replicas.

label: Boudouard reaction
summary: 2 CO ⇌ CO2 + C; explains TG peaks during CaCO3-related steps.

label: Bound exciton
summary: Exciton localized near an impurity with enhanced oscillator strength; strong Raman enhancement observed near such states.

label: Bound exciton luminescence in diamond
summary: Luminescence from bound excitons (X, B0X, P0X) in diamond; used to determine dopant densities via cathodoluminescence at low temperature.

label: Bound exciton luminescence P0X_TO
summary: Bound exciton luminescence observed in nanocrystalline, phosphorus-doped diamond (P0X_TO).

label: boundary layer
summary: Region near the wall where gas velocity changes from zero to bulk flow; inversely related to Reynolds number.

label: Bragg angle
summary: Angle at which lattice planes satisfy Bragg reflection during X-ray diffraction topography.

label: Bragg curve
summary: A characteristic shape of the specific energy loss dE/dx as a function of penetration depth in matter.

label: Bragg's Law
summary: Diffraction condition used in X-ray wavelength dispersion (WDX) analysis.

label: Bragg's law (diffraction condition)
summary: Relation d = nλ/(2 sin μ) used to determine d-spacing from diffraction measurements.

label: Branching-Chain Explosion
summary: An explosion mechanism in the CO–O2 mixture characterized by exponential growth of active species.

label: Branching point energy (E_B)
summary: Reference energy level (E_B) in MIGS model, near mid-gap, related to charge neutrality.

label: Bread-loafing
summary: Unwanted overhang or bread-loafing of films in trenches during deposition.

label: breakaway oxidation
summary: A phenomenon characterized by the degradation of the oxide film during coal conversion, leading to accelerated oxidation.

label: Breakdown electric field in diamond FETs
summary: Measured breakdown electric field around 6.2 MV/cm in diamond JFET pn-junctions, highest among diamond FETs reported.

label: Breakdown field and oxide reliability limits
summary: Oxide field guidance: keep below ~4 MV/cm for reliability; SiO2 breakdown ~10 MV/cm.

label: Breakdown field strength (E_BR)
summary: Breakdown field strength of doped CVD-diamond; estimated around 2×10^6 V/cm, with potential improvement to 8×10^6 V/cm.

label: Breakdown voltage
summary: Breakdown voltage observed around 566 V (room temperature) in diamond JFETs; breakdowns attributed to pn-junction edges.

label: Breakdown Voltage (BV)
summary: Breakdown voltage of p-i-n diodes terminated with JTE; BV degrades with increasing interface charge for uniform JTE but remains robust under CD-JTE up to a threshold.

label: Breakdown voltage close to ideal BV
summary: Edge termination strategies aim to achieve BV close to the ideal value in GaN/SiC devices.

label: Breakdown voltage (VB)
summary: Breakdown voltage as a figure of merit for high-voltage operation, related to LGD and device structure.

label: Bremsstrahlung isochromat spectroscopy
summary: BIS; inverse photoemission spectroscopy technique to study empty states.

label: Brillouin scattering
summary: Inelastic scattering of light by acoustic phonons; used to probe acoustic properties in semiconductors.

label: Brillouin zone
summary: Reciprocal-space zone in which interband transitions near the zone center and along ⟨111⟩ directions are discussed.

label: Bubble
summary: A gaseous cavity within a crystal (volume defect).

label: Buckling
summary: Buckling of a thin film strip under compressive stress; includes post-buckling behavior and circular patch buckling.

label: Buckling by debonding
summary: Film buckles by debonding from the substrate when stressed.

label: Buckling due to thermal mismatch
summary: Equibiaxial compressive mismatch stress caused by cooling; buckling predicted; onset at Tb = 228 °C.

label: Built-in potential
summary: Potential difference across the Schottky barrier formed at zero bias (V_d).

label: Bulk diffusion
summary: Diffusion of incorporated atoms through the bulk lattice to equilibrium positions.

label: Bulk hole mobility in diamond
summary: Bulk diamond exhibits high hole mobility (~2000 cm2/Vs) when not limited by 2D interface effects.

label: Bulk lifetime
summary: Carrier lifetime intrinsic to the epitaxial SiC bulk material.

label: bulk modulus B_S
summary: Bulk modulus at constant entropy B_S = -V/(∂p/∂V)_S = V/(∂^2 U/∂V^2)_S.

label: Bulk phonon scattering
summary: Phonon scattering in the bulk that increases with temperature, reducing mobility at high temperature.

label: Bulk plasmons
summary: Collective oscillations of the electron gas in the bulk; interact with surface states at appropriate energies.

label: Buoyancy flow
summary: Buoyancy-driven flow due to temperature variations (Grashof-related) in the system.

label: Burgers circuit
summary: Atom-to-atom closed path in a crystal; closure failure indicates presence of dislocations; used to define the Burgers vector.

label: Burgers vector
summary: A lattice-distortion vector associated with a dislocation; its orientation relative to an interface (and its normal component bn) governs interfacial motion such as climb or glide.

label: Burgers vector (b)
summary: Vector characterizing the lattice distortion of a dislocation; area under the distribution equals b.

label: Burgers vector distribution f(x)
summary: Derivative of Δu; the area under f(x) equals the Burgers vector b.

label: Burgers vector for cubic film dislocations
summary: Burgers vector components for dislocations; example b_i = b [1/2, -1, 1/2] for ε_m > 0.

label: c(4 × 4) surface reconstruction
summary: c(4 × 4) reconstruction observed on GaAs surface during TBAs annealing and maintained during typical MOCVD growth.

label: c-axis in-plane orientation
summary: Cr underlayer texture evolution forces Co alloy c-axis to be in-plane, the magnetic easy axis.

label: C-center
summary: A common FTIR optical center in diamonds with characteristic absorption peaks.

label: C-H-O triangle (Bachmann diagram)
summary: Triangular diagram used to understand CVD diamond growth chemistries; growth occurs near the CO tie line.

label: C–IA–IR transitions
summary: Sequence of transition between commensurate and incommensurate phases observed in Xe/Kr on surfaces.

label: C2_4(y') symmetry operation
summary: Two-fold rotation about the y' axis; part of the group of X with translations involved.

label: C2 emission in plasma
summary: Intense light emission from C2 species observed in the plasma discharge.

label: C2 rotation
summary: Two-fold rotation about one of the three mutually perpendicular [100], [010], or [001] axes.

label: C2(x) rotation
summary: Rotation about the x-axis; y and z change sign under this operation.

label: C2v point group
summary: Symmetry group for the (100) surface of diamond/zincblende crystals; contains C2 rotation and reflections across specific planes.

label: C3^{-1} rotation
summary: Rotation by 120 degrees counterclockwise about the same axis as C3.

label: C3 rotation
summary: Rotation by 120 degrees about one of the four C–H bonds.

label: C3 rotation about [111] axis
summary: Symmetry operation used in the analysis; example given for transformation of coordinates.

label: C3v point group
summary: Symmetry group for the (111) surface; two non-equivalent flat (111) surfaces; one with dangling bonds.

label: Canonical ensemble
summary: Microscopic distribution for systems at fixed T, V, N.

label: Capacitance of the space-charge region
summary: Per-unit-area capacitance in the depletion region of SBDs; used to determine built-in potential from 1/C^2 vs V plots.

label: Capacitance-Voltage (C-V) characteristics
summary: C-V behavior of SPND; C constant below ~1.2 V; depletion width inferred from C.

label: Capillarity theory of heterogeneous nucleation
summary: Capillarity theory extended to heterogeneous nucleation on a substrate; relates ΔG, r, and interfacial energies.

label: Capillary forces
summary: Forces arising from liquid menisci that can cause stiction between nearby surfaces.

label: Capillary waves
summary: Capillary waves on the helium surface excited by radio frequency driving; the motion excites these waves and is used to probe melting.

label: Capture cross section (σ_N)
summary: Capture cross section of interface traps for electrons; decreases exponentially with energy toward the conduction/majority carrier band edge; energy dependence described by γ.

label: carbide compound formation
summary: Enhanced by Silicon presence, leading to lower delamination probability in diamond films.

label: Carbon accumulation near interface
summary: during high-temperature Ni sintering, carbon accumulates near the Ni/SiC interface and can move to the surface

label: Carbon antisite–carbon vacancy pair (C_Si–V_C)
summary: Defect pair consisting of a carbon antisite and a carbon vacancy in 4H-SiC.

label: Carbon di-interstitial ((Ci)2)
summary: Carbon di-interstitial defect; proposed origin for HK0 center.

label: Carbon diffusion to surface
summary: carbon diffuses/moves toward the surface during annealing, contributing to ohmic behavior

label: Carbon interstitial (Ci)
summary: Carbon interstitial defect in SiC; high diffusion at elevated temperatures; implicated in HK0 center.

label: Carbon–nickel solid-solution reaction
summary: Solid-solution reaction between carbon and nickel responsible for selective diamond etching and formation of atomically flat surfaces.

label: Carbon split interstitial ((C_2)_C)
summary: Dicarbon interstitial defect in 4H-SiC where a C-C pair occupies a carbon site.

label: Carbon vacancy (V_C)
summary: Carbon vacancy defect in 4H-SiC; identified as a dominant defect in some HPSI samples.

label: Carbon vacancy (VC)
summary: Carbon vacancy defect in SiC (VC) with charge states; identified as origin of Z1/2 and EH7 centers.

label: Carrier compensation
summary: The effect observed in P-doped diamond where acceptor states compensate for P donors.

label: Carrier diffusion constant
summary: Ambipolar diffusion constant used in diffusion simulations for SiC epitaxial layer and substrate.

label: Carrier recombination
summary: Recombination of charge carriers in SiC including surface, bulk, and substrate contributions.

label: Carrot defect
summary: Macroscopic defect associated with epitaxial growth; density typically 0.1–1 cm−2.

label: Carrot defect (comet defect)
summary: Defect features observed under certain growth conditions in SiC epitaxy.

label: Carrot defects
summary: Comparative extended defect type used in analysis of SFs in SiC epitaxial layers.

label: Cascade model of resonant Raman scattering
summary: Sequential-step explanation of exciton-assisted multiphonon Raman scattering via relaxation with LO phonon emission.

label: Cathode Spots
summary: Localized current centers on the cathode that appear when significant current is required but the cathode cannot be uniformly heated.

label: Cathodoluminescence
summary: Technique used to evaluate killer defects that increase leakage current.

label: cavitation damage
summary: A phenomenon affecting hydrogenated diamond surfaces more than oxygenated surfaces due to hydrophilicity.

label: Cell Proliferation
summary: The process by which cells divide and increase in number, significantly affected by plasma treatment of scaffolds.

label: Center-of-Mass Coordinates
summary: A coordinate system used to analyze collisions between particles, treating them equally.

label: center-to-center spacing p
summary: Center-to-center spacing of islands, p = L/N.

label: Central cell corrections
summary: Corrections to impurity potentials near the core affecting donor states.

label: Central cell effects
summary: Modifications of impurity states due to the atomic core (central cell) effects in doped semiconductors.

label: Channeling
summary: Effect used to distinguish RBS spectral features in crystalline vs. amorphous films and to influence dopant profiles.

label: Characteristic X-rays
summary: X-rays generated by electron-beam evaporant gun; may damage sensitive dielectrics such as SiO2.

label: Charge neutrality level (E_n)
summary: Energy where MIGS states change from valence to conduction character; central to MIGS model.

label: Charge States of Defects
summary: Defects in diamond can exist in multiple charge states affecting their behavior.

label: Chemical etching
summary: A process involving surface reactions and gasification of polymers in plasma, particularly with oxygen-containing species.

label: Chemical potential (μ)
summary: Chemical potential; energy associated with adding a particle (μ); μ appears in dU expressions.

label: Chemical potential for electrons
summary: Chemical potential for electrons, defined as the difference between the electron quasi-Fermi level and the intrinsic level.

label: Chemical potential of vapor (μ_v)
summary: Chemical potential of vapor; μ_v = h_v - Ts + kT ln(p) used in phase equilibria.

label: chemical potential Phi
summary: surface chemical potential field driving surface mass transport on S.

label: chemical reactions
summary: Reactions that occur in gas and surface phases during plasma processing.

label: Chemisorption
summary: A process where molecules adhere to a surface through chemical bonds, influenced by energy barriers.

label: χ (chi) ratio
summary: system parameter χ = γ_f / (M_f ε_m^2)

label: Child-Langmuir equation
summary: Relation describing ion current and sheath thickness in collision-free motion within the RF plasma sheath.

label: Chirality
summary: Mirror-image relationship between crystals with different space groups; property of trigonal semiconductors Se and Te.

label: Chlorine adsorption on diamond surface
summary: Abrupt increase in growth near 250°C attributed to adsorption of chlorine on the diamond surface.

label: Circular delamination
summary: Initially circular delamination observed in equi-biaxial compression of mica-aluminum bilayer; may develop wrinkles.

label: Clausius–Clapeyron equation
summary: An equation that relates the change in vapor pressure with temperature for phase equilibrium.

label: Clean interface
summary: An interface free of contaminants can achieve high fracture energy.

label: climb
summary: Diffusion-assisted movement of dislocations via vacancy emission/absorption, enabling boundary motion as a whole.

label: Closed core screw dislocation
summary: Screw dislocation with a closed hollow core; observed as large white spots in SWBXT.

label: Closed-core screw dislocations
summary: Screw dislocations with closed cores observed in SiC.

label: Co binder diffusion into substrate
summary: Carbon diffusion from the Co binder into the WC-Co substrate during deposition, influencing adhesion.

label: Co migration
summary: Migration of cobalt from the binder during diamond deposition, affecting adhesion and surface quality.

label: CO tie line in C-H-O diagram
summary: Line indicating favorable gas chemistries for diamond growth within the C-H-O diagram.

label: coalescence of diamond films
summary: The process by which diamond films grow together to form a continuous layer.

label: Coble creep
summary: Inelastic straining due to mass transport along grain boundaries (Coble creep).

label: CoCrTaPt f1011g parallel to Cr (110)
summary: CoCrTaPt grows epitaxially such that its f1011g plane is parallel to Cr (110) plane.

label: Coefficient of thermal expansion
summary: CTE, a material property describing thermal expansion.

label: Coexistence of high Tc phase and liquid
summary: High Tc phase and liquid coexistence observed between 865°C and 875°C in partial-melting experiments.

label: Coherent interface
summary: Planar defect without long-range strain fields, contributing to characteristic TEM contrast.

label: Cohesive zone model
summary: Model describing interface separation with traction depending on separation delta; Barenblatt 1959.

label: Coincidence-site lattice
summary: CSL concept describing special misorientation angles producing lattice coincidence; examples include Σ5.

label: Coincident-site lattice (CSL)
summary: Dichromatic arrangement where some lattice sites coincide between two lattices; described by the symbol Σ (e.g., Σ = 5 in the given example).

label: Cold plasma dielectric
summary: Dielectric response of a collisional, cold plasma given by ε_r = 1 - ω_p^2 / [ω(ω + i ν_e,n)].

label: Collection levels in the diamond bandgap
summary: Dopant-related collection levels in the bandgap near the conduction band that influence electron relaxation paths.

label: Collinear interaction
summary: Strong dislocation interaction contributing to τ_i.

label: Collision cross-section
summary: σ_c; circular area representing the probability of interaction between particles; larger σ_c increases collision likelihood.

label: Collisional Sheath
summary: A sheath condition where ions suffer collisions as they cross, requiring a self-consistent analysis.

label: collisionless heating
summary: Heating of electrons in a plasma due to the interaction with time-varying electric fields without collisions.

label: Collisionless Plasma Limit
summary: Condition where the frequency of collisions is much lower than the frequency of electromagnetic waves.

label: Color centers
summary: Impurities and vacancies in diamond that possess interesting optical and spin characteristics.

label: Color centers (NV and SiV) in diamond
summary: Color centers in diamond used for quantum sensing and information processing.

label: Columnar form of CVD graphite
summary: Crystallites deposited with basal planes parallel to the surface, tending to columnar growth.

label: Columnar grain structure
summary: CVD microstructure type with columnar grains and domed tops (Zone A).

label: Columnar morphology
summary: Columnar-like film structure prevails in films evaporated by the discussed methods.

label: comets
summary: rare surface features formed by local disordering; indicate two-dimensional growth disruption; observed on thicker films.

label: Commensurate (C) phase
summary: C-phase where adsorbate lattice is commensurate with graphite substrate.

label: Commensurate-incommensurate transition
summary: Structural transformation observed in adsorbed films as coverage and interactions change; highlights changes in overlayer ordering.

label: Commensurate overlayer √3 × √3 on graphite
summary: Overlayer structure with symmetry matching substrate; krypton forms such a phase at low density.

label: Commensurate solid
summary: Commensurate solid concept; listed in Acronyms.

label: Common interfacial lattice parameter
summary: Common in-plane lattice parameter at the Si/Ge0.4Si0.6 interface.

label: Compensating defects
summary: Residual acceptors and deep levels in n-type/p-type SiC; typical densities in lightly-doped 4H-SiC.

label: compliance tensor
summary: Elastic compliance tensor Sij (inverse of stiffness) used in relation to the contracted stiffness matrix.

label: Compliance tensor S_ijkl
summary: Tensor describing material compliance; symmetry analysis performed for zincblende structure.

label: Composite fermions
summary: Keyword related to fractional quantum Hall effect topics.

label: Compound interface
summary: An interfacial region formed by chemical reaction and diffusion between film and substrate; can be brittle.

label: compressive stress
summary: Stress induced at the nucleation region that propagates to the surface of the diamond.

label: Condensation–evaporation
summary: Mass exchange process on the surface affecting evolution under μ_v and U_m.

label: Conductance
summary: Piping conductance; flow rate divided by the pressure difference, affecting pumping speed.

label: Conduction
summary: Heat transfer by conduction within the SiC charge system.

label: Conduction band
summary: Band of states in which electrons can move freely to conduct electricity.

label: Conduction-band deformation potential a_c
summary: Deformation potential constant describing electron-LA phonon coupling.

label: Conduction band E_C
summary: Conduction-band energy edge.

label: Conduction band minima in silicon
summary: six conduction band minima in silicon along the six equivalent [100] directions

label: Conduction-band offset (ΔEc)
summary: Band offset between oxide and SiC used to estimate barrier height for electron transport.

label: Conductivity modulation
summary: Drift region is partially or totally conductivity modulated in the saturation regime.

label: Cone formation
summary: Topography class where ion bombardment induces micron-scale cones and related features on surfaces.

label: Cone-shaped island shape transition
summary: Shape transition between different cone-like island configurations with discrete lateral face orientations; energy-based transition analysis.

label: configurational force
summary: Work-related force on a dislocation due to the elastic energy change as the dislocation moves.

label: Confined Optical Modes
summary: Optical phonon modes confined to a component of a superlattice; frequencies read from bulk dispersion with discrete k-vectors k_m = mπ/(d_A,B).

label: Confined optical phonons
summary: Confined optical phonon modes in quantum wells; width-dependent behavior.

label: Confinement of optical phonons
summary: Restriction of optical phonon modes in quantum-confined structures.

label: Conformal coverage
summary: Uniform coating of horizontal and vertical surfaces; relates to conformal deposition.

label: Congruent melting
summary: An intermediate compound AB melts such that solid and liquid have identical composition at the melting point.

label: Congruent melting of Lu3Al5O12 and Lu4Al2O9
summary: The monoclinic Lu4Al2O9 and garnet Lu3Al5O12 melt congruently in the Lu–Al–O2 system.

label: Conservative climb
summary: Climb without net loss or gain of vacancies or self-interstitial atoms; occurs when a prismatic loop moves in its plane at temperatures too low for bulk diffusion.

label: constrained grain boundary diffusion
summary: Diffusion along grain boundaries in a polycrystalline thin film under constraint, relaxes normal traction and promotes dislocation nucleation.

label: Contact angle θ
summary: Wetting angle between film and substrate; depends on interfacial energies.

label: Convection
summary: Bulk gas flow caused by temperature/density gradients; buoyancy-driven in reactors.

label: Convolution
summary: Device-dependent convolution of the sample signal with a response function g, causing peak smearing.

label: Copper diffusion into silicon
summary: Copper diffusion into silicon substrate; a reliability concern.

label: core-level binding-energy shifts
summary: Shifts of core levels due to ionicity; for zinc-blende structures the total shift can be described by terms involving Madelung energy and distance; typical values depend on charge transfer and geometry.

label: Core–valence–valence (CVV) transitions
summary: CVV transitions in core-valence interactions affecting spectra.

label: Corona Discharge
summary: A weakly luminous discharge occurring at atmospheric pressure near sharp points or edges where the electric field is large.

label: corrosion protection
summary: The mechanism by which plasma-polymerized films protect metals from corrosion.

label: Cosine distribution of sputtered atoms
summary: Emission distribution generally follows a cosine law; deviations occur with energy, material, or texture.

label: Cottrell atmospheres
summary: Dense atmospheres of solute atoms around dislocations forming when EI is large and negative; requires diffusion.

label: Cottrell-Bilby formula
summary: Form of the interaction energy EI between a point defect and a dislocation derived in the elastic model.

label: Coulomb blockade
summary: Low-temperature energy level spacing in nanoscale devices leading to conduction suppression due to electron–electron interactions.

label: Coulomb collisions
summary: Collisions characterized by the strong velocity dependence and large range of Coulomb fields, affecting particle interactions in weakly ionized plasmas.

label: Coulomb scattering
summary: Scattering mechanism due to charged impurities; the mobility improvement is not attributed to a reduction of this scattering.

label: Covalent bonding
summary: Strong directional bonds in covalent solids; examples Si, Ge, diamond, GaAs, InP.

label: Covalent carbides (boron and silicon carbides)
summary: Two covalent carbides with covalent bonding; low density; very hard; semiconductor properties.

label: covalent immobilization
summary: A method of biomolecule attachment that improves stability and reduces denaturation.

label: Cr plane f200 parallel to CoCrTaPt f1120
summary: Cr f200 plane parallel to CoCrTaPt f1120 plane, affecting epitaxy.

label: Cr planes f110g
summary: f110g planes of the bcc Cr underlayer are the closest-packed planes and align with the substrate.

label: Cr underlayer [200] texture
summary: When Cr is deposited on a heated amorphous substrate, Cr grows with [200] texture.

label: Crack array in thin film
summary: Periodic array of cracks forming in the film to relieve residual stress; spacing λ_min determined by energy balance between release and fracture work.

label: Crack deflection at an interface
summary: Crack may kink out of an interface or deflect into the interface depending on energy release rate and local phase angle.

label: Crack growth
summary: Growth or contraction of a fracture surface as the crack advances or retreats.

label: crack opening displacement δ^*
summary: Average opening displacement of cracks under prescribed boundary conditions; δ^* = h_f^{-1} ∫ [u_x(0+; z) − u_x(0−; z)] dz.

label: crack spacing
summary: Spacing p between cracks in the periodic array.

label: Crack spacing (s)
summary: Equilibrium spacing of cracks in films; spacing varies with system parameters.

label: Cracking
summary: Thermal stress-induced cracking observed in thick CVD diamonds, associated with edge polycrystallinity and growth conditions.

label: Creep
summary: Time-dependent plastic deformation under sustained load.

label: critical cluster size n*
summary: cluster size at which nucleation becomes favorable

label: Critical electric field E_critical (4H-SiC)
summary: Maximum critical electric field used: 2.50 MV/cm for 4H-SiC.

label: Critical electric field E_critical (GaN)
summary: Maximum critical electric field used: 3.75 MV/cm for GaN.

label: Critical implant dose for amorphization
summary: Implant dose threshold above which the implanted region becomes amorphous for room-temperature implantation.

label: Critical mismatch strain
summary: Mismatch strain value at which dislocation formation becomes possible; ~20% of buried-wire value and ~250% of uniform-layer value.

label: Critical nucleus area (A*)
summary: Surface area of a nucleus at the critical size.

label: Critical nucleus radius (r*)
summary: Equilibrium size of a nucleus at the nucleation barrier.

label: Critical phenomena
summary: Mean-field predictions for surface vs bulk critical behavior and the role of fluctuations in the critical region.

label: critical resolved shear stress
summary: CRSS; stress required to initiate slip on a slip system; discussed for iron with temperature dependence.

label: Critical thickness criterion
summary: Smallest film thickness h_cr in a strained epitaxial film for which a dislocation can form, defined by W_d(ℓ) + W_m(ℓ) = 0 for some ℓ > 0.

label: Critical wavelength
summary: λ_cr = (¼) US / ((1+ν) Um): wavelength discriminating stability of surface perturbations.

label: cross-linking
summary: A process that enhances selectivity in polymer membranes by forming connections between polymer chains.

label: Cross slip
summary: Cross slip of screw dislocations enabling bypass of obstacles; variations depending on edge vs screw character.

label: Cross-slip traces
summary: Surface evidence of dislocation activity during high-temperature deformation.

label: crowdions
summary: Self-interstitial atom crowdions; clusters migrate along [111] axis via forward/backward jumps.

label: crystal-field splitting
summary: Crystal-field splitting in Oh or Td environments splits d degeneracy; in zinc-blende the d levels split into twofold and threefold components; generally small for core levels compared to spin–orbit splitting.

label: Crystal morphology of emerald crystals
summary: Emerald crystals exhibit prism and plate morphologies; morphology depends on the flux (MoO3-based vs MoO3-B2O3) and B2O3 content.

label: Crystallite coalescence at grain boundaries
summary: Thermodynamic model of crystallite coalescence leading to stress changes.

label: Crystallization of amorphous films
summary: transition from amorphous to crystalline structure due to bombardment

label: Crystallization peak
summary: Crystallization peak observed during cooling (e.g., after supercooling).

label: Crystallization upon heating
summary: Amorphous solids can crystallize when heated, releasing energy as atoms reorder into lattice sites.

label: Crystallographic effects on sputtering
summary: Emission reflects lattice symmetry; preferred directions in FCC ([110], [100], [111]) and BCC ([111]).

label: Crystallographic texture evolution
summary: Evolution of crystallographic texture of grains during tensile loading observed via x-ray diffraction.

label: Cu - Bragg and Nye analogy
summary: Copper (Cu) referenced in Bragg and Nye (1947) as part of the interatomic potential discussion.

label: Cubic crystal symmetry
summary: Crystals with cubic symmetry have c11=c22=c33; c12=c23=c31; c44=c55=c66; three independent elastic constants; stiffness and compliance matrices take a specific form.

label: Current crowding
summary: Two-dimensional current crowding in DMOSFET/UMOSFET; occurs at MOS channel ends and bottom of JFET region in blocking state.

label: Current density
summary: Current density values discussed in JBS/MPS analysis (e.g., 100 A/cm^2, 1 kA/cm^2, 200 A/cm^2).

label: Current density–Voltage (J-V) characteristics
summary: J-V behavior with rectification ~1×10^12; forward J > 60,000 A/cm² at ~6 V.

label: Current density–voltage properties (J–V properties)
summary: J–V properties describing current density versus voltage behavior.

label: Current freewheeling
summary: In inductor circuits, current continues through the diode when the MOSFET is turned off.

label: curvature δ
summary: Mean curvature of the bilayer system defined by δ in terms of film/substrate properties; exhibits maxima under certain conditions.

label: Curvature Drift
summary: Drift of charged particles due to the curvature of magnetic field lines, resulting in a net current.

label: Curvature of substrate midplane
summary: Curvature of the substrate midplane (∇) arising from mismatch strain, used to relate to the film-substrate system.

label: Curvature of substrates
summary: Biaxial curvature induced by mismatch stress in oxide films and patterned lines; measured at room temperature.

label: Curvature (substrate midplane curvature δ)
summary: Bending of the substrate midplane induced by film stresses; quantified by δ via Stoney formula and its extensions for finite film thickness.

label: Cutoff density (n_c)
summary: Density where the plasma frequency equals the microwave frequency; example values provided for 2.45 GHz and 915 MHz.

label: D center
summary: Deep boron-related center (BSi–VC) in SiC; energy EV + 0.45 eV; enhanced under Si-rich growth.

label: D centers (Ev + 0.45 eV)
summary: High density of defects generated near the boron tail region detected by DLTS.

label: d-spacing (ψ)
summary: Interplanar spacing in the strained film; dψ relates to d0 via dψ = d0(1 − ¾_m) + … and to d0 via dψ = d0 − (2ν_f/E_f) ¾_m d0 etc., with d0 as stress-free spacing.

label: d-spacing d_hkl
summary: Interplanar spacing for a crystallographic family (hkl) measured by x-ray diffraction.

label: D−X bound exciton emission
summary: Exciton bound to an ionized donor (D−X) recombination peak.

label: D0e bound electron bound to neutral donor
summary: Electron bound to a neutral donor (D0e) bound exciton analog.

label: D0X bound exciton emission
summary: Emission from exciton bound to a neutral donor (D0X).

label: Damkohler number
summary: Dimensionless group relating chemical reaction rate to transport rate; indicates diffusion limitation when large.

label: Damon–Eshbach mode
summary: Surface magnetostatic spin wave mode; dispersionless and can mix with the bulk continuum depending on propagation direction.

label: Dangling bond state
summary: Half-filled surface hybrid state that lies in the fundamental gap; contributes to surface state occupancy.

label: Dangling bonds
summary: Surface dangling bonds on GaAs(110) contributing to surface states; rotation leads to semiconducting surface.

label: Dark current
summary: Thermally-generated leakage current in the depletion region; contributes to shot noise in the photodiode.

label: Dark current in UV detectors
summary: Dark current levels in diamond UV detectors; influenced by UV irradiation and device structure.

label: Dark Townsend Discharge
summary: A discharge characterized by negligible electron and ion densities, maintaining an unperturbed external electric field.

label: de Broglie wavelength
summary: Wavelength associated with a particle's momentum; used to discuss scattering of He atoms at keV energies.

label: Deal–Grove model for oxide growth
summary: Oxide thickness can be explained by the Deal–Grove model with parabolic and linear rate constants.

label: Debye length
summary: A characteristic scale length that describes the distance over which electric fields are screened in a plasma.

label: Debye sphere
summary: A region in plasma where the number of electrons and ions is significant, confirming plasma ideality.

label: Debye-Waller Factor
summary: Exponential factor accounting for reduction of pseudopotential form factors with temperature due to atomic vibrations; can influence E_g with temperature.

label: Deep centers
summary: Defect centers whose properties are not well described by the effective mass approximation.

label: Deep depletion in SiC MOS capacitors
summary: C–V curves exhibit deep depletion due to extremely low intrinsic carrier density and lack of inversion at room temperature.

label: Deep depletion-mode
summary: A mode achieved in diamond MOSFETs with specific structural characteristics.

label: deep-level emission
summary: Light emission from deep levels in the vicinity of the p–n junction, observed alongside free-exciton recombination.

label: Deep levels generated by RIE in SiC
summary: Deep defect levels created by RIE in n- and p-type SiC; densities can be reduced by thermal oxidation.

label: deep-UV light emission
summary: Visible light emission observed at forward currents exceeding 5 μA, with strong deep-UV emission at 79 mA.

label: defect-activated leakage
summary: A phenomenon observed in diode structures that leads to pronounced leakage currents.

label: Defect-assisted field emission in diamond
summary: Defects introduce energy bands in the diamond band gap aiding electron emission at lower fields.

label: Defect-induced nucleation
summary: Nucleation nucleation enhanced by surface defects on oxides and fluorides.

label: Defects near SiO2/SiC interface
summary: Charge transition levels of defects near the SiO2/SiC interface predicted by theory.

label: Deflectless flow
summary: Under very high stresses, slip planes can move rigidly; large plastic strain rates occur above a theoretical limit.

label: deformation potential (a_c + a_v)
summary: Relative deformation potential for conduction and valence bands; ΔE_c - ΔE_v = (a_c + a_v) Tr(e).

label: Deformation potential d
summary: Shear deformation potential including internal strain; d = d' - (1/4) * dot * d0.

label: Deformation potential d' (for [111] shear)
summary: Shear-deformation potential for the conduction/valence bands in the tight-binding model under [111] shear; given by d' = (8/35*sqrt(2))*(V_pp_sigma - V_pp_pi) = (2/(3*sqrt(2))) * V_xy.

label: deformation potential scattering
summary: Acoustic phonon scattering via deformation potential interaction.

label: deformation twinning
summary: Deformation twinning observed in body-centered cubic metals at low temperature/high strain to produce twin boundaries on {112} h111i systems.

label: Degeneracy at X point
summary: X1 and X2 representations are doubly degenerate; degeneracy lifted in zinc-blende structure.

label: Degree of ionization
summary: Fraction of ions among plasma constituents.

label: delamination
summary: Delamination of the film from the substrate during buckling; growth of debonded zones can trigger buckling/damage.

label: Delamination driving force and energy release
summary: Energy release rate for delamination driven by edge loads ta - tm and ma; G(a) expression; Gm and ¡Ic values referenced.

label: Delamination front
summary: Moving boundary between delaminated film and substrate in the interface.

label: Delamination (interface debonding)
summary: Separation at the film/substrate interface; observed in ILD-TiN interface examples and multilayer stacks.

label: Delta doping
summary: A doping technique that allows for efficient activation of doping impurities in semiconductor materials.

label: delta_gap
summary: Maximum gap δ_gap between island surfaces, on the order of 0.17 nm.

label: Delta v
summary: Volume per unit area of grain boundary; volume deficiency when a boundary is eliminated.

label: Demagnetizing energy
summary: Macroscopic energy due to sample shape; influences domain formation and magnetization orientation.

label: Demixing
summary: Demixing of a mixed crystal or solution into two phases with different compositions.

label: Density Functional Theory (DFT)
summary: A framework to solve the ground-state of a many-electron system by expressing energy as a functional of the electron density n(r).

label: Density of states
summary: DOS for a parabolic band in three dimensions (including spin degeneracy).

label: Density of States (DOS)
summary: Energy distribution of available states; disorder broadens delta-function DOS into peaks with nonzero widths.

label: Denuded zones near steps
summary: Terraces near steps with denuded zones affecting adatom/dimer dynamics.

label: Depletion capacitance (C_dep)
summary: Depletion-region capacitance in the MOS structure.

label: Depletion layer
summary: Depletion layer in a Schottky junction with an internal electric field impacting carrier collection.

label: Depletion region width (x_D)
summary: Width of the depletion region in a MOS capacitor as a function of surface potential and doping.

label: Depletion width
summary: Depletion layer width determining Schottky barrier width on diamond/metal interfaces.

label: Depolarizing field
summary: Electrostatic field arising in MQWs that reduces LO–TO splitting for certain propagation directions.

label: Deposition model (two-step)
summary: Two-step deposition model: (1) surface activation by removal of surface-bonded hydrogen by atomic hydrogen; (2) activated surface carbon radical reacts with C2H2 to enable carbon addition.

label: Desorption
summary: Process by which adsorbed particles leave the surface, related to desorption rate.

label: Desorption energy (E_d)
summary: Enthalpy change between adsorption state and transition state; energy barrier for desorption.

label: detachment
summary: A process that can dominate negative ion loss, depending on conditions.

label: Detailed Balancing
summary: A principle that relates the cross sections and rate constants for forward and reverse reactions, expressing time reversibility.

label: Development of preferred crystal orientation
summary: ion bombardment leads to preferred crystallographic texture

label: DI center
summary: Defect luminescence center in SiC with a strong zero-phonon line (L1) near 2.901 eV in 4H-SiC; L1–L3 observed in 6H-SiC.

label: Dia(3 1 1) azimuthal scan width
summary: Azimuthal scan width for diamond Dia(3 1 1) reflection: 0.12 ± 0.04°.

label: Diad screw axis
summary: Twofold rotation about an axis combined with translation along the axis.

label: Diamond adhesion to hardmetal substrates
summary: Adhesion of diamond coatings is influenced by nucleation, interfacial voids, and cobalt diffusion/dissolution during deposition and heat treatment.

label: Diamond bandgap (E_C - E_V)
summary: Diamond energy gap between conduction and valence bands: ~5.47 eV.

label: Diamond crystal structure
summary: Crystal structure of silicon and related materials; tetrahedral network (diamond lattice).

label: Diamond Domain
summary: A region in the C-H-O phase diagram where diamond growth is feasible.

label: Diamond growth rate
summary: Growth rate observed in MW-plasma jet; reported to depend on methane percentage and comparable to other DC/RF jet methods.

label: Diamond Nucleation
summary: The process of forming diamond nuclei on a substrate, enhanced by specific conditions.

label: Diamond nucleation density
summary: Critical factor in fabricating heteroepitaxial diamond films, influenced by the growth process.

label: Diamond tetrahedral bonding
summary: Tetrahedral coordination of carbon in diamond with four strong covalent bonds forming a 3D network.

label: Diamond XRD rocking curve Dia(0 0 4) width
summary: Rocking curve width measured for Dia(0 0 4) reflection; value 0.064 ± 0.011° across a 92 mm wafer.

label: DIBL
summary: Drain induced barrier lowering, a phenomenon affecting device performance.

label: Dicarbon antisite ((C_2)_Si)
summary: Dicarbon antisite defect in 4H-SiC where a C-C pair substitutes a Si site.

label: Dielectric Function
summary: Frequency-dependent dielectric function A(t) describing the medium's electromagnetic response, including lattice and electronic contributions.

label: Dielectric Tensor
summary: A mathematical representation of the dielectric properties of a medium, particularly in the context of plasma physics.

label: Difference strain ²_dif
summary: Equi-biaxial difference strain defined as ²_dif(z) = ¾(z)/M(z) - ²_m(z).

label: Diffraction cones
summary: Cones of diffracted X-rays produced by dislocations; overlap forms circular images.

label: Diffraction-contrast
summary: Diffraction-contrast feature referenced in imaging analyses.

label: Diffuse forces
summary: Internal shear stress τ_i arising from misfit δ, producing a force on a dislocation distributed over an obstacle spacing Λ.

label: diffusion
summary: Diffusion is a physical phenomenon where atoms move into another solid when heated above a certain temperature.

label: Diffusion along interfaces and grain boundaries
summary: Diffusive relaxation mechanisms along the film-substrate interface, grain boundaries, and passivation interfaces; diffusion influenced by passivation/capping layers.

label: Diffusion-controlled reaction rate (k0)
summary: Rate constant for diffusion-controlled surface reactions; 3D vs 2D behavior discussed.

label: Diffusion equation
summary: Non-steady-state diffusion: dC/dt = D d²C/dx².

label: Diffusion interface
summary: Interfacial region with gradual change in composition; mutual solubility; may exhibit Kirkendall effect.

label: Diffusion length
summary: Diffusion length relevant to carrier transport in diamond.

label: Diffusion length temperature dependence
summary: Diffusion length increases slightly with temperature.

label: Diffusion-limited growth
summary: Growth controlled by the supply of source species to the surface; diffusion is rate-limiting.

label: Diffusion of boron in 4H-SiC
summary: Boron diffusion modeled on dual sublattices; involves BSi, BC, and boron interstitials with charge-state considerations.

label: Diffusional creep
summary: Viscous creep in films due to diffusion: Nabarro–Herring (within grains) and Coble (through grain boundaries) creep; coupled to stress.

label: DII center
summary: Defect luminescence center that appears after ion implantation and annealing; strong zero-phonon line at 3.2045 eV (4H) and 2.9459 eV (6H).

label: Dimer
summary: Dimers formed on semiconductor surfaces; organized into dimer rows (e.g., Si surfaces).

label: Dipole layer
summary: Dipole sheet across polar GaAs surfaces; compensates long-range fields.

label: Dirac fermions
summary: Dirac-like massless charge carriers observed in graphene.

label: Dirac-type massless fermions in graphene
summary: Graphene exhibits a two-dimensional gas of massless Dirac fermions at the Fermi energy.

label: Direct absorption edge
summary: Optical edge due to direct interband transitions near Eg; described by joint density of states near the edge.

label: Direct bandgap E0 of Germanium (Ge)
summary: Direct-bandgap E0 in Ge; example of gap renormalization through electron–phonon interaction at very low temperatures.

label: Direct insertion of atomic carbon into surface C-H bonds
summary: Proposed mechanism for diamond growth at lower temperatures involving direct insertion of atomic carbon into surface C-H bonds.

label: Directional variation of effective modulus
summary: Effective modulus Ehkl for uniaxial loading along [hkl] in cubic crystals; extreme values E100 and E111; dependence on direction given by transformation relations.

label: Disappearing anode
summary: Deposition issue where anode area diminishes due to insulating films forming on chamber surfaces.

label: α-discharge
summary: A form of moderate-pressure RF-CCP discharge characterized by low luminosity in the plasma volume.

label: Discharge Poisoning Effect
summary: A phenomenon where high concentrations of nitrogen oxides (NOx) stop the generation of ozone (O3) in non-thermal discharges.

label: Disclinations
summary: Topological defects (five- and seven-fold) in 2D lattices; related to liquid-like behavior.

label: Disconnection
summary: Interfacial defect with both dislocation and step character; can glide if hλ = hμ and sufficient stress is applied.

label: Disconnections
summary: Interfacial defects with nonzero Burgers vector and finite step height, arising from unequal translation vectors across the interface.

label: Disconnections (interfacial defects)
summary: Interfacial defects with both dislocation and step character that participate in martensitic interfaces and other interfacial motions.

label: dislocation
summary: Line defect in a crystal that can glide under applied shear stress; its motion and barriers to movement influence flow-stress dependence on temperature and strain rate.

label: Dislocation annihilation
summary: Opposite dislocations with opposite Burgers vectors annihilate and restore a perfect crystal when they meet.

label: Dislocation climb
summary: Dislocations circumvent obstacles by climbing vertically and then gliding; climb-controlled creep is temperature and stress dependent.

label: Dislocation core disregistry Δu
summary: Displacement difference across the slip plane: Δu = u(B) − u(A).

label: Dislocation core width (w)
summary: Width of the dislocation core, indicating core size; undissociated cores typically span between b and 5b.

label: dislocation density
summary: The measure of dislocations in a crystal structure, critical for determining the quality of diamond.

label: Dislocation density evolution
summary: Evolution of dislocation density during tensile loading as observed by x-ray diffraction.

label: Dislocation dynamics
summary: Continuum/dislocation dynamics simulation method used to model dislocation arrangements.

label: Dislocation dynamics in SiC during high-temperature growth and cooling
summary: Generation, movement, and interaction of dislocations in SiC during growth and cooling.

label: Dislocation formation
summary: Process by which strain is relieved via dislocation nucleation and glide.

label: Dislocation-free regions in GaN above SiO2 mask (MCE)
summary: TEM/CL analyses show dislocation-free GaN above mask regions with selective overgrowth.

label: Dislocation (general)
summary: Energy and force considerations for dislocations in a layered film–substrate system; includes screw and edge components.

label: dislocation glide
summary: Dislocations move under sufficiently high stress; glide is impeded by obstacles such as impurities and other dislocations.

label: dislocation loops
summary: Loops formed by interstitials and vacancies; observed in irradiation damage scenarios.

label: Dislocation loops from precipitates, impurities, or dopants
summary: Defect category arising from cooling and solid-state reactions.

label: Dislocation (misfit dislocation)
summary: Lattice defects formed to relieve lattice mismatch between film and substrate.

label: Dislocation nucleation
summary: Nucleation process of dislocations in small-scale epitaxial structures, including energy barriers and processing sensitivity.

label: Dislocation pile-up at film-substrate interface
summary: Pile-up of dislocations at the film-substrate interface contributing to a boundary layer and hardening.

label: Dislocation pile-ups
summary: Arrays of dislocations formed behind a source against an obstacle; lead to amplified stress at the pile-up head and long-range interactions.

label: dislocation propagation
summary: The movement of dislocations within the CVD diamond, influenced by the substrate shape and growth conditions.

label: Dislocation reduction during growth
summary: Raman line width and etch pit density (EPD) decrease with thickness; 1/d trend observed; final EPD below 10⁸ cm⁻² for thick films.

label: Dislocations
summary: Line defects on crystal surfaces; act as sources for growth spirals on the {0001} and {1010} faces; bundles observed on surfaces.

label: dislocations (in heteroepitaxial diamond films)
summary: Topological defects in heteroepitaxial diamond films; density estimated from etch pits after oxygen-containing etching.

label: Disorder-induced gap state (DIGS) model
summary: Model describing Fermi-level pinning due to stress at the Pd/p-GaN interface in ohmic contact behavior.

label: Dispersion strengthening
summary: Strengthening by incoherent dispersal of oxide particles in a softer metal matrix (e.g., TD Ni–ThO2, SAP Al–Al2O3).

label: Disproportionation (Ge-related)
summary: Thermodynamic process Ge(s) + 2I2(g) = GeI4(g) and related equilibria used in Ge film growth.

label: Dissociated chemisorption
summary: Chemisorption bonding character resulting from dissociation of the molecule on the surface.

label: dissociation
summary: The process of breaking down molecules into smaller components, often into atoms or radicals.

label: dissociation factor Z
summary: A parameter related to non-equilibrium dissociation processes.

label: Dissociation of H2
summary: The process of breaking down H2 molecules into H atoms after electron impact excitation.

label: Dissociation of H2S
summary: The process of breaking down hydrogen sulfide into hydrogen and sulfur, which can occur in various plasma conditions.

label: Dissociation of molecules by direct electron impact
summary: Stimulated by both vibrational and electronic excitation, can occur in one collision.

label: Dissociative Adsorption
summary: A process where a molecule A2 adsorbs onto a surface requiring two sites, leading to the formation of A:S.

label: Dissociative Attachment
summary: A process where an electron attaches to a neutral molecule, leading to its dissociation.

label: Dissolution-precipitation
summary: Rate-controlling mechanism where soluble reactants dissolve and precipitate to form solid product.

label: Divacancy (V_Si–V_C)
summary: Divacancy defect comprising silicon and carbon vacancy in 4H-SiC.

label: dividing surface
summary: Imaginary boundary used to separate phases at an interface, allowing the system to be treated as uniform up to the interface with a surface term.

label: Domain-like structural behavior
summary: Slightly misoriented domains with low-angle boundaries and high dislocation density.

label: Domain wall
summary: Walls separating commensurate domains in overlayer structures; domain walls form in weakly incommensurate phases.

label: Domain-wall dislocations
summary: Dislocations at junctions of p domain walls; related to topological defects in incommensurate phases.

label: Domain walls
summary: Domain walls/solitons/misfit dislocations between I-phase domains.

label: Dome morphology
summary: Dome-shaped islands with {113} and other facets; final stage in the sequence from pyramidal to dome shapes.

label: Donor–acceptor pair (DAP) recombination
summary: Radiative recombination between electron bound to a donor and hole bound to an acceptor; photon energy given by Eg - ΔED - ΔEA + e^2/(4πεs r).

label: Donor doping concentration (N_D)
summary: Donor concentration in drift/channel affecting pinch-off and device behavior.

label: Donor ionization energy in 4H-SiC (N)
summary: E_C - E_D = 0.059 eV for N in 4H-SiC.

label: Donor ionization energy in GaN (Si)
summary: E_C - E_D = 0.02 eV for Si in GaN.

label: Donor level (cubic/hexagonal sites)
summary: Donor energy levels in cubic and hexagonal lattice sites of hexagonal SiC.

label: Donor level E_D
summary: Donor energy level located just below the conduction band.

label: Donor-like interface states
summary: Donor-like trap states in the lower half of the SiC bandgap; positive when holes are trapped; very high density in dry-oxide MOS structures; reduced by wet oxidation; do not directly affect n-channel mobility.

label: dopant ionization energy E_A,D
summary: Ionization energy of dopants (E_A,D) that can decrease when an impurity band forms.

label: Doping in semiconductors
summary: Small dopant additions (group III or V) create donor/acceptor states altering conductivity.

label: doping superlattice
summary: Superlattice formed by n-type and p-type layers with an intrinsic layer between them (nip i).

label: Doping techniques
summary: Techniques for introducing impurities into semiconductors.

label: Doppler broadening
summary: Line broadening due to the Doppler effect from random molecular motion (gas emissions and related contexts).

label: Doppler effect
summary: The effect that modifies the shape and location of the resonance zone due to the frequency shift for electrons incident on the zone.

label: Double group
summary: Double groups (including Td double group) include 2π rotations that reverse spin; used to determine splitting of J=5/2 and J=3/2 states.

label: Double group notations
summary: Notations used for electron wave functions under spin-orbit coupling; rotation by 2π changes sign for spin-1/2; to be discussed in Sect. 2.6.

label: Double positioning boundary (DPB)
summary: Boundary between ABC and ACB stacking domains; associated with unacceptably large leakage currents when devices are fabricated on DPB-containing SiC.

label: Double positioning domains (DPDs)
summary: Two possible stacking orders (ABCABC… and ACBACB…) observed in 3C-SiC on on-axis 6H- or 4H-SiC(0001).

label: Double Schottky barrier configuration model
summary: Model describing MNM structure with forward and reverse Schottky diodes

label: doubly periodic array of misfit dislocations
summary: A complete periodic array of misfit dislocations along two perpendicular directions; used to model overall elastic strain relaxation.

label: Down-fall defect
summary: Macro-defect induced by particles in SiC epitaxial layers; usually contains stacking faults in basal or prismatic planes or a 3C-lamella.

label: Drain current I_D
summary: Drain current of the MOSFET in various regions.

label: Drain-source voltage V_DS
summary: Drain-source voltage of the MOSFET.

label: Dresselhaus effect
summary: A spin-orbit interaction caused by inversion asymmetry in the crystal lattice, relevant in H-diamond.

label: drift mobility
summary: The measure of how quickly charge carriers can move through a semiconductor material.

label: Drift velocity
summary: Drift velocity associated with a displaced electron distribution under an applied field.

label: Drifted Maxwell–Boltzmann distribution
summary: Perturbed MB distribution under an applied field with drift velocity vd.

label: driving force G on threading dislocation
summary: Driving force on a threading dislocation as it moves, averaged over a period in intersecting arrays; bar G(h,p) defined in the text.

label: driving force on the threading dislocation
summary: Configurational force G(h_f) acting on the dislocation due to the background stress field.

label: Drude edge
summary: Plasma edge where reflectivity drops; associated with the plasma frequency (Drude model).

label: Drude model
summary: Model describing the free-carrier response and reflectivity in metals and doped semiconductors.

label: Druyvesteyn distribution
summary: A non-Maxwellian electron distribution observed at high pressures in plasma discharges.

label: dual-carrier drift
summary: A phenomenon observed in detectors where both types of charge carriers (holes and electrons) contribute to the signal.

label: Dual-Sublattice Diffusion Modeling
summary: Modeling approach assuming ISi and IC diffuse on their own sublattices; boron interstitial pair diffusion (BSiISi and BCIC) used in diffusion simulations.

label: Dundurs parameter D1
summary: Mismatch in extensional stiffness between two joined materials: D1 = (E_f - E_s)/(E_f + E_s).

label: Dundurs parameter D2
summary: Mismatch in volumetric stiffness between two joined materials; bounds depend on D1.

label: Dundurs parameters (D1, D2)
summary: Dimensionless elastic mismatch parameters; D1 = (E_f − E_s)/(E_f + E_s); D2 defined as given; used to characterize film/substrate elasticity.

label: Dust acoustic waves
summary: Waves in dusty plasmas that can be used as a diagnostic tool.

label: dusty plasma
summary: A type of plasma where the number of dust particles can be low compared to the Debye sphere.

label: Dusty Plasmas
summary: Dusty plasmas are important in processing discharges, affecting etching and deposition processes, and offering possibilities for powder synthesis and surface modification.

label: dV/dt triggering
summary: Premature activation of the thyristor caused by rapid dV/dt during forward-blocking mode.

label: DX center
summary: Deep defect center in semiconductors (example: Ge in GaAs) that can capture electrons; may exhibit negative-U behavior.

label: DX centers
summary: deep donor-related defect centers in semiconductors with metastability and lattice relaxation

label: Dynamic Equilibrium
summary: The state between boron–hydrogen complexes and isolated ions in boron-doped diamond at elevated temperatures.

label: Dynamic scattering
summary: Electron diffraction requires dynamic scattering theory.

label: Dynamic strain aging
summary: Portevin–Le Chatelier effect: serrated yield due to repeated locking/unlocking of dislocations by solute atmospheres at high T and low strain rate.

label: Dynamical matrix
summary: Matrix whose eigenvalues yield optical phonon frequencies; strain modifies D_{kk'} via a contracted fourth-rank tensor K_{ijkl}.

label: E′0 transition
summary: Weaker structures involving transitions to higher conduction bands at the zone center.

label: E′1 transition
summary: Weaker structures involving transitions along ⟨111⟩ directions.

label: E. coli reduction
summary: A significant reduction in the surviving fraction of E. coli after treatment with DBGD.

label: E (identity) operation
summary: Identity symmetry operation.

label: e*_PS
summary: Pseudopotential-based transverse dynamic effective charge e^*_{PS}.

label: E0 transition
summary: Fundamental absorption edge at the Brillouin zone center between valence and conduction bands.

label: E1 and E2 optical transitions in GaN
summary: E1 and E2 peaks in GaN; E2 split from E1 by hexagonal crystal field; observations discussed in ellipsometry context.

label: E1/E2 center
summary: DLTS-observed centers in 6H-SiC equivalent to Z1/2 and EH7 in 4H-SiC.

label: E1 transition
summary: Higher-energy interband transition near eight equivalent ⟨111⟩ directions; may split with spin–orbit coupling.

label: E2 mode
summary: Raman-active planar mode at the Γ point for hexagonal SiC polytypes.

label: E2 peak
summary: Strong maximum in absorption related to transitions near ⟨100⟩ and ⟨110⟩; associated with M2 points.

label: Eddy currents
summary: Heat generated by eddy currents in the graphite susceptor due to RF heating.

label: Edge bending moment m_a
summary: Bending moment at the bulge edge calculated from the edge curvature.

label: Edge delamination
summary: Delamination occurring at the edge of the debonded zone in mica/steel systems.

label: Edge delamination driving force G_b(a)
summary: Driving force for edge delamination when volume V is fixed, as a function of width a.

label: Edge deterioration
summary: Edge deterioration of thick CVD-diamond layers due to cleavage of diamond crystals.

label: Edge dislocation
summary: Dislocation associated with an extra half-plane, yielding a stress field with dilational and shear components; described under plane strain with σxx, σyy, σxy components and σzz = ν(σxx+σyy).

label: Edge dislocation glide on (110) slip plane
summary: Edge dislocation glides on its (110) slip plane; there is no displacement along the line and the emergent point charge does not change sign.

label: Edge dislocations
summary: Dislocations on predetermined slip systems; edge dislocations can exist in the film.

label: Edge dislocations with Burgers vector C/2
summary: Edge dislocations with Burgers vector C/2 observed in stacking faults of Ca9(PO4)6 clusters.

label: Edge effect on delamination
summary: Deviation of driving force near the film edge as the front approaches the edge.

label: Edge electrons
summary: Electrons residing on the outermost guiding centers, located at the Fermi level and significant for transport.

label: Edge roughness–induced field enhancement
summary: Roughness at the electrode edge increases the electric field magnitude, leading to hot spots.

label: Edge singularity in interface stress
summary: Singular behavior of stress at the film–substrate interface edge.

label: Edge stress
summary: Stress distributions along the interface near the film edge (edge stress).

label: Edge stress concentration
summary: Local stress concentration near the film edge due to abrupt geometric change, coupling with substrate interaction.

label: Edge stress phase angle
summary: Phase angle of the local interface stress state at the edge of the buckled zone.

label: Effective carrier lifetime
summary: Lifetime derived from the slope of the main decay curve after fast initial decay.

label: Effective g-factor (g*)
summary: Conduction-electron g-factor modified by spin–orbit coupling in zinc-blende-type semiconductors.

label: Effective heat of formation (EHF) model
summary: Model predicting first compound to form based on most negative ΔH_f near the liquidus minimum (metal-Si/germanide systems).

label: effective mobility (SiC MOSFETs)
summary: electron mobility in 4H-SiC MOSFETs; increases with nitridation; values for n- and p-channel devices

label: Effective normal field (E_EFF)
summary: Electric field in the semiconductor normal to the surface at the inversion layer centroid.

label: EH6/7 center
summary: Dominant deep-level defect (Ec − 1.55 eV) in as-grown 4H-SiC; density ~0.3–2×10^13 cm^-3; stable to annealing.

label: EH6/7 centers
summary: Centers related to a carbon vacancy with different charge states; EH7 is VC(0/+).

label: EH7 center
summary: VC(0/+) defect; not important in n-type carrier recombination but relevant in p-type.

label: Ehrlich–Schwoebel barrier
summary: Barrier at a down step that gives adatoms a temperature-dependent probability to be reflected, affecting step-edge diffusion.

label: Einstein model of the solid
summary: Vibrational model used to describe solid behavior at high temperature in relation to vapor pressure.

label: EL2 defect center
summary: a deep defect center in III–V semiconductors with metastability, lattice relaxation, and negative-U behavior

label: Elastic and Inelastic Collisions
summary: Types of collisions in plasma processes, where elastic collisions conserve kinetic energy and inelastic collisions involve energy transfer.

label: Elastic anisotropy
summary: Path-independence of stress work and the existence of a strain energy density function; symmetry reduces independent elastic constants (21 for triclinic; fewer for higher symmetry).

label: Elastic collision
summary: Collision where momentum and translational kinetic energy are conserved; no atomic excitation occurs.

label: elastic collisions
summary: Elastic collisions occur when ions collide with carbon atoms in the diamond lattice, dominating at low energies.

label: Elastic constants (Young's modulus, Y; Shear modulus, G)
summary: Elastic constants relating stress and strain; Y and G define linear elastic response.

label: Elastic energy
summary: Elastic energy change due to surface perturbation, as a function of mode amplitudes a1 and a2 (E(a1; a2)).

label: Elastic energy change
summary: Change in elastic energy due to perturbation (E(h)).

label: elastic energy W
summary: total elastic strain energy of the island-substrate system

label: Elastic field change due to surface perturbation
summary: Stress field in an elastic solid resulting from a perturbed surface, computed to first order.

label: Elastic fracture mechanics
summary: Framework used to assess applicability and the size of the inelastic zone in delamination buckling.
