summary: High-frequency C–V measurement at 1 MHz.

label: 1 Torr
summary: Pressure maintained during microwave plasma CVD process

label: 100 MHz measurement
summary: High-frequency C–V measurement at 100 MHz.

label: 1000°C
summary: Oxide growth temperature; oxides grown above this temperature are expected to be unstressed.

label: 1100°C
summary: Oxide growth temperature where SiO2 is discussed; associated with decay of stress.

label: [111] shear strain
summary: Shear strain that lowers cubic symmetry to tetragonal, causing splitting of the X, Y, Z valence-band states.

label: 13.56-MHz plane-parallel capacitive discharge
summary: Type of discharge used in PIC simulation with specific frequency.

label: 2×1 surface reconstruction on Ge(111)
summary: Metastable 2×1 surface reconstruction immediately after cleaving Ge(111).

label: 200 K
summary: Temperature reached after 300 K during the relaxation sequence; precedes low-temperature glide.

label: 250°C heating
summary: Heating to around 250°C causes the p-bonded 2x1 structure to transform irreversibly into the 7x7 on Si(111).

label: 260°C
summary: Low-temperature deposition condition mentioned for CVD aluminum.

label: 3ω method
summary: Three-omega method referenced in subject index

label: 300 K
summary: Starting temperature in a relaxation sequence for dislocation processes; followed by cooling to 200 K.

label: 4.2 K
summary: Liquid helium temperature at which Josephson device operation and hillock formation considerations are discussed.

label: 40-fs laser pulses
summary: Laser pulses emitted at 800 nm used to treat diamond.

label: 450°C
summary: Maximum temperature in the thermal cycling of metallic films; part of the relaxation history.

label: 600°C
summary: Substrate temperature during PMMA laser ablation experiments.

label: 830°C transformation to 1x1
summary: Reversible transformation of the 7x7 structure to (1x1) at 830°C.

label: A*
summary: Area affected by strain around the nucleus (A* = 2π r* a0 sin θ).

label: A/B ratio
summary: Stoichiometric ratio of A-site to B-site precursors; given as A/B = 1 in the discussed diagram.

label: A/B ratio = 1
summary: Stoichiometric ratio of precursors for ABO3 perovskite; A/B = 1.

label: Abnormal Glow Discharge
summary: A type of discharge used in active plasma cleaning, performed in argon at specific pressures and voltages.

label: Abrasion
summary: A technique to enhance diamond nucleation on non-diamond surfaces by physical scratching with hard particles.

label: Absolute quenching
summary: Cooling process that saves all products formed in the high-temperature zone but converts unstable atoms and radicals back into initial reagents.

label: Absorbed Power
summary: The power absorbed by the discharge, which is a function of the discharge voltage and resistance.

label: Absorption coefficient
summary: A measure of how much electromagnetic energy is absorbed per unit length in plasma.

label: AC magnetron sputtering (20–150 kHz)
summary: AC magnetron sputtering in a dual-target configuration; frequencies 20–150 kHz.

label: AC (RF) sputtering
summary: Sputtering using alternating current to sustain the plasma, enabling deposition on dielectric targets and allowing materials with high resistivity to be sputtered.

label: Acceleration factor (A_F)
summary: Factor used to relate design failure time to accelerated test time in Black's framework.

label: Acceptor density for p-type SiC (Al-based contacts)
summary: to achieve low contact resistivity (<1e-5 Ω cm^2), acceptor density should be ≥3×10^19 cm^-3

label: Acheson method
summary: Commercial production method for SiC via a high-temperature solid-state reaction yielding low-purity polycrystalline SiC.

label: Acheson process
summary: Process for synthesizing SiC from silica and carbon; provided volume production of SiC powders.

label: Acid cleaning and surface termination
summary: Surface cleaning with H2SO4/HNO3 at 200°C to remove surface contamination and terminate with oxygen.

label: Acid etching
summary: Acid etching to remove nondiamond carbon from surfaces.

label: Acid/ozone surface treatment
summary: Immersion in hot mixed acid and ozone treatment to modify diamond surface properties.

label: activation energy
summary: Energy barrier for reactions in the diamond CVD process, influencing reaction feasibility.

label: Activation energy E_a
summary: Activation energy associated with dissociative chemisorption/sticking (e.g., E_a ~ E_d).

label: Activation energy E_m
summary: Diffusion barrier for adsorbates on surfaces; typical values ~5–20% of chemisorption energy.

label: Activation energy for chlorine-assisted diamond growth
summary: Arrhenius analysis yielding two activation energies: 3.6 kcal/mol (600-750°C) and 7.9 kcal/mol (400-600°C).

label: Activation energy for diffusion (E_D)
summary: Activation energy for diffusion in D = D0 exp(-E_D/k_B T).

label: Active Plasma Cleaning
summary: A cleaning process characterized by applying electric current directly to the treated material.

label: adiabatic approximation
summary: Approximation used by Kane for the heavier mass direction when solving exciton problems.

label: adiabatic relaxation
summary: A slow process characterized by high Massey parameters during electronic excitation relaxation.

label: Adsorption isotherm
summary: Isotherm describing adsorption as a function of conditions; related to growth modes.

label: air damping
summary: Affects the switching speed of cantilever switches in RF applications.

label: ALD deposition on diamond at 450 °C
summary: High-temperature ALD deposition of Al2O3 on C–H diamond to improve gate oxide interface.

label: ALE
summary: Atomic Layer Epitaxy; growth of films one atomic layer at a time; self-limiting surface reactions with sequential precursor exposure.

label: alpha-quartz to beta-quartz transition temperature
summary: Displacive phase transition in SiO2 from α-quartz to β-quartz; transition temperature around 573 °C (846 K).

label: Alternating tungsten and silicon layers
summary: Chemical strategy to control grain growth by layering tungsten and silicon during deposition.

label: Aluminum doping
summary: Impurity doping with aluminum promoting 6H-SiC growth.

label: Aluminum implantation/diffusion
summary: Aluminum-doped diffusion process used in 4H-SiC edge termination (diffusion of aluminum is negligible in practice).

label: aluminum-ion implantation
summary: Ion implantation used to form p+-n junctions in 4H-SiC JBS diodes; effects on diffusion discussed.

label: ambient air
summary: Ambient air used during Pt crucible growth; affects defect formation and threshold behavior.

label: Ambient pressure (1 bar) atmosphere
summary: Gas atmosphere at ambient pressure of 1 bar used in measurements.

label: Ambipolar Diffusion
summary: A process describing the diffusion of charged particles in a plasma, significant in low-pressure conditions.

label: Ambipolar Diffusion Coefficient
summary: The ambipolar diffusion coefficient along B0 is given by Da = C/p(Torr).

label: Amorphization dose
summary: Ion dose required for amorphization estimated from energy density; Φ = E0/E with E0 ≈ 1 keV and RP ≈ 10 Å; typical practical doses >10^16 ions/cm^2.

label: Angle-Integrated Photoelectron Spectroscopy
summary: Angle-integrated photoemission; collects electrons over a wide range of angles; valence-band DOS weighted by P(E).

label: Angle of impingement
summary: Angle of impingement onto the substrate during deposition.

label: Angle-Resolved Photoelectron Spectroscopy
summary: ARPES; technique measuring j(ˆL, EL, ıe, Êe) with angular resolution to study valence bands.

label: Angle-resolved photoelectron spectroscopy (ARPES)
summary: Photoemission technique that varies electron emission angles to resolve momentum information.

label: Angle-resolved photoemission
summary: Method used to map electronic band structure by angular dependence of photoemission.

label: Angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES)
summary: Angle-resolved variant of photoemission; used to determine k-parallel components and conduction-band structure by varying emission angles.

label: Anisotropic etchant
summary: Etchant that dissolves material faster in certain crystallographic directions.

label: Anisotropic mismatch strain
summary: Mismatch strain differing in x- and y-directions to elicit asymmetric deformation

label: Annealing
summary: Heat treatment at sufficiently high temperatures to enable defects to diffuse to favored positions.

label: Annealing above a bulk phase transition
summary: Annealing the sample above a bulk phase transition as an alternative surface preparation approach.

label: Annealing after metal deposition
summary: Post-deposition annealing (200–500 C) improves ideality factor and reproducibility of Schottky contacts.

label: Annealing at 1100°C
summary: Thermal treatment transforming polished/sputtered surface to 7x7 reconstruction.

label: Annealing at 150°C
summary: Annealing at 150°C to enable precipitate nucleation and growth.

label: Annealing at 200°C for 24 hours
summary: Post-deposition anneal used to study interdiffusion in multilayer films (Ag/Au/Ag/Pb).

label: Annealing at 380°C
summary: Thermal treatment transforming Si(111) 2x1 metastable reconstruction to 7x7.

label: Annealing at 420 °C in Ar
summary: Annealing step described to lower specific contact resistance for Ti/p-type diamond.

label: Annealing at 450°C for 2 h
summary: Annealing of Cu–Co alloy films at 450°C for 2 h leading to textured grains (example: development of (200) texture after annealing).

label: Annealing at 850°C
summary: Thermal treatment used to remove damage after certain etching processes.

label: annealing_at_852C_for_150h_in_air
summary: Annealing of BSCCO crystals at 852°C for 150 hours in air to maximize the 2223 phase fraction.

label: Annealing at 900°C
summary: Thermal treatment producing an unstable 1×1 surface structure.

label: Annealing Au–Co amorphous film to 470 K
summary: Heating Au–Co amorphous alloy to 470 K triggers irreversible transformation to a metastable FCC crystalline phase.

label: Annealing Au–Co amorphous film to 650 K
summary: Further heating to 650 K leads to appearance of equilibrium Co and Au phases.

label: Annealing in Ar
summary: Annealing in argon atmosphere used to activate dopants after implantation.

label: Annealing in argon at 1600 °C for 30 minutes
summary: Post-implantation annealing to activate dopants; high-temperature step.

label: Annealing Ni/Zr bilayer to 300°C for 4 h
summary: Anneal Ni and Zr bilayers at 300°C for 4 hours, yielding amorphous NiZr despite negligible solid solubility in the phase diagram.

label: Annealing of deep levels
summary: HK2/HK3/HK4 centers disappear with annealing at 1450–1550°C; Z1/2 and EH6/7 remain.

label: Annealing of Ti/Al ohmic contact at 900 °C for 20 s
summary: Annealing step to achieve low-resistance n-GaN ohmic contacts.

label: Annealing temperature
summary: Annealing temperatures for void formation studies: 600–800°C.

label: Annealing time
summary: Annealing duration for Nd-doped PZT films: 20 minutes.

label: Annealing to 1150C
summary: Annealing above 1150°C; hydrogen desorption occurs and 2x1 reconstruction observed; positive EA on resulting surface.

label: Annular heating pattern
summary: Converging thermal wave technique uses an annulus-shaped heating pattern on the sample surface.

label: Anodic-arc deposition
summary: arc deposition with molten anode material that evaporates through arc plasma to the substrate; variant of ion plating.

label: Antenna-edge microwave plasma chemical vapor deposition (AE-MPCVD)
summary: Deposition method used for heteroepitaxial nucleation of diamond on 3C-SiC substrates.

label: Applied bias for alpha-detection (synthetic type IIa diamond)
summary: Applied bias of 250 V for alpha-detection using a 0.31 mm thick synthetic type IIa diamond with sandwich Pt contacts.

label: Applied pressure p
summary: Pressure acting on the film face that drives bulge deflection.

label: Applied pressure to bulge region (p)
summary: External pressure applied to the film to create a straight-sided bulge; used in large-deflection analysis.

label: Ar annealing at 950 °C
summary: Annealing step following electron irradiation to stabilize defects.

label: Ar atmosphere
summary: Argon atmosphere used during Ir crucible growth to manipulate defect formation and gray-track threshold.

label: Ar flow
summary: Argon gas flow used in measurements; inert atmosphere during TG/DTA.

label: Ar post-oxidation annealing (Ar POA)
summary: Inert gas annealing after oxidation; aids removal of excess carbon and improves oxide reliability; high-temperature variants used.

label: Ar+ primary ion beam
summary: Argon ion beams commonly used to bombard the surface in SIMS.

label: Arbitrary orientation of film material
summary: Description of transforming material properties when film is rotated relative to global coordinates.

label: Arc Discharges
summary: A phenomenon characterized by the flow of electric current through a gas, resulting in plasma formation.

label: ARC plasma deposition
summary: arc discharge deposition process with high current arc; ions formed in arc plasma.

label: Arcjet (plasma-torch) CVD
summary: Arcjet deposition technology enabling higher growth rates for diamond.

label: Area (A)
summary: Surface area A; discussed as an additional thermodynamic variable.

label: Argon anneal
summary: In situ argon anneal used during oxide processing.

label: Argon annealing at ~1800 C
summary: High-temperature annealing in Ar to convert BPD to TED near the surface.

label: Argon atmosphere
summary: Argon gas environment used during calibration and measurements.

label: Argon environment during SiC growth
summary: Argon atmosphere used in Lely and modified Lely growth at atmospheric pressure.

label: Argon gas atmosphere
summary: Argon partial pressure maintained during heating and growth (600 Torr to 1 atm; reduced to 1–40 Torr to initiate growth).

label: Argon pressure
summary: Ar pressure affecting the transition between compressive and tensile film stress.

label: Arrhenius number
summary: Dimensionless group Ar = E_a / (R T); ratio of activation energy to thermal energy.

label: Arrhenius plot
summary: Plot of isothermal desorption rate data used to extract desorption energy Ed.

label: Arsenic pressure for diffusion direction reversal
summary: Arsenic pressure at which lateral diffusion directions cross unity (roughly 1.4×10^-3 Pa).

label: As overpressure
summary: Arsenic overpressure during GaAs growth affecting surface incorporation.

label: AsH3/TMG ratio
summary: Ratio of AsH3 to TMG used in MOCVD growth, influencing impurity incorporation and properties.

label: Aspect ratio a/h_f
summary: Geometric aspect ratio of patch to film thickness

label: associative desorption
summary: A type of desorption where molecules are released from a surface.

label: Associative Detachment
summary: A mechanism of destruction of negative ions where a negative ion reacts with a neutral particle, releasing an electron.

label: ASTM E793
summary: DTA signal convention standard (exothermic in T(S)−T(R) definition).

label: ASTM E794
summary: DTA signal convention standard (alternative sign convention).

label: Atmospheric air arc
summary: The environment where the aqueous uranyl nitrate solution is injected for decomposition.

label: atmospheric DBD
summary: A type of dielectric barrier discharge used for charging water droplets in bioprinting.

label: Atmospheric pressure
summary: A condition under which diamond synthesis can occur using non-equilibrium plasma.

label: Atmospheric Pressure Chemical Vapor Deposition
summary: Atmospheric pressure CVD (APCVD) technique.

label: Atmospheric-pressure CVD
summary: CVD conducted at atmospheric pressure; APCVD includes high-temperature systems for Si and compounds.

label: Atmospheric-pressure gliding arc
summary: A process condition used for plasma-stimulated partial oxidation of diesel fuel.

label: atmospheric pressure glow mode (APG)
summary: A mode of dielectric barrier discharge (DBD) that operates without streamers and at lower voltages.

label: atmospheric-pressure non-equilibrium gliding arc discharges
summary: A process used for the effective conversion of gasoline into syngas by partial oxidation.

label: atmospheric-pressure thermal plasma
summary: A plasma state characterized by atmospheric pressure, relevant for the dissociation of diatomic molecules.

label: Atmospheric-pressure transitional arc discharge
summary: A process condition used for plasma-catalytic conversion of ethanol into syngas.

label: Atomic layer deposition (ALD)
summary: Deposition technique used to form oxide films (e.g., Al2O3) on diamond surfaces.

label: Atomic layer deposition (ALD) passivation of hydrogen-terminated diamond with Al2O3
summary: Atomic layer deposition passivation of hydrogen-terminated diamond surfaces.

label: Attenuated total reflection (ATR)
summary: Technique to excite surface polaritons by coupling via an evanescent field, typically using a prism to achieve total internal reflection.

label: Attenuation coefficient
summary: Describes how the amplitude of electromagnetic waves decreases as they propagate through plasma.

label: Auger data ratio technique
summary: Ratio method using channels A and B to extract Auger information; (A - B)/(A + B).

label: Auger Neutralization
summary: A nonradiative transition process involving two electrons where one electron neutralizes a positive ion and the other absorbs excess energy.

label: Auto-ionization states
summary: States that can lead to resonant rotational excitation.

label: Autocatalysis
summary: Kinetic acceleration due to catalytic products (e.g., MnO2) formed during reaction.

label: Avrami's Theory
summary: A model describing the time dependence of the volume fraction of a phase transition governed by nucleation and growth.

label: Axial temperature gradient
summary: Axial gradient controlled by coil position; typical range 10–20°C/cm affecting growth rate.

label: Axially symmetric deformation (nonlinear range)
summary: Axially symmetric deformation mode considered within the nonlinear deformation range.

label: B_{rot}
summary: Rotational energy constant relevant for calculating the mean number of rotational states occupied.

label: b111N diffraction
summary: A diffraction technique used to analyze dislocations in diamond samples.

label: Background strain (ε^m_{ij})
summary: Spatially uniform equilibrium elastic field consistent with a traction-free surface in the reference state.

label: Background vacuum pressure
summary: Vacuum pressure used during evaporated film deposition (4 x 10^-8 torr).

label: Backscattering geometry on (100) GaAs surface
summary: Backscattering Raman configuration with ki and ks antiparallel; q along [100] for a (100) surface.

label: Bakeout
summary: Bakeout procedures used to outgas vacuum systems and achieve clean vacuum before experiments.

label: Bakeout temperature
summary: Bakeout temperature around 200°C used to reduce outgassing before use.

label: ballistic transport
summary: A transport mechanism where precursor collisions are infrequent compared to trench dimensions.

label: Base pressure
summary: Vacuum base pressure during deposition (2 x 10^-5 Torr).

label: Base pressure (P_b)
summary: Base pressure in the deposition system influencing film growth.

label: Bastard boundary condition
summary: Interface condition: (1/m_A) dφ_A/dz = (1/m_B) dφ_B/dz at the QW interfaces.

label: BCF length
summary: Burton–Cabrera–Frank length xs used in step-flow crystal growth; xs is the BCF length.

label: BEN
summary: Bias enhanced nucleation method for diamond growth involving applying a negative bias voltage during plasma exposure.

label: BEN (Bias Enhanced Nucleation)
summary: A process used for diamond nucleation on iridium, characterized by specific conditions that differ from classical nucleation theory.

label: BEN procedure
summary: Diamond nucleation procedure on Ir/SrTiO3(001); carbon overlayer forms and evolves via carbon solubility, subplantation of CxHy ions, and surface segregation.

label: BEN process
summary: BEN process described as a nucleation method for heterogeneous diamond growth.

label: Beveled edge termination
summary: Beveled junction profile to reduce surface field; positive bevel increases depletion width along the surface; negative bevel can increase surface field; bevel angles 0–90°.

label: bias-enhanced nucleation
summary: Bias-enhanced nucleation method to increase nucleation density; involves substrate bias and bombardment effects.

label: Bias Enhanced Nucleation (BEN)
summary: An in-situ process executed in the CVD chamber prior to diamond coating growth, involving a negative bias applied to the substrate.

label: Bias sputtering
summary: Negative substrate bias increases energy of bombarding ions; basis of bias sputtering.

label: Bias Voltage
summary: The voltage applied during the nucleation process, affecting diamond morphology and nucleation density.

label: Bias voltage (Vs)
summary: Substrate bias potential used in ion bombardment studies.

label: Biased-secondary Electron Imaging (b-SEI)
summary: secondary electron imaging technique using negative bias to enhance surface contrast

label: Biasing Current Density
summary: A typical value for the biasing current density on Ir is 100 mA/cm².

label: Biaxial crystal
summary: Crystals with lower symmetry showing two directions with identical propagation velocity; examples GeS and GeSe.

label: biaxial modulus of film M_f
summary: In-plane biaxial modulus of the film; M_f ≈ 6.1 × 10^11 N/m^2.

label: biaxial modulus of substrate M_s
summary: Biaxial modulus of the substrate; M_s ≈ 7.6 × 10^10 N/m^2.

label: Biaxial strain
summary: Strain in epitaxially grown diamond- or zincblende-type semiconductors; decomposed into hydrostatic and uniaxial components.

label: Biaxial strain in epitaxial layers along [001]
summary: Two-dimensional in-plane strain in epitaxial layers grown along [001], due to lattice mismatch Δa; described by in-plane and out-of-plane components.

label: Biaxial stress
summary: Biaxial stress state discussed in the three-dimensional surface perturbation analysis.

label: biaxial tensile mismatch stress
summary: Mismatched film stress applied biaxially in the plane of the film.

label: Bipolar pulses
summary: Bipolar pulsing in pulsed-power reactive sputtering; alternate electrode polarity.

label: bleaching
summary: The effect causing progressive loss of the TL signal when diamond is illuminated in the spectral range 400–550 nm.

label: Blech length
summary: Critical interconnect length L_c below which electromigration is halted; L_c = σ / (Z* q p j(c)).

label: Bloch condition
summary: Bloch condition of the potential φ(x, z) applied to one layer B and its A neighbors in the MQW analysis.

label: Blocking voltage
summary: Blocking voltage used to design both SBD and pin diode; example plane-junction blocking voltage referenced.

label: Bohm sheath criterion
summary: Condition that the ion velocity must exceed a certain threshold for a collisionless sheath to form.

label: Bohm velocity
summary: The velocity into the wall limited to a finite value, defined as uB = (eTe/M)^(1/2).

label: Boltzmann collision integral
summary: A term in the Boltzmann equation representing the change in distribution function due to collisions.

label: Boltzmann Distribution Function
summary: A statistical distribution function used to describe the distribution of particles over different energy states in plasma.

label: Boltzmann equilibrium
summary: Condition for negative ions where the ratio of the negative ion flux to the negative ion diffusion flux is less than or equal to one.

label: Boltzmann–Matano analysis
summary: Method to obtain interdiffusion (D) or chemical diffusion coefficients for diffusion couples.

label: boron delta-doping
summary: A doping technique used to create a conductive channel in diamond for RF power transistors.

label: boron doping
summary: Doping diamond with boron produces free charged carriers and induces metallic behavior.

label: Boron implantation
summary: Implantation of boron to create conductive channels; surface concentrations up to 1e21 cm^-3; can yield very low ρ_c.

label: Bottom crucible temperature (Tbottom)
summary: Temperature at the bottom of the crucible during growth.

label: boundary angle
summary: The angle between inclined lateral and top faces that plays a critical role in preserving bent dislocations during growth.

label: Boundary conditions
summary: Effects of boundary conditions on confined optical phonons in semiconductor nanostructures.

label: Boundary layer
summary: Boundary layer referenced as a process-related condition.

label: Box profile formation
summary: 200-nm-deep box dopant profile formed by multistep implantation.

label: Brazilian disk test
summary: Compression-based test for mixed-mode fracture properties, adapted for interfacial studies.

label: Brazing temperature
summary: Brazing temperature range used for diamond window attachment (600-900°C).

label: Bremsstrahlung Isochromat Spectroscopy (BIS)
summary: Inverse photoemission variant where fixed-photon-energy detection is used while incident electron energy Ee is swept to obtain BIS spectra.

label: Bridgman
summary: Bridgman growth (and gradient freeze) from melt; used for ZnSe and ZnS crystal growth.

label: Bridgman growth
summary: Bridgman growth method used for BGO; dislocation density comparisons with Cz-grown crystals discussed.

label: Bridgman growth process
summary: Bridgman crystal growth method used for CaF2 crystals in graphite crucibles.

label: Bridgman growth technique
summary: Bridgman technique used for growing bismuth germanate crystals as cited in the text.

label: Bridgman Method
summary: Crystal growth method with a temperature gradient along the crucible.

label: Bridgman technique
summary: Bulk crystal growth method.

label: Brooks–Herring approach
summary: Quantum mechanical impurity scattering treatment; includes screening via q0; cross section computed in Born approximation.

label: Buckling at ~800 °C
summary: Buckling observed for relatively low-viscosity BPSG at approximately 800 °C.

label: buffer gas density
summary: Density of the buffer gas, typically neon, in the discharge process.

label: Bulge test
summary: pressurized bulge configuration where a free-standing thin-film window is deflected; used to determine elastic modulus, mismatch stress, or adhesion energy.

label: Bulk-charge theory
summary: Approach accounting for variation in bulk charge along the channel in MOSFET analysis.

label: Bulk micromachining
summary: Formation of free-standing SiC structures by removing the underlying substrate.

label: Burner-to-substrate distance 11 mm
summary: Distance between burner and substrate as depicted in the deposition condition figure.

label: c(2×8) reconstruction on Ge(111)
summary: Surface reconstruction of Ge(111) to the c(2×8) structure after cleaving and annealing.

label: C-H-O gas mixtures
summary: Gas mixtures containing carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen used in diamond deposition processes.

label: C-H-O phase diagram
summary: A phase diagram that provides a common scheme for diamond chemical vapour deposition methods.

label: c_p method
summary: Calibration method using baseline measurements with and without a standard sample to determine sensitivity K_s(T).

label: C-rich growth conditions
summary: C-rich conditions during epitaxial growth affecting defect formation.

label: C–ψ_s Method
summary: Modified high-low method using surface potential to determine D_IT with extended high-frequency limit.

label: C/Si ratio
summary: Carbon-to-silicon ratio in the gas; near stoichiometric when ~1.0–1.3 for good surface morphology.

label: C/Si ratio ~0.7
summary: Low C/Si ratio condition used to suppress micropipe closing behavior.

label: C/Si ratio control during sublimation growth
summary: Control of the carbon-to-silicon ratio at the growing surface.

label: C/Si ratio during CVD
summary: Ratio of carbon to silicon during CVD processing.

label: CAP geometry
summary: Current at an angle to the plane; combines CIP and CPP geometries.

label: Capacitance–voltage (C–V) measurements
summary: Determine net doping density and depletion region in Schottky SiC structures; frequency considerations affect accuracy.

label: Capacitive discharge
summary: A type of discharge used in experiments to study dusty plasmas.

label: Capacitive Discharges
summary: A section covering the models and parameters associated with capacitive discharges in plasma.

label: capacitively coupled RF glow discharge
summary: RF plasma deposition method used for DLC film formation.

label: Capacitively driven radio frequency discharges
summary: Commonly used for materials processing, involving a vacuum chamber with planar electrodes driven by an rf power source.

label: Carbon cap during high-temperature annealing
summary: Cap with carbon suppresses surface roughening and Si desorption during annealing.

label: Carbon diffusion barrier
summary: A barrier on the substrate surface that assists in nucleation enhancement.

label: Carbon ion implantation
summary: Technique to reduce Z1/2 and EH6/7 centers when followed by high-temperature Ar annealing.

label: Carbon ion implantation followed by Ar annealing at 1650 °C
summary: Defect reduction/improvement of lifetimes via carbon ion implantation and high-temperature Ar annealing.

label: Carbon-rich growth condition (4H-SiC)
summary: Growth condition under which boron tends to form BC- shallow acceptor in SiC.

label: Carbon saturation level of Co binder
summary: Carbon content status in the Co binder (high, medium, or low) affecting diffusion and carbide formation.

label: Carburizing
summary: Gradient-inducing diffusion process for carbon content (tribological protection).

label: Carrier gas
summary: Gas used as a carrier in deposition processes.

label: Cascode arrangement
summary: Combination of depletion-mode SiC JFET with an enhancement-mode silicon MOSFET to realize normally-off operation.

label: Catalyst-referred etching (CARE)
summary: A catalyst-assisted etching method for removing SiC without abrasives.

label: cathode boundary-layer microdischarge
summary: A type of microdischarge characterized by a specific electrode configuration and plasma behavior.

label: cathode sheath
summary: Region where the electric field is high enough to accelerate electrons, leading to plasma-like conditions.

label: Cathodic arc
summary: PVD coating deposition process.

label: Cathodic-arc deposition
summary: arc deposition where material from the cathode is vaporized and emitted as ions/atoms; arc spot steered magnetically to achieve film uniformity.

label: Cavity Depth
summary: Different cavity depths (d = 0.5 mm, d = 1 mm, d = 1.5 mm, and d = 2 mm) were used during diamond growth.

label: Ce doping
summary: Impurity additive Ce stabilizing 4H-SiC.

label: Center point deflection w_0
summary: Center point deflection of the bulged film, w_0

label: Central cell corrections
summary: Localized potentials within one unit cell that influence deep center energies.

label: Centrifugal Field
summary: A field that influences the distribution and size of sulfur clusters during H2S decomposition in plasma.

label: CF2Cl2 + O2 plasma etching
summary: Plasma etching step used to fabricate AlGaAs devices.

label: CH4 flow rate
summary: Methane flow rate during deposition.

label: Chamber Pressure
summary: Pressure maintained during the diamond deposition process, set at 90 Torr.

label: charge exchange
summary: The process that occurs when acceptors and diamond are brought into contact, leading to electron transfer.

label: Charge neutrality
summary: Assumption that Δn(x) ≈ Δp(x) at every point.

label: Chemical Equilibrium
summary: A state in a chemical reaction where the concentrations of reactants and products remain constant over time.

label: Chemical Etching
summary: Etching process using molten KOH, NaOH, or Na2O2 at elevated temperatures to reveal dislocations and pits.

label: Chemical-mechanical planarization
summary: CMP used to planarize copper after filling vias/wires.

label: Chemical-mechanical polishing (CMP)
summary: CMP used to planarize metal after trench filling in the damascene process.

label: chemical modification
summary: A method for grafting diazonium salts to diamond surfaces, requiring the diamond to act as a reducing agent.

label: Chemical Nucleation
summary: A proposed pathway for diamond film growth utilizing diamondoids as nuclei.

label: Chemical polishing
summary: Chemical polishing to remove polishing contaminants from surfaces.

label: Chemical potential (μ)
summary: Independent variable in adsorption equilibrium; related to θ via μ = -(1/β) ∂F/∂θ.

label: Chemical transport process
summary: Crystal growth by a chemical transport process (e.g., ZnS).

label: chemical vapor deposition
summary: Flow-based deposition where gases react on a heated substrate; includes LPCVD, APCVD, MOCVD/OMVPE, PECVD.

label: Chemical Vapor Deposition (CVD)
summary: Growth of thin crystalline layers from gas phase at high temperatures; uses silane or hydrocarbons as sources; carrier gas is hydrogen.

label: Chemical Vapor Deposition (CVD) diamond growth
summary: Homoepitaxial growth of diamond on HPHT single-crystalline diamond substrates via CVD.

label: Chemical Vapor Deposition variants
summary: Variants including MOCVD/OMVPE and PECVD mentioned in the text.

label: Chemical vapor transport (CVT)
summary: CVT growth method using chemical agents (e.g., iodine) to transport material at temperatures below melting point.

label: Chemical vapour deposition (CVD)
summary: A process used to grow diamond films by breaking carbonaceous gas phase molecules into reactive fragments.

label: Chemo-mechanical polishing
summary: Polishing process used with plasma treatment to maintain low surface roughness.

label: Child–Langmuir Law
summary: Describes the steady self-consistent ion density in a high-voltage sheath.

label: Child Law Sheath
summary: A sheath that accounts for the decrease in ion density as ions accelerate across the sheath, described by the Child law.

label: Chlorine-assisted Diamond CVD
summary: Chlorine-activated hydrogen/hydrocarbon CVD for diamond growth; wide temperature range explored.

label: Chlorine-based chemistry for fast SiC epitaxy
summary: Use of chlorine-containing precursors (SiCl4, SiHCl3, SiH2Cl2, SiH3Cl) to suppress Si clustering and enable high growth rates.

label: Chlorine-based plasma
summary: Chlorine-based ICP plasmas used for GaN and SiC etching; typically yield high etching rates; etch efficiency affected by volatile etch products.

label: chromium surface temperature
summary: A temperature condition influencing the desorption rate of Cr(CO)6 in non-equilibrium plasma synthesis.

label: CIP geometry
summary: Current in-plane geometry in multilayer devices; tends to higher resistance.

label: Cl₂ etching
summary: A well-studied etching process for copper that involves continuous exposure to chlorine gas.

label: Clamped inductive load
summary: External load modeled as clamped inductive load for switching-energy calculations.

label: Cleaning
summary: Semiconductor cleaning step mentioned in chip fabrication.

label: Close-Spaced Vapor Transport
summary: CSVT technique for CdTe deposition involving vapor transport from a nearby CdTe source to a substrate.

label: clusterization
summary: A process that accompanies the dissociation of hydrogen sulfide and is influenced by centrifugal forces.

label: CMP (chemo-mechanical polishing)
summary: Chemo-mechanical polishing steps used to finish SiC wafers.

label: CMP of SiC (colloidal silica slurry at ~50 °C, pH > 10)
summary: Chemomechanical polishing of SiC using colloidal silica slurry at elevated temperature and high pH.

label: CO2 content in starting materials
summary: CO2 content in starting materials used in stability diagrams; related to carbonate contamination.

label: CO2 dissociation
summary: A chemical process involving the breakdown of carbon dioxide (CO2) into its constituent elements, influenced by vibrational kinetics and energy input.

label: Coagulation
summary: The process where small particles aggregate to form larger dust particles.

label: Coal Gasification
summary: A process that converts coal into syngas using thermal plasma jets.

label: Coats–Redfern method
summary: Kinetic analysis method using linear fits of specific transformed plots.

label: coefficients A, B, C (valence-band dispersion relation)
summary: Coefficients related to Kohn–Luttinger parameters via A1, A2, A3.

label: Coil position (zcoil)
summary: Vertical position of the induction coil; affects temperature difference between charge and seed and thus growth rate.

label: Cold-implantation rapid-annealing (CIRA) method
summary: Technique to decrease radiation damage by cold implantation and rapid in situ/ ex situ annealing to enhance recombination of vacancies and interstitials.

label: cold uniform plasma
summary: A plasma state characterized by low temperature and uniform density, used in the context of conductivity and diffusion.

label: Collimated sputtering
summary: Sputtering variant with improved uniformity and step coverage for thin films.

label: Collimation
summary: Use collimators to filter sputtered atoms and enhance trench coverage by increasing normal trajectories.

label: Collision Frequency
summary: The collision frequency for a constant cross-section process is given by n_{AB} = n_B s_{AB} \tilde{v}_{AB}.

label: collisional absorption
summary: A mechanism of energy deposition in plasma believed to be weak at low pressures but may dominate at higher pressures.

label: collisional sheath
summary: A sheath model that accounts for collisions during high-voltage implantation.

label: Collisionless Electron Dielectric Tensor
summary: The dielectric tensor components that describe the behavior of electrons in a collisionless plasma.

label: Collisionless Sheath
summary: A condition in which the sheath thickness is significant and affects the behavior of probes in plasma.

label: Colloidal Epitaxy
summary: Depositing colloidal particles on patterned substrates to form photonic crystals.

label: Combinatorial materials development
summary: Automated deposition and annealing sequences to screen a large matrix of compositions.

label: combined plasma–catalytic approach
summary: Application of both plasma and conventional catalytic systems to convert hydrocarbons to hydrogen-rich gases.

label: combustion CVD
summary: combustion chemical vapor deposition technique for diamond growth

label: Combustion flame deposition
summary: Combustion-based deposition of diamond as described in the section.

label: Combustion synthesis
summary: Rapid synthesis method mentioned for creating composition gradients.

label: Complete Thermodynamic Equilibrium (CTE)
summary: A state of plasma where chemical equilibrium and all plasma properties are functions of a uniform temperature.

label: Complex dielectric permittivity
summary: A property of plasma that influences electromagnetic wave propagation.

label: Compliant substrate
summary: Thin substrate bonded to a handle wafer to share lattice-mismatch strain and extend the range of stability against dislocation formation.

label: Compressive surface strain condition
summary: Sufficiently compressive strains that can create minima in surface energy U_S away from μ = 0.

label: compressive uniaxial stress along z direction
summary: Compressive uniaxial stress along the z direction used to modify valence-band structure in semiconductors.

label: concentrated sulfuric acid electrolytic system
summary: The electrolytic system used for microarc oxidation coating of aluminum.

label: Condensation coefficient
summary: Fraction of adatoms incorporated into steps during surface growth; given by (2 xs/d) tanh(d/(2 xs)).

label: Conductivity modulation
summary: Technique used for current enhancement in diamond JFETs.

label: Congruent melt growth
summary: Growth from a congruent melt; LiNbO3 example cited in literature.

label: Constant-current stress
summary: Alternate accelerated oxide reliability test measuring charge-to-breakdown (Q_BD) under constant current.

label: Constant deposition flux
summary: Constant deposition flux onto a lattice-mismatched substrate.

label: Constant heating rate (β)
summary: Constant heating rate β used in thermoanalytic measurements.

label: constant-pressure process
summary: A process where pressure remains constant, significant in plasma reactors.

label: Constant-voltage stress
summary: Accelerated oxide reliability test applying constant voltage at elevated temperature.

label: Contact metasomatism
summary: Metasomatic crystallization due to magma intrusion; trapiche ruby forms in carbonate strata.

label: Conventional microwave-plasma CVD
summary: Diamond growth method using microwave plasma; substrate temperature tends to exceed 500°C and must be cooled to control temperature.

label: Conventional TSSG method
summary: Conventional crystal growth method used as a baseline for CLBO crystal production.

label: Conwell–Weisskopf approach
summary: Classical Coulomb-scattering treatment of electrons by ionized impurities; Rutherford-like cross section; includes maximum impact parameter considerations.

label: Cooling Rate
summary: Typical cooling rate during thermal analysis: dot T ≈ 10 K/min, affecting crystallization pathways.

label: core cutoff radius r_o
summary: Core cutoff radius used in dislocation energy expressions (r_o = b/2).

label: Corona
summary: Discharge type used for lavsan metallization in production of wrapping materials.

label: Corona, 2 kHz
summary: Discharge type used for reduction of shrinkage, felting, and wearing of wool, cotton, and mohair.

label: Coulomb collisions
summary: Elastic scattering process between charged particles, such as electron-electron or ion-ion collisions.

label: Counter-doped Junction Termination Extension (CD-JTE)
summary: Edge termination technique using n-type counter-doping to create multiple zones in a uniformly p-type-doped JTE region.

label: Covalent grafting
summary: A method used for the immobilization of proteins to diamond surfaces.

label: covalent immobilization
summary: A method to attach proteins to surfaces that helps prevent denaturation and improves stability.

label: CPP geometry
summary: Current-perpendicular-to-plane geometry; higher resistivity.

label: Cr deposition at room temperature
summary: Chromium deposition performed at room temperature during fabrication.

label: Crack depth a_c
summary: Final or steady-state crack depth in the film (a_c) which may be ≤, =, or > h_f.

label: Critical cluster size i (2D islands)
summary: 2D analog i = (X / (2D_m'))^2 for monolayer nucleation.

label: Critical cluster size i (3D islands)
summary: Size at which ΔG(j) is maximized; i = (2X / 3D_m)^3.

label: Critical electric field (E_c)
summary: Threshold electric field at which drift velocity begins to decrease in GaAs.

label: Critical electron density
summary: The electron density at which total reflection of electromagnetic waves occurs in plasma.

label: Critical film thickness (dc)
summary: Film thickness below which cracking cannot occur for a given residual strain e0.

label: Critical film thickness h*
summary: Thickness beyond which a planar film roughens and islands begin to form (S-K onset).

label: critical layer thickness
summary: Maximum thickness of a strained layer before relieving strain via dislocations.

label: Critical nucleus radius r*
summary: Radius of the nucleating island at which dΔG/dr = 0.

label: Critical Size of Primary Nanoparticles
summary: The critical size for nanoparticle growth is approximately 2 nm, with a critical number of atoms being about 1000.

label: Critical temperature (Tc)
summary: Temperature above which barriers can be overcome thermally without external stress; Tc = (2ΔF/k) ln(ε̇/ρmA).

label: Critical thickness
summary: Film thickness at which a threading dislocation begins to glide to relieve strain; a threshold for relaxation.

label: Critical thickness for dislocation formation
summary: Minimum film/substrate thickness for which dislocation formation becomes energetically possible.

label: Critical thickness h_cr
summary: Critical thickness for threading dislocation glide as a function of misfit, defined in the context (6.46).

label: critical wavelength λcr
summary: Wavelength threshold above which a flat stressed surface becomes linearly unstable to sinusoidal perturbations.

label: Cross Section
summary: A fundamental quantity that characterizes a collision, defined as the effective area for interaction between particles.

label: Cross-slip
summary: Cross-slip of screw dislocations onto {100} planes; may be favorable if APB energy differs between planes; can lead to locking (Kear–Wilsdorf).

label: cryogenic temperatures
summary: Specimen maintained at cryogenic temperatures during field ion microscopy.

label: Crystal orientation (1 1 1)
summary: (1 1 1) orientation used for diamond growth on substrates.

label: Cs+ primary ion beam
summary: Cesium ion beams used to enhance negative ion yields in SIMS.

label: CsCl to NaCl phase transition temperature
summary: Phase transition temperature Tt where CsCl structure converts to NaCl structure; value 470 °C.

label: Curie temperature
summary: Curie temperature of PNZT films around 360°C.

label: Curie temperature for LiNbO3
summary: Ferroelectric/paraelectric transition (Curie temperature) for Li1−xNbO3; Tc ≈ 1020 °C.

label: Current density
summary: Electrical driving force for electromigration; rule of thumb j < 10^6 A/cm^2 in designs.

label: Current density (i)
summary: Current density parameter in Black's electromigration failure time relation.

label: Cutoff Frequency
summary: The frequency at which a wave ceases to propagate in a medium, often due to the properties of the medium.

label: Cutoff radius ro
summary: Cutoff radius ro; the discussion assumes ro = 1/2 b.

label: CVD
summary: Chemical Vapor Deposition, a process used to produce thin films and coatings, including diamond.

label: CVD (Chemical Vapor Deposition)
summary: A method used to grow diamond electrodes, influencing their electrochemical properties based on growth conditions.

label: CVD conditions
summary: Chemical vapor deposition conditions under which diamond films are grown on substrates.

label: CVD diamond
summary: Chemical Vapor Deposition process used for growing diamond films.

label: CVD Diamond Plasma Deposition
summary: CVD processes for diamond involving plasma deposition.

label: CVD diamond process
summary: A process involving atomic hydrogen that terminates diamond with chemisorbed hydrogen atoms.

label: CVD epilayer growth
summary: Chemical Vapor Deposition growth of epilayers in substrate/epilayer studies.

label: CVD growth
summary: Chemical vapor deposition growth of diamond; high-rate growth aided by nitrogen addition; typical rates and reactor conditions discussed.

label: CVD growth conditions
summary: Conditions used for growing a thick film, including power density, pressure, and temperature.

label: CVD hot-filament
summary: CVD method using a hot-filament configuration.

label: CVD method
summary: Chemical vapor deposition method used for diamond synthesis.

label: CVD of 4H-SiC
summary: Chemical-vapor deposition process for 4H-SiC.

label: CVD synthesis
summary: Chemical Vapor Deposition process used for growing thick diamond layers.

label: CVD synthesis technology
summary: A method for producing high-purity, homoepitaxial diamond layers.

label: CVD vapor phase epitaxy
summary: Chemical Vapor Deposition epitaxy used to deposit a single-crystal Si film on a Si wafer.

label: CVD1
summary: Chemical vapor deposition process for type I and III samples.

label: CVD2
summary: Chemical vapor deposition process for type I, II, and III samples.

label: CVI
summary: Chemical-vapor infiltration process for reinforcing materials.

label: Cyclic Voltammetry
summary: A measurement method where the current through an electrode is measured as its potential is varied linearly over time.

label: Czochralski crystal growth atmosphere
summary: Oxygen-containing atmosphere required for growth of oxyborate crystals; Ar atmosphere used to create oxygen defects.

label: Czochralski (Cz) growth
summary: Conventional Czochralski growth method used for BGO; issues with stoichiometry and voids; rotation and temperature gradient considerations discussed.

label: Czochralski growth
summary: Crystal growth from a melt using the Czochralski technique.

label: Czochralski growth (Cz)
summary: Crystal growth method referenced as an alternative to TSSG in the comparison of UV transmission of RVO4 crystals.

label: Czochralski method
summary: Method for growing bulk single crystals by pulling from a melt.

label: Czochralski technique
summary: Crystal growth method by pulling from the melt; high-temperature melting (>2000 C) requires iridium crucibles; oxygen evolution; post-growth annealing.

label: Damage threshold (Dc)
summary: Critical radiation damage level; damage beyond Dc can cause graphitization during annealing.

label: damascene process
summary: Process of electrodepositing copper into trenches in silicon oxide; produces patterned copper lines.

label: Damascene process with CMP
summary: Damascene interconnect fabrication employing chemical-mechanical polishing to planarize embedded metal lines.

label: Damkohler number (gas phase)
summary: Dimensionless group Da_g = r_g / v; ratio of chemical reaction rate to bulk flow rate.

label: Damkohler number (surface)
summary: Dimensionless group Da_s = r_s / v; ratio of chemical reaction rate to diffusion rate.

label: Damped Plasma Oscillation
summary: Oscillation of plasma that decreases in amplitude over time due to frictional forces.

label: Damping Γ_1
summary: Damping of the n=1 exciton intermediate state; affects resonant Raman near excitonic series.

label: Damping Γ_a
summary: Damping constant for the intermediate state; Γ_a ≈ ħ/τ_a; used to avoid divergence in resonance denominators.

label: Damping Γ_b
summary: Damping (linewidth) of the bound exciton; appears in resonant Raman expression near bound states.

label: damping constant gamma
summary: Damping constant introduced for TO phonons in the equation of motion.

label: DBD
summary: Discharge type used for strengthening of gluing connection of siliconized cloak fabric with lining materials.

label: DBD air decontamination
summary: A process involving dielectric barrier discharge for treating bio-aerosols in air.

label: DBD and Glow Discharges
summary: Types of discharges used for ammonia synthesis experiments.

label: DBD (Dielectric Barrier Discharge)
summary: A method used for plasma sterilization of airborne micro-organisms combining ozone, hydroxyl, and UV radiation.

label: DC arc plasma jet CVD
summary: Diamond films prepared using DC arc plasma jet CVD.

label: Dc (critical damage level)
summary: Radiation damage threshold (~1×10^22 cm⁻³) above which irreversible sp3→sp2 transformation (graphitization) occurs in diamond during postimplantation annealing.

label: dc Glow Discharge
summary: A glow discharge in argon operated at p = 100 mTorr with specific parameters.

label: DC Magnetic Field
summary: A magnetic field applied during probe diagnostics that affects electron diffusion.

label: dc plasma chemical vapor deposition
summary: A method used for growing diamonds on substrates.

label: dc plasma CVD
summary: Direct-current plasma assisted chemical vapor deposition used for diamond growth on c-BN and related substrates.

label: DC reactive sputtering
summary: DC mode reactive sputtering to deposit insulating films without arcing.

label: DC sputtering
summary: Direct-current sputtering, the baseline process referenced in triode sputtering discussions.

label: DC thermal plasma
summary: A plasma process used for synthesizing aluminum nanoparticles.

label: DCDC test (Double Cleavage Drilled Compression)
summary: Compression-based interface fracture test with a central drilled hole to nucleate cracks; allows optical imaging.

label: DD plasma operation
summary: The operational condition of JET during the experiments, involving deuterium-deuterium plasma.

label: de-excitation losses
summary: Losses affecting the density of excited neutral detaching species in plasma.

label: Debye Length
summary: The electron density decays on the order of a Debye length lDe.

label: Debye screening
summary: Screening model using a Debye length to modify impurity scattering cross section.

label: Decomposition of metal carbonyl
summary: MoC produced by the decomposition of the carbonyl.

label: Deep depletion concept for diamond MOSFET
summary: Concept of fully depleting the drift region to enhance BV and reduce RONS in diamond MOSFETs.

label: Deep-depletion mode boron-doped diamond MOSFET
summary: Bor on-doped diamond MOSFET operated in deep depletion mode.

label: Deep Level Transient Spectroscopy (DLTS)
summary: Technique to monitor electrically active deep levels via capacitance transients to obtain density, energy position, and capture cross-sections of deep levels.

label: Deep Level Transient Spectroscopy (DLTS) analysis
summary: DLTS used to characterize shallow and deep levels generated by ion implantation in SiC.

label: delta (atomic size difference) δ
summary: Atomic size difference; typically δ ≤ 8.5% for miscibility.

label: Delta-depletion approximation
summary: Approximation modeling depletion region as uniform charge and inversion layer as a delta charge sheet.

label: Delta-doping
summary: A technique explored to improve mobility in diamond by forming a thin, highly boron-doped layer between intrinsic layers.

label: Delta temperature (∆T)
summary: Temperature difference between seed and charge during growth; typical 30 K.

label: density
summary: The measure of mass per unit volume of plasma, crucial for diagnostics.

label: Density Functional Method (DFT)
summary: Ground-state energy expressed as a functional of the electron density; practical implementation via coupled equations.

label: Density Functional Theory (DFT)
summary: Quantum mechanical modeling method; starting point for chemisorption studies and surface processes.

label: Depletion gilding
summary: Thermal oxidations followed by dissolution of copper oxide to enrich surface gold in tumbaga.

label: Deposition area
summary: Deposition area in the NIRIM reactor is small (about 1-2 cm^2).

label: Deposition area (LADS)
summary: Large-area deposition with wafers up to 20 cm diameter; substrate oven provides temperature control.

label: Deposition chamber pressure
summary: Vacuum chamber pressure during PNZT deposition: 4×10^-5 to 6×10^-5 mbar.

label: deposition conditions
summary: Conditions under which diamond growth occurs, including gas pressure and input power.

label: Deposition coverage 2 ML
summary: Two monolayers of Ag deposition forming the initial layer before island growth.

label: deposition flux f
summary: constant flux (volume per unit area per unit time) of material deposited onto the surface, starting at time t = 0.

label: Deposition from chloride and methane
summary: VC deposited from the chloride and methane.

label: Deposition from iodide
summary: ThC2 deposited from the iodide.

label: Deposition from iodide (UC2)
summary: UC2 deposited from the iodide.

label: Deposition pressure
summary: Deposition pressure during Nd-doped PZT film growth: 6 to 8 × 10^-6 mbar.

label: Deposition pressure (a few Torr)
summary: Low pressure during deposition influencing diffusion and grain growth.

label: Deposition rate
summary: Rate of deposition of evaporant; influences film purity and impurity incorporation.

label: Deposition rate (R)
summary: Deposition rate or flux dependent on source-substrate distance, angle of impingement, Ts and Pb.

label: Deposition Temperature
summary: Temperature used for deposition of metal interconnections; specified as 400°C in the Ellingham/SiO2 context.

label: Deposition temperature 550°C
summary: Temperature used during tungsten deposition and silicon layer formation.

label: Deposition temperature 750 K
summary: Low-temperature deposition condition mentioned for the low-temperature combustion deposition study.

label: Depth profiling
summary: Technique combining sputtering and electron spectroscopy to determine composition as a function of depth.

label: desorption
summary: The process of atoms or molecules detaching from a surface.

label: detachment
summary: The process by which negative ions are removed from the plasma by collisions with excited neutral species.

label: Detachment-dominated loss
summary: A condition where detachment processes dominate the loss of negative ions.

label: Detachment Process
summary: A process involving the detachment of an electron from an ion during collisions with excited particles.

label: diamond chemical vapour deposition (CVD)
summary: A method for synthesizing diamond on various substrates using gas phase activation.

label: Diamond Etching
summary: A process using reactive gases to etch diamond, producing volatile products and modifying surface properties.

label: diamond growth
summary: The rate of diamond growth is influenced by the flux of hydrogen and oxygen species in the gas mixture.

label: Diamond nano-particles etching conditions
summary: Conditions for etching diamond nano-particles including RF power, pressure, and gas flows.

label: Diamond surface passivation
summary: Passivation of diamond surface to prevent oxidation and enable higher operating temperatures.

label: Dielectric Barrier Discharge (DBD)
summary: A discharge process involving dielectric layers between metal electrodes, typically operating at frequencies of 0.05 - 100 kHz and voltages around 10 kV.

label: Differential Hall effect spectroscopy (DHES)
summary: Analytical method to obtain donor levels and density without data fitting.

label: Differential microwave photoconductance decay (differential μ-PCD)
summary: Differential μ-PCD improves SNR; uses 26 GHz microwaves.

label: Differential pressure rating
summary: Diamond window must withstand differential pressures greater than 1 atm.

label: Differential Scanning Calorimetry (DSC)
summary: Heat-flow technique; related to DTA with reference/sample temperature control.

label: Differential Scattering Cross Section
summary: A measure of the likelihood of scattering at a specific angle, used to calculate total scattering cross sections.

label: Differential Thermal Analysis
summary: DTA; thermal analysis technique used in crystal characterization.

label: Differential Thermal Analysis (DTA)
summary: Technique relying on temperature difference between sample and reference during heating.

label: Diffusion
summary: Migration of defects enabling them to reach favorable positions near dislocations.

label: diffusion along grain boundary
summary: Mass diffusion path along grain boundaries; slows relaxation when included in models.

label: diffusion barrier
summary: energy barrier for adatom diffusion on the surface (Ed)

label: Diffusion Coefficient
summary: The diffusion coefficient for A molecules due to collisions with B molecules is defined by the equation D_{AB} = \frac{eT}{MRn_{AB}}.

label: Diffusion energy Ed
summary: Diffusion energy along the diffusion path for Kr on graphite (Ed in terms of Vg).

label: Diffusion in a Magnetic Field
summary: A condition where plasma is generated in a cylindrical tube under a magnetic field.

label: Dilatometry
summary: Characterization technique used for LiNbO3 and related crystals.

label: DIN 51007
summary: DTA/DTA-signal convention standard referenced in the text.

label: Direct Current (DC) Sheaths
summary: A section discussing the basic concepts and equations related to DC sheaths in plasma.

label: Direct ionization
summary: Result of interaction between a high-energy electron and a valence electron of a neutral atom or molecule.

label: Direct ionization by electron impact
summary: Ionization of neutral and previously unexcited atoms, radicals, or molecules by a high-energy electron in one collision.

label: Direct plasma exposure
summary: The exposure time of bacteria to plasma in the DBGD device.

label: Direct Plasma Treatment
summary: A method of sterilization where the tissue acts as an electrode, allowing charged particles to interact directly with the bacteria.

label: discharge current density
summary: A condition measured in the experiments at various pressures.

label: Discharge pressure
summary: RF sputtering discharge pressure range.

label: Discharge Voltage
summary: The voltage across the discharge that influences the discharge resistance and reactance.

label: Discrete exchange factors (DEF) radiative transfer
summary: Method for solving radiative heat transfer by dividing surfaces into rings and computing view factors.

label: dislocation bending
summary: A phenomenon observed in thick CVD diamond grown on inclined planes, leading to reduced dislocation density.

label: Disorder (defects/imperfections) in 2DEG
summary: Disorder-induced potential that broadens the density of states and enables integral QHE.

label: Displacement energy threshold
summary: Threshold energy for an atom to displace from its lattice site under electron irradiation; about 25 eV.

label: Disproportionation
summary: CVD transport mechanism where metal forms volatile higher-valent halides and deposits as lower-valent metal.

label: Dissociation Energy
summary: The energy required to dissociate a molecule, which is modified in certain models to account for translational temperature.

label: Dissociation of F2
summary: Dissociation of F2 produces two fluorine atoms with a dissociation enthalpy of 1.64 eV/mol.

label: Dissociation of H2
summary: Dissociation of H2 yields two hydrogen atoms with a dissociation enthalpy of 4.5 eV/mol.

label: Dissociation of HCl
summary: Dissociation of HCl produces H2 and Cl2 with a dissociation enthalpy of 0.96 eV/mol.

label: Dissociation of HF
summary: Dissociation of HF leads to the formation of H2 and F2 with a dissociation enthalpy of 2.83 eV/mol.

label: Dissociation of HI
summary: Dissociation of HI results in H2 and I2 with a dissociation enthalpy of -0.27 eV/mol.

label: Dissociation of N2
summary: Dissociation of N2 results in two nitrogen atoms with a dissociation enthalpy of 9.8 eV/mol.

label: Dissociation of Water Vapor
summary: The process of breaking down water vapor into hydrogen and oxygen in plasma-chemical systems.

label: Dissociation Rate Coefficient
summary: A constant coefficient used in the context of particle growth in capacitive discharge.

label: Dissociative Attachment
summary: A reaction condition where dissociative attachment dominates over polar ionization for the production of negative ions in an oxygen discharge.

label: Dissociative Electron Attachment
summary: A process where an electron collides with a molecule, resulting in the production of negative ion fragments.

label: Dissociative electron–ion recombination
summary: The fastest electron neutralization mechanism in molecular gases, involving the dissociation of molecular ions.

label: Dissociative ionization
summary: Ionization process that occurs when electron energy substantially exceeds the ionization potential, resulting in the formation of neutral atoms.

label: DLTS (Deep Level Transient Spectroscopy)
summary: Measurement technique used to study deep levels in SiC (e.g., Z1/2, EH6/7, etc.).

label: Dopant dose
summary: Dopant dose used in implantation experiments: 8.4 × 10¹⁶ cm⁻².

label: Dopant implantation energy
summary: Implantation energy used for phosphorus into silicon (160 keV).

label: Dopant surface concentration
summary: Dopant concentration at the silicon surface.

label: Doping diffusion
summary: Diffusion-based doping approach discussed as alternative to ion implantation.

label: Doping of nitrogen (N) and aluminum (Al) in SiC epitaxy
summary: Nitrogen and aluminum doping across wide densities; aluminum incorporation may saturate at high densities.

label: Doping techniques in diamond
summary: Doping of diamond via boron using gaseous, liquid, or solid precursors; in-situ and ex-situ methods.

label: double-trench structure
summary: Trench MISFET with source and gate trenches (double trenches) reducing RonA and field crowding.

label: DRIE
summary: Deep reactive ion etching process used for fabricating silicon moulds.

label: Drift test
summary: Drift-test experimental configuration: metal stripe on TiN with current, observing electromigration-induced drift and void/hillock formation.

label: Drift velocity measurements
summary: Technique to measure the drift velocity of carriers using the time-of-flight method.

label: Drude model
summary: Classical free-carrier model used to derive the Drude dielectric response for doped semiconductors.

label: dry etching
summary: Dry-etching used to form unidirectional patterned lines in oxide films.

label: Dry oxidation
summary: Oxidation performed in dry oxygen; associated with higher interface state density on non-0001 crystal faces.

label: Dry oxidation at 1000 °C
summary: Dry oxidation step performed at 1000 °C for oxide formation.

label: Dual-Frequency Discharges
summary: Discharges driven at one high and one low frequency source, allowing independent control of ion flux and energy.

label: Dual frequency operation
summary: An approach using high- and low-frequency rf sources to control ion flux and bombarding energy independently.

label: Dundurs parameters (D1, D2)
summary: Dimensionless Dundurs parameters describing modulus mismatch effects on fracture behavior.

label: Duty factor
summary: Fraction of the switching period when the transistor is ON (δ = t_ON/T).

label: Dynamic SIMS
summary: SIMS operation mode with high sputtering rates enabling depth profiling.

label: Dynamical annealing
summary: Elevating substrate temperature during implantation to promote recombination of vacancies and interstitials, thereby increasing Dc and reducing damage accumulation.

label: Dynamical X-ray diffraction topography
summary: Topography technique for evaluating defect structures (implied by high-resolution X-ray topography discussions).

label: E_ads
summary: Activation energy for adsorption.

label: E-beam lithography
summary: Electron-beam lithography used to pattern nanoscale features in MEMS/NEMS fabrication.

label: E_des
summary: Desorption energy of adatoms from the surface.

label: E_i
summary: Critical dissociation energy of a cluster containing i atoms.

label: E_s
summary: Diffusion barrier energy for adatoms on the surface.

label: ECR conditions
summary: Conditions under which microwave discharges operate for semiconductor processing.

label: ECR Discharge
summary: A discharge type where a right circularly polarized wave propagates along a DC magnetic field, providing wave energy absorption via collisionless heating.

label: Effective diffusion length
summary: An effective diffusion length in the bulk plasma denoted as d_eff.

label: Effective Mass Approximation
summary: Modeling approach treating electrons and holes as particles with effective masses in conduction and valence bands.

label: Effective medium approximation
summary: Approximation that smears out all charge except that of a single embedded atom; immersion energy as a function of density.

label: Effective Medium Theory (EMT)
summary: Semi-empirical modeling approach used in surface science and catalysis.

label: effective mismatch strain epsilon*
summary: Effective mismatch strain used in blocking criterion, epsilon* defined by b/(4πh*) ln(4h*/b) and epsilon* = tau*/mu.

label: effective reaction time in after-glow
summary: Time interval for CO2 molecules in the after-glow zone, denoted as tau_p.

label: Effective Temperature
summary: A temperature used in models to describe the dissociation process, calculated as a weighted average of vibrational and translational temperatures.

label: Efficiency α
summary: A key parameter describing the influence of plasma excitation of molecules on their chemical reaction rates.

label: Ehrlich–Schwoebel barrier
summary: An energy barrier affecting adatom diffusion over steps, discussed in step-nucleation context.

label: Elastic Collision
summary: A type of collision where kinetic energy is conserved, important in gas discharges.

label: Elastic limit
summary: Frequency range in which folded acoustic mode dispersion can be treated as linear (elastic limit).

label: elastic reciprocity
summary: Reciprocity principle used to relate Δf and δ^* for calculating f (two boundary-value problems).

label: Electric Arc Plasma
summary: A method for direct coal gasification using water vapor in an atmospheric-pressure electric arc discharge.

label: electric dipole radiation
summary: A type of radiation emitted by atoms transitioning between energy levels, subject to selection rules.

label: electric field
summary: A constant along the positive column that defines the voltage in arc discharges.

label: electric field distribution
summary: The distribution of electric fields within the cavity, affecting plasma generation and diamond deposition.

label: Electric field F
summary: Applied external electric field.

label: Electrochemical etch
summary: Electrochemical etching to remove nondiamond carbon from surfaces.

label: Electrochemical etching
summary: Selective removal of p-type SiC using dilute HF; involves anodic oxidation at the SiC/solution interface.

label: electrochemical grafting
summary: A method where electrons are supplied during cathodic polarization to facilitate grafting.

label: Electrochemical-hydrothermal method
summary: Hydrothermal process combined with an electric field for material synthesis.

label: electrochemical modification
summary: A method involving the electrochemical reduction of phenyl diazonium salts to graft aromatic groups onto diamond surfaces.

label: Electrochemical oxidation
summary: Electrochemical oxidation of the diamond surface to control Vth.

label: Electrochemical polishing
summary: Electrochemical polishing to improve surface quality.

label: Electron Attachment
summary: A process where electrons attach to neutral particles, influencing the dynamics of plasma discharges.

label: Electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD)
summary: Technique based on SEM that measures crystallographic orientation of individual grains to assess microtexture.

label: Electron-beam evaporation
summary: Technique used to deposit Ir films on substrates.

label: Electron bombardment
summary: Electron bombardment used for surface cleaning and modification in vacuum.

label: Electron Collision Frequency
summary: Frequency of collisions involving electrons, denoted as \(\nu_e\).

label: Electron Cyclotron Resonance (ECR)
summary: A plasma discharge process where microwave energy is absorbed by electrons in a magnetic field, affecting plasma density and behavior.

label: Electron Cyclotron Resonance (ECR) Discharge
summary: A type of discharge excited at microwave frequencies, requiring a strong dc magnetic field for wave absorption.

label: Electron Debye length
summary: A measure of a plasma's ability to shield electric fields.

label: electron density
summary: Typical values of electron density in low-pressure discharge CO2 laser systems.

label: Electron density in RF plasma
summary: Electron density range in RF plasma.

label: Electron Detachment
summary: A process where negative ions lose electrons, affecting the balance of charged particles in plasma.

label: Electron energy
summary: The energy of electrons that influences vibrational excitation rates.

label: Electron Energy Loss Spectroscopy
summary: EELS is used to probe molecular orientation via dipole-active vibrational modes; leverages pseudo-selection rules for orientation relative to surface.

label: Electron gyration frequency
summary: The frequency at which electrons gyrate in a magnetic field.

label: Electron-hole condensate
summary: A state formed when sufficient excitation power creates a large number of electron-hole pairs in a semiconductor.

label: Electron Impact Detachment
summary: A detachment mechanism where an electron collides with a negative ion, resulting in the release of electrons and neutral particles.

label: Electron impact excitation
summary: A process where electrons collide with molecules, causing excitation and potential dissociation.

label: Electron–ion recombination
summary: A highly exothermic process involving the neutralization of a positive ion and an electron.

label: Electron irradiation (116–250 keV) with varying fluences
summary: Low-energy electron irradiation used to increase Z1/2 center density; annealed afterward.

label: Electron irradiation (200–400 keV) for semi-insulating SiC
summary: Electron irradiation creates semi-insulating regions; stable after high-temperature annealing with sufficient fluence.

label: electron number density
summary: A measure of the concentration of electrons in the plasma, affecting diamond growth.

label: Electron Paramagnetic Resonance (EPR)
summary: Technique to detect unpaired electron spins at point defects; provides g-factor information for defect identification.

label: Electron plasma frequency
summary: The frequency at which electrons oscillate in a plasma.

label: Electron temperature
summary: Electron temperature in the plasma (2 eV).

label: Electronegative Discharge
summary: A discharge characterized by the presence of negative ions and specific voltage conditions.

label: Electronegative Discharges
summary: Discharges used for most materials processing, such as O₂ or Cl₂.

label: Electronegative Equilibrium
summary: Condition in cylindrical coordinates for high-pressure diffusion in an infinite cylinder.

label: Electronegative Plasma
summary: A plasma where electron attachment is significant, affecting charge distribution.

label: Electronegative Plasmas
summary: Plasmas that require modifications in analysis compared to electropositive plasmas, particularly in heating and density calculations.

label: Electronic excitation by electron impact
summary: Requires higher electron energies (> 10 eV) than vibrational and rotational excitation.

label: Electroplating
summary: Electroplating used to fill copper in damascene process.

label: Electroplating gold
summary: Process used to form air bridges with a thickness of 1.2 μm.

label: Electroporation
summary: The process of membrane rupture in bacterial cells due to electrostatic forces from charged particles.

label: Electroreflectance
summary: Electroreflectance techniques used to probe semiconductor band structure.

label: Electrostatic actuation
summary: A driving mechanism in MEMS where two plates of opposite electrical polarity attract each other using Coulomb force.

label: Electrostatic boundary conditions
summary: Boundary conditions for E_parallel and D_perp at AB and BA interfaces in the MQW analysis.

label: Electrostatic Seeding
summary: A widely used seeding technique for diamond growth on various surfaces, requiring knowledge of the substrate's zeta potential.

label: Electrostatic Self-Assembly Seeding
summary: A method for diamond nucleation on non-diamond substrates using self-assembly of diamond particles by adsorption.

label: Elementary Reaction (6–2)
summary: An endothermic reaction involving a vibrationally excited N2 molecule that limits the Zeldovich mechanism for NO synthesis.

label: Elevated gas pressures
summary: Elevated gas pressures are required for effective three-body attachment of oxygen atoms to molecular oxygen.

label: Elevated-temperature stabilization of CLBO
summary: Maintaining CLBO at elevated temperature (e.g., 160°C) to prevent hydration stress and stabilize crystallinity; long-term operation possible in dry ambient air.

label: Eley-Rideal (ER) mechanism
summary: Reaction between a gas-phase species and a chemisorbed species.

label: Eley–Rideal kinetics
summary: A reaction mechanism where an adsorbed species reacts directly with a gas-phase molecule.

label: Ellingham-type predominance diagram
summary: Thermodynamic diagram used to assess equilibrium with respect to the atmosphere.

label: Ellipsometry
summary: Ellipsometric measurements used to determine A^2(E) for GaN; measurements performed with synchrotron radiation.

label: Embedded Atom Method (EAM)
summary: Semi-empirical modeling scheme widely applied to surface processes and catalysis.

label: Empirical Energy Moment Approach
summary: DOS-based approach to impurity binding energies via Bloch function basis.

label: Empirical Pseudopotential Method (EPM)
summary: Pseudopotential form factors determined by fitting experimental data (optical reflectivity, photoelectron spectra); used to calculate band structure; can interpolate to other compounds.

label: Endothermic Reaction
summary: Reaction (10–95) is endothermic, requiring energy input.

label: Energy Cost
summary: The energy required for the treatment of VOCs in plasma, measured in eV per molecule.

label: Energy diffusion coefficient
summary: A parameter related to the diffusion of energy in plasma processes.

label: Energy Efficiency
summary: A measure of the efficiency of MHD generators, defined as the ratio of power density to the work of electromagnetic force.

label: Energy Efficiency of Ozone Production
summary: Energy efficiency for ozone synthesis from oxygen is 4-6%, while from air it is 2-3%.

label: Energy minimization
summary: Minimization of the total potential energy to determine deformation characteristics of the film-substrate system.

label: Energy relaxation length
summary: The distance over which energy is relaxed in a plasma.

label: Envelope function approximation
summary: Approximation expressing wavefunctions in QWs as products of envelope functions and Bloch states.

label: Epitaxial bonding
summary: Boundary where boundary atoms occupy natural lattice sites of the crystal on the other side; used in epitaxial growth.

label: Epitaxial deposition
summary: Deposition of a film onto a substrate in an epitaxial (aligned) fashion.

label: Epitaxial deposition of Ge on Si(001)
summary: Epitaxial deposition leading to SiGe island formation.

label: Epitaxial growth
summary: Deposition of atoms onto a single-crystal substrate to grow a single-crystal film with matching lattice, often requiring lattice-m parameter control.

label: Epitaxial growth of SiGe on Si
summary: SiGe film grown epitaxially on a silicon substrate; interface is along the f100 plane.

label: Epitaxial growth (SiC epitaxy)
summary: Epitaxial growth process for SiC structures.

label: epitaxial lateral overgrowth
summary: A growth technique used to form continuous diamond films.

label: Epitaxial Lateral Overgrowth (ELO)
summary: Technique employed to minimize defect densities in materials epitaxially grown on lattice-mismatched substrates.

label: Epitaxial layer thickness
summary: Thickness of SiC epitaxial layer used as a parameter in lifetime simulations (e.g., 50, 200, 300 μm).

label: Epitaxial temperature (T_E)
summary: Temperature range defining epitaxy on alkali halide substrates; specific values given for Ag on LiF, NaCl, KCl, and KI.

label: Epitaxy
summary: Growth process for crystalline metal films on substrates; noted as a common problem when metals do not form regular layer-by-layer growth on non-conducting substrates.

label: EPR spectroscopy
