summary: A technique used to investigate the dynamics of NO levels in tissues.

label: epsilon (stabilization parameter)
summary: Defined as ε = T ΔS_mix / ΔH_mix; large ε stabilizes high-entropy alloys.

label: Equi-biaxial compression
summary: Compression mode used in experiments to study initially circular delaminations.

label: Equi-biaxial in-plane tensile stress σ_m
summary: Remotely applied equi-biaxial tensile stress in the plane of the film; magnitude σ_m.

label: Equi-biaxial mismatch stress
summary: Uniform equi-biaxial stress applied to film before bonding to substrate.

label: Equi-biaxial stress
summary: Stress state with σ_xx = σ_zz = σ_m in the surface evolution analysis.

label: equi-biaxial tensile stress
summary: Remote equi-biaxial tensile stress applied to the material far from the hole.

label: Equibiaxial mismatch strain
summary: Equibiaxial mismatch strain ε_m between film and substrate; drives energy terms in island formation.

label: Equibiaxial remote traction
summary: Condition where the material block is subjected to equi-biaxial remote traction initiating the studied stress state.

label: Equilibrium approach
summary: Local equilibrium method yielding the same results as energy minimization, using translational invariance and symmetry.

label: Equilibrium Charge
summary: The equilibrium charge on a dust particle in a plasma is determined by the balance of positive ion flux and electron flux.

label: equilibrium vapor pressure
summary: pressure pe at which condensation and evaporation proceed at the same rate

label: Escape barrier energy (Si)
summary: Escape barrier energy for silicon surface atoms (7.8 eV).

label: Escape depth
summary: Depth from which electrons escape in spectroscopies; can be as small as ≈5 Å at 50–100 eV.

label: eta
summary: Film-to-substrate thickness ratio h_f/h_s.

label: etch pits
summary: Technique used by Johnston and Gilman to reveal dislocation positions during velocity measurements.

label: Etch stop
summary: Etchant stop layer to terminate etching at a predetermined depth.

label: etchants-to-unsaturates flux ratio
summary: A critical parameter in etching that influences etch rate and anisotropy.

label: Etching
summary: Etching processing step in GaN/4H-SiC fabrication.

label: Evacuation rate
summary: Tube evacuation rate feasible at about 1 bar/sec.

label: Evaporation (evaporative deposition)
summary: Evaporative deposition as part of PVD; material vaporized from a source and condensed on substrate.

label: Evolved Gas Analysis (EGA)
summary: Thermal-analysis technique described under Techniques.

label: excitation energy
summary: The energy required to excite an electron to a higher energy level, referenced for argon.

label: Exothermic Reaction
summary: The process using CH4 to convert Br2 into HBr is slightly exothermic.

label: extreme classical limit
summary: Regime where ⟨A⟩ >> E0; no-loss line at zero energy is negligible and P(E) is Gaussian.

label: extreme quantum limit
summary: Regime in which ⟨A⟩ and kT are small relative to E0, recovering perturbation theory results.

label: Fabrication of Self-Organized Quantum Dots by the Stranski–Krastanow Growth Method
summary: Growth method for fabricating self-organized quantum dots.

label: Fast homoepitaxy of SiC
summary: High growth-rate epitaxy (up to 100 μm/h or more) enabled by altered gas-phase chemistry and reactor design.

label: FE-DBD plasma treatment
summary: A non-thermal plasma treatment method used for sterilization and blood coagulation without damaging surrounding tissue.

label: Ferrite–austenite transformation temperature
summary: Temperature boundary (~723°C) below which diamond deposition rate on steel can be affected.

label: Field Emission Microscopy (FEM)
summary: technique imaging the tip with emission dependent on crystallographic orientation; often uses plate anode

label: Field Emission Spectroscopy (FES)
summary: one of the adsorption/diffusion example techniques in FEM/FES/TFE discussion

label: Filament temperature
summary: Filament temperature in hot-filament CVD; increases near 1700–2200°C, affecting CN/H concentrations and reactions.

label: Filament temperature (max)
summary: Maximum filament temperature around 2500 K in hot-filament CVD environment.

label: film–film bonding energy
summary: magnitude of energy for film atoms bonding to other film atoms (E_f)

label: film–substrate bonding energy
summary: magnitude of energy for film atoms bonding to the substrate (E_fs)

label: Film thickness
summary: Nd-doped PZT film thickness: 500 nm.

label: film thickness h
summary: Film thickness in the model system, relevant to blocking and glide of threading dislocations.

label: film thickness h_f
summary: Thickness of the thin film; h_f = 5 μm in the example.

label: Finite element calculation
summary: Finite element method calculation referenced in analysis.

label: Finite element simulations
summary: Numerical simulations used to compare predicted stresses with FE results.

label: Fire gilding
summary: Gilding process using mercury; health hazard due to mercury vapor; practiced into the 19th century.

label: First-order consecutive reactions
summary: A type of reaction where the rate of formation of products is governed by the smallest rate constant in a series of reactions.

label: First principles modeling
summary: Accurate modeling techniques used for determining electronic and excitation processes of point defects.

label: Float-glass process
summary: Continuous deposition operation on hot glass (float-glass process)

label: Floating field-ring (FFR) terminations
summary: Concentric p+ rings surrounding the main junction; formed in the same processing step; reduce lateral electric field and increase blocking voltage.

label: Floating Field Rings (FFRs)
summary: Edge termination using floating rings to terminate field lines.

label: Flow method in open tubes
summary: Vapor-phase growth method for ZnS using open-tube flow.

label: Fluidized-Bed CVD of Graphite
summary: Deposition of pyrolytic graphite in a fluidized bed; often yields isotropic structure.

label: Fluorine termination of diamond surfaces
summary: F-terminated surfaces with C–F or CFx species; potential for diamond MIS structures using CaF2 gate insulators.

label: Fluorocarbon feedstocks
summary: Gases used in etching processes that influence etch rates and selectivity.

label: Flux evaporation rate
summary: Flux evaporation rate for MoO3-B2O3 flux: 5.8 × 10−4 g/(h mm²).

label: Flux growth
summary: High-temperature solution growth method; lead vanadate Pb2V2O7 commonly used as flux; challenges include oxygen stoichiometry, multi-phase formation, flux impurities, and flux removal.

label: Flux growth method
summary: Crystal growth method from high-temperature solutions for Bi-based oxides.

label: forced oscillator model
summary: Model used to evaluate energy loss distribution when a forced oscillator couples to excitations (phonons, electron-hole pairs).

label: Forcefill (Hi-Fill) process
summary: High isostatic pressure-assisted filling to plastically flow metal into vias at relatively low temperatures.

label: Forepressure
summary: Pressure region preceding operation of the main pumps.

label: Forward-active mode
summary: Biasing condition with EB junction forward-biased and CB junction reverse-biased.

label: Forward-active region
summary: Operational region of the power BJT where CB junction is reverse biased and minority carriers are not injected into the collector drift region.

label: Forward Blocking Regime

label: Forward Conducting Regime

label: Four-point bending

label: Four-point flexure test configuration
summary: Symmetric four-point bend loading with central notch and delamination precrack for interfacial fracture testing.

label: FPLAPW
summary: Full potential, linearized augmented plane wave method used for spin-density calculations.

label: Fragmentation
summary: The process where molecules break into atoms upon impact with a surface, dependent on impact energy.

label: Frank-Van der Merwe growth
summary: Layer-by-layer epitaxial growth mode (FV growth).

label: Free carrier concentration spectroscopy (FCCS)
summary: Analytical method for donor/acceptor density without data fitting.

label: frequencies
summary: Operating condition for RF plasma systems, typically in the range of 2–4 MHz.

label: Fridman-Macheret α-Model
summary: A model used to determine the relative accuracy for application to endothermic, exothermic, and thermo-neutral reactions.

label: Friedman analysis
summary: Isoconversional kinetic analysis using identical conversion levels across conditions.

label: Full-width at half-maximum
summary: FWHM; a measurement parameter listed in Acronyms.

label: ΔG*
summary: Nucleation energy barrier ΔG*, governing nucleus stability.

label: ΔG_s

label: ΔG_v
summary: Volume free energy change associated with island formation per unit area.

label: gamma_fv
summary: Film-vapor surface energy γ_fv.

label: gamma_m (mismatch shear strain)
summary: Mismatch shear strain parameter related to the background field: gamma_xz = 1/2 gamma_m.

label: gamma_sf
summary: Interfacial energy between film and substrate γ_sf.

label: gamma_sv
summary: Surface energy of substrate-vapor interface γ_sv.

label: GaN oxidation in Oxygen ambient at 850 °C for 12 h
summary: Thermal oxidation of GaN in an oxygen ambient to form β-Ga2O3.

label: gap between charge and seed
summary: Gap between the charge and seed affecting growth in the system.

label: Gas composition during BEN
summary: Gas mixture used during BEN: H2 (96%) and CH4 (4%).

label: gas density
summary: The density of gas denoted as ng, relevant in the context of capacitive discharge.

label: Gas Flow Rate
summary: Flow rate of the main gas influences discharge stability in the MW-plasma jet.

label: Gas flow velocity 600 cm/s
summary: Uo = 600 cm/s in the axisymmetric stagnation flow model.

label: Gas mixture for CVD (N2/CH4/H2)
summary: Gas composition used in diamond growth experiments; example: 0.5 N2 / 2 CH4 / 100 H2.

label: Gas-phase oxygen addition (0.25%)
summary: Adding a small amount of O2 to the gas phase to improve growth with boron doping and suppress soot.

label: Gas-Phase Synthesis of Carbides
summary: A plasma-chemical synthesis process for producing carbides from halides and hydrocarbons.

label: Gas Pressure
summary: Gas pressure maintained at 10 kPa in the equilibrium calculations.

label: Gas pressure (P)
summary: Inert sputtering gas pressure affecting film microstructure.

label: Gas purity
summary: Purity level of gases used in processes.

label: Gas Source MBE
summary: MBE variant using gas sources for reactants.

label: Gas supersaturation
summary: Gas-phase supersaturation governs nucleation and morphology; low supersaturation favors single-crystal growth, high supersaturation promotes amorphous deposition.

label: Gas temperature range
summary: Gas temperature varied from 500 to 3500 K to determine equilibrium species concentrations.

label: GaSb deposition temperature
summary: Deposition temperature for GaSb on AlSb substrates: 500°C.

label: Gasdynamic stimulation of CO2 dissociation
summary: A process where CO2 dissociation is stimulated by gas cooling and vibrational non-equilibrium in supersonic flow.

label: Gate Oxide Breakdown
summary: The amplitude of the voltage oscillation can be large enough to break down the gate oxide.

label: Gate voltage
summary: Gate voltage applied to MOSFET to control 2DEG density and conduction.

label: Gay-Lussac number
summary: Dimensionless group Ga = ΔT / T_ref; ratio of temperature difference to reference temperature.

label: Generalized Langevin equation
summary: Equation modeling energy exchange with memory kernel and random force in the primary zone.

label: Generalized plane strain
summary: two-dimensional conditions of generalized plane strain used in perturbation analysis.

label: Geometrically nonlinear deformation
summary: Deformation range where curvature distribution becomes nonuniform; nonlinear response.

label: glide plane orientation α
summary: Angle of the glide plane with respect to the interface; set to 90 degrees in the model.

label: gliding arc
summary: A type of electric discharge characterized by a continuous arc that glides along a surface.

label: Glow
summary: Discharge type used for enhancement of adsorption properties and durability of polyether and polyamide fibers.

label: Glow, 1–10 kHz
summary: Discharge type used for enhancement or wettability of polyether fabrics.

label: Glow, 50 Hz
summary: Discharge type used for enhancement of adhesion of polyethylene film for gluing sliding ski surface.

label: Glow, 70 kHz
summary: Discharge type used for metallization of polypropylene.

label: glow discharge
summary: A discharge method used for synthesizing KrF2 with specific pressure and cooling conditions.

label: Gold beating
summary: Ancient process to produce thin gold leaf; leaf thickness can reach sub-micron levels.

label: Gold coating (5 nm) for SEM
summary: Depositing a semi-transparent 5 nm gold layer to mitigate charging during SEM.

label: graded buffer layer
summary: Graded buffer layer used to progressively change lattice constant and suppress rapid dislocation nucleation.

label: Gradual-channel approximation
summary: One-dimensional electrostatics approximation used along the MOS channel.

label: Graphitization-etching method
summary: Graphitization-etching method to form a heavily doped surface region using Dc with low-energy implantation.

label: Grashof number
summary: Dimensionless number describing buoyancy-driven flow; used to assess convection relevance.

label: Grashof number (thermal)
summary: Dimensionless group Gr = g β ΔT L^3 / ν^2; ratio of buoyancy to viscous forces.

label: Grazing incidence angle y = 5°
summary: Grazing-angle geometry to make films effectively thicker for X-ray diffraction of thin films.

label: Grazing incidence geometry
summary: Diffraction geometry with incidence angle less than ~1°, controlling X-ray penetration depth.

label: Grazing-incidence X-ray scattering (GIXS)
summary: X-ray scattering technique using a grazing angle to probe near-surface strain; depth-resolved measurements possible.

label: Green’s Function Method
summary: Approach to determine impurity binding energy using a delta-function potential.

label: Green’s functions approach
summary: Green’s function method used to calculate deep center energies.

label: grid-patterned growth
summary: A growth method that effectively reduces dislocation density in heteroepitaxial diamond.

label: grid-patterned nucleation region
summary: A specific growth condition with a grid pattern of 100-μm spacing used for diamond growth.

label: Griffith condition for delamination
summary: Griffith criterion equating the energy release rate to γ for delamination front.

label: Growth along [001]
summary: Growth orientation for GaAs/AlAs and GaAs/AlSb superlattices.

label: growth flux
summary: Growth flux during deposition providing supersaturated adatoms on the growth surface.

label: Growth flux (Rgr)
summary: Mass or volume flux onto the growth surface; related to supersaturation of the vapor.

label: growth interface temperature
summary: Temperature at the growth interface (z = 0) within a boundary layer (~500 μm).

label: α, β, γ growth parameters
summary: Geometric model parameters used to evaluate facet growth advantages relative to (1 0 0).

label: growth plasma pressure
summary: plasma pressure increased from 10-20 Torr to 100 Torr during growth

label: Growth pressure
summary: SiC CVD growth pressures cited: 25 kPa; 11 kPa; 4 kPa; and atmospheric pressure in comparisons.

label: Growth rate
summary: Growth rate values: below a few μm h−1 at low temperature; ~50 μm h−1 at 1700 °C with 0.2° off-axis.

label: Growth rate (≥50 μm h−1)
summary: Growth rate in epitaxial SiC; higher rates (≥50 μm h−1) associated with increased in-grown stacking faults.

label: growth sector boundary angle
summary: The angle that defines the boundary between top and lateral growth fronts during CVD diamond growth.

label: Growth Temperature
summary: The temperature range for single-crystal diamond growth is 800 °C to 1300 °C, with an optimal range of 750–1000 °C.

label: Growth temperature effects on defect densities
summary: Z1/2 and EH6/7 densities increase with higher growth temperature; trade-off with surface morphology.

label: guiding center motion
summary: Motion of a charged particle in a time-varying magnetic field.

label: H-diamond
summary: Hydrogen-terminated diamond, a specific form of diamond used in the study of carrier transport.

label: h* (gap width)
summary: Width of the glide-plane gap between boundary region and free surface used in blocking criterion.

label: H-plasma processes
summary: A process related to smoothing diamond surfaces, involving hydrogen plasma treatment.

label: H-termination
summary: A stable surface condition of diamond at ambient temperature, chemically active and can convert to oxygen-termination under certain conditions.

label: H2 annealing
summary: Hydrogen atmosphere annealing used to facilitate catalytic etching by Ni.

label: H2/CH4 Flow Rates
summary: Flow rates of hydrogen and methane during deposition, set at 300 and 9 sccm, respectively.

label: H2 dissociation
summary: The process of breaking down hydrogen molecules into atoms.

label: H2/O2 plasma etching
summary: Hydrogen/oxygen plasma treatment to remove surface contamination and minimize stress.

label: H2O/C ratio in gas phase
summary: Water to carbon ratio in the gas phase during sintering or deposition.

label: Hall effect measurements
summary: Electrical characterization to measure carrier density and mobility in SiC; isolation via semi-insulating substrate or pn junction.

label: Halogen-Based Deposition
summary: Halogen-based reactions can deposit diamond; thermodynamics must favor solid carbon and stable gaseous fluorides/chlorides.

label: Harmonic approximation
summary: Approximation keeping only second-order terms in the displacement expansion of the ion Hamiltonian; treats lattice vibrations as a set of independent harmonic oscillators.

label: HCl oxidation
summary: A plasma-chemical process for producing molecular chlorine from HCl.

label: Heat exchanger method
summary: Growth method used for bismuth germanate crystals in conjunction with flux approaches.

label: Heat Exchanger Method (HEM)
summary: Heat exchanger method for growing BGO crystals; reported to have less melt stoichiometry variation than Cz.

label: Heat resistance network
summary: One-dimensional heat resistance network analysis for conduction and radiation in a SiC growth system.

label: heat treatment
summary: Process that shortened the time response of category II detectors.

label: Heat treatment above 500°C
summary: Heat-treatment above 500°C during fabrication can cause hillocks or cracks due to unequal thermal expansion.

label: Heat treatment of deposited diamond
summary: Heat treatment leading to diamond dissolution into an unsaturated Co binder; reduces interfacial voids but may smooth interface and induce stresses.

label: Heavy-Particle Collisions
summary: Collisions between heavy particles with varying energies, characterized by specific timescales for collisions.

label: Helicon Discharge
summary: A discharge excited at rf frequencies, utilizing a whistler wave for heating the bulk plasma electrons.

label: Helicon Mode Decay Constants
summary: Constants related to the decay of helicon modes in plasma.

label: Helium atmosphere
summary: Helium gas environment used in measurements to improve heat transport.

label: Henry's law adsorption (2D gas)
summary: Two-dimensional adsorption behavior described by Henry's law for mobile adatoms.

label: Heteroepitaxial diamond growth
summary: Heteroepitaxial growth on non-diamond substrates (e.g., silicon); includes bias-enhanced nucleation and buffer layer steps.

label: Heteroepitaxial growth
summary: A promising approach to achieve single-crystal diamond wafers with large sizes and controlled electrical properties.

label: heteroepitaxy
summary: Heteroepitaxy is the process used to grow single-crystal diamond films on substrates like Ir and Al₂O₃.

label: Heterogeneous Integration
summary: A technology combining diamond PMOS with other NMOS or integrating diamond and GaN on the same substrate.

label: HFCVD
summary: Widely used reactor for diamond deposition, utilizing hot filaments to decompose reaction gases into atomic species.

label: high anodic over-potentials
summary: Conditions under which the H-terminated surface can be converted to an O-terminated one.

label: high density
summary: A condition referring to a high density of plasma or particles.

label: high density inductively coupled mode
summary: Operational mode of discharge above 150 W power.

label: High-Density Oxygen Discharge
summary: A model for a high-density oxygen discharge slab in a quartz chamber at low pressures.

label: High-density plasma (HDP) / Electron cyclotron resonance (ECR) PECVD
summary: Remote HDP/ECR plasma configurations used to improve film deposition and etching control.

label: High-energy particle irradiation
summary: Irradiation process capable of creating point defects in materials.

label: high-energy physics experiments
summary: Experiments that investigate the properties and interactions of matter at very small distances using large accelerators and complex detectors.

label: High flame speed
summary: Simulation result indicating flame speed influences atomic hydrogen production.

label: High-frequency C–V measurement
summary: High-frequency C–V measurement used in IT extraction.

label: high-frequency capacitive discharge
summary: A type of discharge characterized by high frequency, such as 13.56 MHz.

label: high-frequency discharges
summary: Used for supersonic and hypersonic drag reduction.

label: High-frequency induction
summary: High-frequency induction.

label: High-frequency plasma activation for Si3N4 deposition
summary: Deposition enhanced by high-frequency (13.56 MHz) plasma or ECR/plasma; enables lower temperatures.

label: high gas flows
summary: Technique used to sweep particles out of the system.

label: High-intensity arc discharges
summary: Used for the conversion of tetrafluoromethane into tetrafluoroethylene.

label: High Ionization Degree
summary: Transitions between electronically excited states are mostly due to collisions with plasma electrons at ionization degrees exceeding 10^-6.

label: High-level injection
summary: Injection regime with Δn ≫ n0 and Δp ≫ p0, allowing Δn ≈ Δp.

label: High–Low Method
summary: Method using low- and high-frequency C–V measurements to extract D_IT.

label: High mechanical stresses
summary: High mechanical stresses.

label: High-power DC-arcjet plasma CVD
summary: High-power DC-arcjet plasma CVD method for large-area CVD-diamond growth.

label: High Pressure
summary: Condition where ion-ion recombination is kinetically limited by ion drift and multiple collisions.

label: High pressure and high temperature (HPHT) synthesis
summary: Another method developed for growing high quality diamond.

label: High-pressure arc discharges
summary: Discharges with electron temperatures of approximately 0.1–2 V and particle densities of 10^{14}–10^{19} cm⁻³.

label: High-Pressure Arcs
summary: Plasma in molecular gases at pressures greater than or equal to 1 atm, usually in quasi equilibrium at any currents.

label: High-Pressure Argon Discharge
summary: A model for the decay of electron density and temperature after the power has been turned off in a high-pressure argon discharge.

label: high-pressure collisional discharge
summary: A discharge characterized by high pressure where v << nm << vpe.

label: High-Pressure High α Electronegative Regime
summary: A regime characterized by positive and negative ion balance in a chlorine discharge.

label: High-Pressure High Temperature (HPHT)
summary: Technique used for producing diamonds under high pressure and temperature conditions.

label: High-Pressure High-Temperature (HPHT) Method
summary: A method used to synthesize artificial diamond under high pressure and temperature.

label: High Pressure High Temperature (HPHT) synthesis
summary: Diamond synthesis technique using HPHT (~1500 °C and 5 GPa) with seed diameters up to ~10 mm.

label: High pressure high temperature techniques
summary: Methods used for growing high purity diamond plates.

label: High-Pressure Hollow Cathode Discharge
summary: A discharge process operated at a higher pressure of 1 Torr.

label: High-Pressure Steady-State Discharge
summary: A discharge condition created in argon gas by ultraviolet radiation.

label: High Temperature
summary: Sufficiently high temperatures to enable defect diffusion and formation of atmospheres.

label: High-Temperature and High-Pressure Method (HPHT)
summary: A method for synthesizing diamonds that replicates the natural conditions found deep within the Earth.

label: High-temperature annealing
summary: Thermal annealing used to repair implantation-induced damage and promote oxide formation.

label: High-temperature annealing cycle
summary: Short-duration, high-temperature annealing cycle used to activate viscous flow in the substrate.

label: High-temperature Ar annealing
summary: Annealing in argon at high temperature after carbon ion implantation to eliminate Z1/2 and EH6/7 centers.

label: High-Temperature Chemical Vapor Deposition
summary: CVD process for SiC with high operating temperatures.

label: High Temperature CVD (HTCVD)
summary: HTCVD variant with inverted flow and graphite susceptor to achieve thicker films; operates at 2200–2300°C.

label: high_temperature_data
summary: High-temperature data around 1050°C for g111 vs g110 vs g113 and g001 comparisons in Si/Ge data.

label: High-temperature deposition
summary: High-temperature deposition process condition.

label: High Temperature Equilibrium
summary: A condition where hydrogen gas at high temperature is used to convert SiO₂ to silicon.

label: High-Temperature High-Pressure (HTHP)
summary: A method used to grow diamonds by transforming graphite into diamond under high temperature and pressure.

label: High-temperature operation
summary: Capability to operate at temperatures well above 200 °C for certain SiC devices.

label: High-temperature operation (≥700°C)
summary: Diamond windows requiring coatings that withstand high temperatures up to or beyond 700°C.

label: high vacuum
summary: Chamber evacuated to a high vacuum during field ion microscopy.

label: High-vacuum CVD
summary: Chemical vapor deposition performed under high vacuum.

label: Holding time
summary: Holding time for flux evaporation: 30 h.

label: Homoepitaxial diamond growth
summary: Growth of diamond on diamond substrates (homoepitaxy).

label: homoepitaxial growth
summary: A process used to fabricate (001)-oriented diamond p–i–n junction diodes with low impurity concentration.

label: Homoepitaxial growth at extremely low CH4/H2 ratios
summary: Growth method enabling step-terrace formation by suppressing 2D island growth via hydrogen radical etching.

label: Homoepitaxy
summary: Epitaxial growth of a material on a substrate of the same material (e.g., Si on Si).

label: Homogeneous broadening
summary: Broadening reflected by a finite lifetime Γ (Lorentzian resonance); typically smaller than inhomogeneous broadening in MQWs.

label: Hopping conduction
summary: Conduction mechanism observed in Hall data on boron-doped diamond at lower impurity densities.

label: Hot acid mixture treatment (H2SO4:HNO3 = 3:1) at 230 °C for 30 min
summary: O-termination of n-type diamond(001) surface by hot acid mixture

label: Hot-filament assisted CVD
summary: CVD method using hot filament to assist deposition (Ihara case at 135°C).

label: Hot-Filament Chemical Vapor Deposition
summary: Hot-filament CVD process used for diamond film growth; involves a heated filament to dissociate gas species.

label: Hot filament chemical vapor deposition (HFCVD)
summary: A technique used to grow N-UNCD films, allowing for low-cost and large-area production.

label: Hot filament chemical vapour deposition
summary: A technique for growing diamond films by thermally cracking hydrogen.

label: hot filament CVD
summary: A chemical vapor deposition process used to grow the nanocrystalline diamond structure on a silicon substrate.

label: Hot Filament (HF) technique
summary: Technique used for hydrogen termination on diamond surfaces.

label: Hot-filament method
summary: Hot-filament deposition method.

label: hot-filament-substrate distance ≤ 1 cm
summary: Distance between hot filament and substrate typically 1 cm or less.

label: Hot implantation
summary: Implantation performed at elevated temperatures to reduce damage and aid recovery.

label: Hot pressing isostatic
summary: Hot pressing isostatic processing.

label: Hot PVD
summary: Elevated deposition temperature to promote metal transport and better trench filling.

label: Hot-Wall CVD
summary: Gas-phase chemical vapor deposition method for SiC growth in a hot-wall configuration.

label: Hot-Wall Halogen-Assisted CVD
summary: Halogen-assisted CVD using a hot furnace with halogen species; substrate temperatures typically range from 250°C to 750°C.

label: HPHT Diamond Synthesis
summary: High-Pressure/High-Temperature synthesis of diamond, used for grit production distinct from CVD diamond.

label: HPHT technique
summary: High-pressure high-temperature synthesis technique for diamonds.

label: HTCVD
summary: High-temperature chemical vapor deposition growth technique for SiC boules.

label: Hybrid Junction Termination Extension (Hybrid-JTE)
summary: Hybrid JTE formed by narrowing RA-JTE to 90 μm and placing the narrowed RA-JTE near the p+ main junction and MFZ-JTE beside it.

label: Hybrid process
summary: Hybrid deposition process.

label: Hydrocarbon gas pyrolysis
summary: Pyrolysis of hydrocarbon gas.

label: Hydrogen annealing
summary: Hydrogen annealing at 800–900 C; affects interface states, particularly on SiC(0001) and (1120).

label: hydrogen atmosphere
summary: Hydrogen gas atmosphere used with silicon-source gases in deposition experiments.

label: Hydrogen content
summary: Amount of hydrogen incorporated into DLC, affecting index of refraction and electrical conductivity.

label: hydrogen etching
summary: A process involving the etching of diamond surfaces by hydrogen, leading to the formation of methane.

label: Hydrogen (H2) etching
summary: Etching technique used prior to epitaxial growth to influence defects.

label: Hydrogen partial pressure
summary: Partial pressure of molecular hydrogen influences discharge stability in the MW-plasma jet.

label: Hydrogen passivation
summary: Hydrogen passivation of dangling bonds near the interface; reduces DIT in silicon technology; limited effect in SiC; involves hydrogen-containing gas during annealing.

label: Hydrogen passivation of amorphous silicon
summary: Passivation of dangling bonds by hydrogen to reduce defect density and raise bandgap.

label: Hydrogen peroxide (H2O2)
summary: Oxidizing agent used in hydrothermal synthesis of PbTiO3 powders.

label: Hydrogen Plasma
summary: A non-thermal plasma used for reducing metal oxide layers.

label: hydrogen plasma exposure
summary: Exposure to hydrogen plasma; yields NEA and 2x1 reconstruction on certain diamond surfaces.

label: Hydrogen Plasma Treatment
summary: Hydrogen-plasma treatment used to form atomically flat diamond(1 1 1) surfaces with equilateral triangular pits.

label: Hydrogen reduction
summary: Hydrogen reduction process.

label: Hydrogen-terminated As surface during MOCVD
summary: H-terminated As surface enhances the surface diffusion of column-III species and increases critical terrace width.

label: Hydrogen-terminated diamond (Diamond:H)
summary: Diamond surface terminated with hydrogen; used to create a 2D hole gas in FETs and influence interfaces.

label: Hydrogen termination
summary: A process that lowers the electron affinity and ionization potential of diamond, enabling surface conductivity.

label: Hydrogen termination (C–H termination) of diamond surface
summary: Hydrogen-terminated diamond surface facilitating 2DHG formation.

label: Hydrogen termination (H-termination) on diamond surface
summary: Surface termination introducing hydrogen; associated with NEA and subsurface p-type conduction.

label: Hydrogen termination of diamond surface
summary: Hydrogen termination produces a surface conduction channel (hole conduction) enabling surface-channel FETs.

label: Hydrogen termination of diamond surfaces
summary: Hydrogen adsorption on diamond surfaces to form H-terminated surfaces; H-terminated diamond(1 0 0) and (1 1 1) structures; associated with NEA.

label: Hydrolysis
summary: Hydrolysis process.

label: Hydrostatic pressure
summary: Pressure used to convert Ge into a DX center; observed effect in the 0.5–1.0 GPa range.

label: Hydrostatic strain (component of biaxial strain)
summary: Hydrostatic part of biaxial strain described by a diagonal tensor with equal in-plane components.

label: hydrostatic stress
summary: Hydrostatic (isotropic) stress applied to zincblende crystal; represented by X with equal diagonal components.

label: Hydrothermal growth
summary: Hydrothermal crystal growth method discussed as an alternative to other high-temperature methods for rare-earth vanadates.

label: Hydrothermal growth conditions (Y2O3–V2O5–H2O)
summary: Hydrothermal growth under mild conditions identifying crystallization field for RVO4 crystals; parameters include T = 240 C, P ~ 100 bar, 60% fill, pH varied.

label: Hydrothermal method
summary: Hydrothermal growth/ crystallization used for ZnSe, ZnTe, ZnO; allows large crystals under high pressure and controlled OH−/ Li+ presence.

label: Hydrothermal synthesis
summary: Aqueous, high-temperature/pressure method for dissolving and recrystallizing materials.

label: i*
summary: Critical nucleus size in the atomistic nucleation model.

label: ICP
summary: Inductive-coupled plasma process.

label: ICP Etching
summary: Inductively Coupled Plasma etching used to remove subsurface damage and assess TD content.

label: Ideal quenching
summary: A process condition where CO2 molecules are decomposed in a high-temperature phase with a specific fraction of CO2 molecules decomposed.

label: Ignition Delay Time
summary: The time required for ignition to occur, specifically 5 ms in the discussed experiments.

label: Implantation energy
summary: Boron implantation energy of 30 keV used in TRIM simulations.

label: Implantation temperature
summary: Implantation conducted at 400 °C for boron implantation experiments.

label: in-situ anneal
summary: A thermal treatment process performed at 400 °C for 60 minutes prior to oxide deposition.

label: In situ chlorination
summary: In situ chlorination process.

label: In situ etching during CVD
summary: In situ etching steps using H2, HCl/H2, hydrocarbons/H2, or SiH4/H2 at high temperature prior to main growth.

label: In Situ Plasma Cleaning
summary: A cleaning process in semiconductor manufacturing that uses plasma to remove silicon oxide deposits from treatment chambers.

label: In situ PLD deposition
summary: In situ deposition with substrate heated to 550–750 C and partial oxygen in chamber to promote direct crystal growth.

label: In situ poling during LiNbO3 growth
summary: Poling performed by applying a high DC electric field along the growth direction [00.1], with the seed holder and crucible polarities arranged accordingly.

label: Incident Power
summary: Microwave power incident on plasma: 600 W.

label: Incompressible substrate (ν_s = 1/2)
summary: Substrate treated as incompressible (ν_s = 1/2) in the analysis.

label: Indirect Plasma Treatment
summary: A method of sterilization using a jet of plasma afterglow generated remotely, lacking direct contact with the tissue.

label: Inductive Coupled Plasma (ICP)
summary: A plasma etching technique with high plasma density, used for diamond patterning.

label: inductive discharge
summary: A low pressure, electropositive inductive discharge characterized by electron power absorption and loss.

label: Inductive Discharge Equilibrium
summary: A state in plasma where the inductive discharge parameters are balanced.

label: Inductive Discharges
summary: A section detailing high-density, low-pressure inductive discharges and their configurations.

label: Inductively Coupled Plasma
summary: A plasma where power is transferred from electric fields to plasma electrons through collisional and collisionless heating processes.

label: inert gas pressure
summary: Pressure of inert gas used during SiC growth; range includes low to high (e.g., 133 to 26666 Pa).

label: Inert gas pressure during Bridgman growth
summary: Use of inert gas to reduce loss of volatile components; typical pressures mentioned range up to 20 MPa depending on setup.

label: infrared temperature measurements
summary: A method used to monitor substrate growth temperature during polycrystalline diamond growth.

label: Inhomogeneous broadening
summary: Broadening due to a distribution of oscillator frequencies (g); MQWs exhibit broadening from random layer-thickness fluctuations, leading to non-conservation of wavevector for certain excitations.

label: Initial gas composition
summary: Initial N2/CH4/H2 mix with molar ratios 0.1/1/100 used for equilibrium calculations.

label: InP substrate growth temperature range
summary: Substrate temperatures of 350–380°C used for InP growth from In and P2 on (100) InP.

label: Instrumented nanoindentation
summary: Nanoindentation with continuous load-displacement recording and possible oscillatory loading for stiffness measurement.

label: Integration PVD
summary: Integration using PVD (physical vapor deposition).

label: Intensity-modulated radiotherapy (IMRT)
summary: A radiotherapy technique that delivers a highly conformed dose to irregularly shaped targets.

label: Interface energy g*
summary: Interfacial energy between deposited material A and substrate B.

label: Interfacial tension
summary: Interfacial tension between hydroxyapatite surfaces and solution phases; reported values range from 10 to 120 mJ/m2.

label: Intraband scattering by acoustic phonons
summary: Electron scattering within the same band via emission of an acoustic (LA) phonon; energy and momentum conserved; quasi-elastic for small q.

label: Inverse photoemission
summary: Technique analogous to photoemission that maps conduction-band dispersion by detecting emitted photons when electrons scatter to lower-energy states.

label: Inversion regime
summary: Device regime in which the channel forms under oxide/interface influence, enabling hole conduction.

label: Iodide decomposition
summary: Decomposition of iodide species.

label: Ion/atom ratio (I/A)
summary: Critical ion/atom ratio for annealing of stress in Ge films by Ar bombardment.

label: Ion beam assisted deposition
summary: Film deposition with simultaneous ion bombardment to densify films and modify interfaces.

label: Ion-beam deposition
summary: Ion beam deposition method used for DLC film formation.

label: Ion-Beam Implantation
summary: A technology that directs high-energy ions onto surfaces for penetration into the atomic structure of a material.

label: Ion-beam milling
summary: Micromachining method used on Ge and ZnS substrates to create microstructures.

label: Ion-beam stitching
summary: High-energy ion beams create stitched, mixed interfacial regions to improve adhesion.

label: Ion Bombarding Energy
summary: The energy distribution of ions bombarding substrate surfaces is critical to plasma processing.

label: Ion Bombardment
summary: The process where ions from plasma damage the lipid layers of micro-organisms, contributing to their deactivation.

label: Ion bombardment induced (IBI)
summary: Ion bombardment induced processing method used in diamond materials work.

label: Ion Collision Frequency
summary: Frequency of collisions involving ions, denoted as \(\nu_i\).

label: Ion conversion reactions
summary: Reactions that lead to the preliminary formation of molecular ions, facilitating faster recombination.

label: Ion Drift Velocity
summary: The ambipolar ion drift velocity corresponds to an energy Ek = 10 V.

label: Ion Dynamics
summary: The behavior of ions in a plasma, particularly their contribution to the dielectric tensor.

label: Ion energy
summary: Kinetic energy of bombarding ions; influences sticking probability, sputtering, and implantation.

label: Ion energy-driven etching
summary: An etching process that combines the effects of XeF2 etchant gas and Ar+ ion beam sputtering, resulting in high etch rates.

label: ion enhanced etching
summary: An etching process that utilizes ion bombardment to enhance material removal.

label: Ion-enhanced inhibitor etching
summary: An etching process that uses inhibitor species to form a protective layer while allowing selective etching.

label: ion flux limited regime
summary: Etching regime where both vertical and horizontal surfaces are flooded with O atoms, and the vertical etch rate is determined by the arrival of energetic ions.

label: Ion implantation
summary: Processing technique to modify subsurface structure by energetic ions; includes electronic and nuclear energy losses.

label: ion interaction with matter
summary: The process by which ions deposit energy in diamond detectors, affecting the ionization profile.

label: Ion-Ion Recombination
summary: A process involving the neutralization of positive and negative ions in collisions.

label: ion irradiation
summary: A process used to initiate diamond nucleation on the Ir surface.

label: Ion milling thinning to a hole
summary: TEM sample preparation: thinning by ion milling until a hole appears after mounting wafers in epoxy.

label: Ion/neutral temperature
summary: Ion and neutral temperature in the plasma (0.03 eV).

label: Ion Plating
summary: evaporated film deposition process with a flux of high-energy ions causing sputtering before and during film formation.

label: Ion scattering spectroscopy
summary: Experimental technique used to probe surface melting and disorder on Pb(110).

label: ionization
summary: The process where an ion colliding with an atom transfers energy to a valence electron, leading to ionization.

label: Ionization by collision of heavy particles
summary: Occurs during ion–molecule or ion–atom collisions when the total energy exceeds the ionization potential.

label: Ionization by high-energy electrons
summary: Ionization process induced by electrons with high energy, typically in the range of 50 KeV to 1–2 MeV.

label: ionization chambers
summary: A type of direct-reading dosimeter that measures ionizing radiation.

label: Ionization Degree
summary: The ratio of electron density to neutral gas density, critical for CO2 dissociation efficiency.

label: ionization energy
summary: The energy required to remove an electron from an atom, specifically referenced for argon.

label: Ionized Physical Vapor Deposition (I-PVD)
summary: A plasma processing technique utilizing hollow cathode discharges at lower pressures.

label: Ionizing radiation
summary: Radiation that induces a change in electrical conductivity in diamond by producing free electron-hole pairs.

label: Ir(100) reconstruction temperature
summary: Temperature in excess of 800 K where Ir(100) undergoes 1×1 to 1×5 reconstruction.

label: Irradiating and annealing at 900 °C
summary: Enhances the production of the Si–V⁻ defect in CVD diamond.

label: Island growth
summary: Growth mode where material forms islands on the surface, often with large lattice mismatch.

label: ISO 11357-1
summary: DTA/Differential Thermal Analysis standard referenced in the text.

label: isobaric conditions
summary: Isobaric conditions (p constant, typically 1 bar) used in thermodynamic modeling.

label: isoelectric point
summary: The pH at which the surface charge of diamond surfaces is neutral.

label: Isothermal measurements
summary: Isothermal data collection as part of kinetic analyses.

label: Isotope dependence
summary: The shift of the lowest indirect energy gap from natural to 13C diamond is measured.

label: Isotropic etchant
summary: Etchant that removes material at the same rate in all directions.

label: isotropic flux
summary: A uniform distribution of precursor molecules incident on the trench.

label: Isotropic hardening
summary: Isotropic hardening rule where H is the tangent modulus and zeta' = H relates stress and plastic strain rate.

label: J_i
summary: Critical nucleation rate for clusters of size i.

label: JFET region doping
summary: Doping of the JFET region in the top drift epilayer; specified as about 1×10^17 cm^-3.

label: Johnson-Mehl-Avrami (JMA) equation
summary: Rate model for hydrothermal synthesis with df/dt = kt^m (1 − f); describes fraction reacted over time.

label: Johnson–Mehl–Avrami–Kolmogorov (JMAK) model
summary: Kinetic model for isothermal data with nucleation and growth; α(t) = 1 - exp(-kt^n).

label: Joule Heating
summary: Heating method provided by electric current of high amplitude and density, widely used in high-brightness radiation sources.

label: Junction Termination Extension (JTE)
summary: Edge-field-suppressing structures used in diamond Schottky diodes; can be formed by ion implantation or dielectric layers.

label: K
summary: Equilibrium constant for adsorption/desorption (k_ads/k_des).

label: k_ads
summary: Adsorption rate constant; varies as ~ exp(-E_ads/(k_B T)).

label: k_des
summary: Desorption rate constant; varies as ~ exp(-E_des/(k_B T)).

label: k·p expansion
summary: Perturbative expansion expressing wavefunctions as a function of crystal momentum k; can mix odd-parity states.

label: k·p Method
summary: Band dispersion extrapolated from zone center gaps and optical matrix elements by treating p·k and k^2 as perturbations; useful for small k and near high-symmetry points; diagonalization with basis functions to cover the Brillouin zone.

label: KAB growth flux composition (K2CO3:B2O3:NaF/NaF variants)
summary: Flux compositions used to control viscosity and nucleation during KAB growth (e.g., 1:1.8:0.2 NaF).

label: Kerr effect
summary: Nonlinear refractive index n = n0 + K E^2; used here to describe intensity-dependent ATR-SP resonance in a nonlinear prism.

label: Kinematic hardening
summary: Yield surface translates in stress space with continued shape; reflects back-stress and Bauschinger effect.

label: Kinetic energy 10 keV
summary: Kinetic energy of He atoms used in scattering study; 10 keV specified in problem statement.

label: Kinetic Monte Carlo (KMC)
summary: A modeling approach used to simulate the growth of diamond by considering various gas-phase and surface reactions.

label: Kissinger–Akahira–Sunose (KAS) method
summary: Isoconversional method using T_alpha,i for multiple conversions to estimate Ea.

label: Kissinger–Akahira–Sunose method
summary: KAS method for kinetic analysis; handles multistep processes.

label: Kissinger method
summary: Isoconversional method using peak temperature vs heating rate to estimate Ea.

label: Knudsen number
summary: Dimensionless group defined as Kn = A/L; ratio of gas mean free path to characteristic length.

label: KOH concentration
summary: Concentration of potassium hydroxide mineralizer; affects stability/yield regions in diagrams.

label: KOH etching
summary: KOH etching used to reveal dislocations around hexagonal voids.

label: Kohn–Luttinger parameters
summary: Parameters A1, A2, A3 in H_L; relate to valence-band dispersion.

label: Kronig–Penney model
summary: One-dimensional periodic square-well model used to describe envelope functions in superlattices.

label: Landau damping
summary: A process by which a wave transfers energy to electrons having velocities near the phase velocity of the wave.

label: Landau-Teller Formula
summary: A formula used to calculate the rate coefficient for VT relaxation of anharmonic oscillators.

label: Langevin capture
summary: A process involving the scattering of a charged particle leading to the formation of an ion–molecular complex.

label: Langevin trajectory simulations
summary: Langevin trajectory simulations used to model NO–metal surface interactions.

label: Langmuir
summary: Unit of exposure (1 Langmuir = 1e-6 torr·s) used to quantify gas exposure in vacuum photoemission.

label: Langmuir adsorption isotherm
summary: Langmuir model for adsorption on a fixed number of sites with configurational entropy.

label: Langmuir exposure
summary: Langmuir (L) exposure unit used to quantify surface exposure; typically ~1 L needed for a monolayer.

label: Langmuir–Hinshelwood kinetics
summary: A reaction mechanism involving two adsorbed species reacting on a surface.

label: Langmuir-Hinshelwood (LH) mechanism
summary: Surface reaction mechanism involving two chemisorbed species.

label: Langmuir Kinetics
summary: Langmuir kinetics describes the adsorption and desorption processes of molecules on a surface.

label: Langmuir-Rideal Mechanism
summary: Mechanism of surface recombination of F atoms during their transportation from remote plasma source for plasma cleaning.

label: Laplace transform
summary: Mathematical tool used in analyzing the reaction chain.

label: Large deflection bending theory
summary: theory used for bulge deflection under large deflection assumptions.

label: laser ablation
summary: A process that involves the removal of material through laser irradiation, affecting the structure of diamond.

label: laser-ablation process
summary: Laser ablation process generating particulates and affecting surface morphology.

label: Laser annealing
summary: Laser annealing used to remove crystallographic imperfections in surface layers.

label: Laser-beam fluence
summary: Laser-beam fluence on PNZT target during deposition: 0.2–3.0 J/cm2.

label: laser beams
summary: Used to break up particulates or push them out of the active region.

label: Laser cutting
summary: Cutting of CVD-diamond using laser radiation.

label: Laser cutting technique
summary: Laser cutting technique used in device processing.

label: Laser-enhanced chemical vapor deposition (LECVD)
summary: CVD process where laser/photo processing enhances and controls film deposition.

label: laser flash technique
summary: A method to determine thermal properties by exciting a thermal wave in a film.

label: Laser heating
summary: Laser heating of the sample to induce unidirectional epitaxial regrowth in a-Si.

label: Laser incident angle
summary: Angle between target surface and incoming laser beam: 45 degrees.

label: laser-induced graphitization
summary: A method that uses laser pulses to induce graphitization in diamond.

label: Laser-plasma coupling chemical vapor deposition (LPCCVD)
summary: Method combining laser-activated plasma with hydrocarbon chemistry for diamond formation.

label: Laser polishing
summary: Laser-based polishing of CVD-diamond surfaces.

label: Laser scanning speed
summary: Laser beam scanning speed during PNZT deposition: 8 mm/s.

label: Lattice misfit ε
summary: Lattice mismatch strain between film and substrate, defined as (a0(s) - a0(f))/a0(f).

label: Lattice-mismatched substrate
summary: Substrate with lattice mismatch influencing island growth behavior.

label: layer-by-layer growth
summary: Growth mode transition from three-dimensional island growth to layer-by-layer growth to achieve macroscopically flat surfaces.

label: LCAO model
summary: Linear combination of atomic orbitals model used to calculate hole miniband energies in GaAs/Ga1−xAlxAs superlattices.

label: LDA slab calculation
summary: Local Density Approximation slab calculation used to model surface electronic structure (Si(111) etc.).

label: LDD (Lightly Doped Drain)
summary: Lateral drift region structure used with RESURF to achieve higher breakdown voltages; depletion of LDD columns terminates field lines in base layer.

label: Lead precursor concentration
summary: Input concentration of lead precursor (0.1 m) in Pb–Ti–H2O yield diagrams.

label: Leak rate
summary: Total leak rate into the system, Q = Ql + Qo (true plus virtual leaks).

label: Lely method
summary: Early vapor growth method for SiC where crystals nucleate on a porous SiC layer inside a graphite crucible.

label: Lever rule
summary: Method to determine relative phase amounts in a two‑phase field.

label: Lift-off
summary: Lift-off processing used in Schottky electrode fabrication.

label: Lift-off process
summary: Lift-off processing step described in processing flow.

label: Lift-off process with ion implantation
summary: Lift-off separation technique used to fabricate freestanding mosaic substrates; relies on matching seed surface orientation to separation plane.

label: Lift-off through ion implantation
summary: Lift-off method to separate CVD-grown diamond from substrate after implantation and graphitization; graphite etched away.

label: LIGA
summary: Mold-based MEMS fabrication process: lithography, galvanoformung (electroforming), and abformung (molding).

label: Light doping technique
summary: Light doping technique used in diamond device fabrication.

label: LiNbO3 growth rates and rotation
summary: Seed pulling rate ~10 mm/h; seed rotation ~30 rpm during LiNbO3 crystal growth.

label: linearly graded composition
summary: Gradual change in lattice constant through thickness creating a virtual buffer layer.

label: Liquid phase bonding
summary: A processing method for joining materials via a liquid phase at the interface.

label: Liquid Phase Epitaxy
summary: Crystal growth technique listed under Growth Techniques.

label: Liquid phase epitaxy (LPE)
summary: Epitaxial growth from supersaturated melts; used historically for GaAs/AlGaAs devices; lower uniformity than vapor-phase methods.

label: Liquid Phase Epitaxy (LPE) of SiC
summary: Liquid phase epitaxy technique for growing SiC and related solid solutions.

label: Lithography
summary: Fabrication step in semiconductor manufacturing mentioned in context of chip fabrication.

label: Local Density Approximation (LDA)
summary: Approximation for exchange-correlation term V_xc as a function of local charge density; gives good ground-state properties but underestimates excitation energies.

label: Local response
summary: Local dielectric response where D at r depends only on E at the same point r.

label: Local Spin Density approximation
summary: Local Spin Density (LSD) approximation used for spin-polarized calculations.

label: Local Thermodynamic Equilibrium (LTE)
summary: A state of plasma where collisional processes are locally in equilibrium, differing from point to point in space and time.

label: Long emitter
summary: Emitter width much larger than diffusion length; used for simplifications.

label: Long-throw deposition
summary: Increase target-substrate distance to improve step/trench coverage by directing more normal flux.

label: Losev Model
summary: A semi-empirical model for calculating dissociation rate coefficients using vibrational temperature.

label: Loss Rate Coefficient
summary: The first-order rate coefficient for loss of particles to the walls is denoted as K_{loss}.

label: low density capacitively coupled mode
summary: Operational mode of discharge below 150 W power.

label: Low-Density Operation
summary: A regime where the conductivity is low and fields fully penetrate the plasma, affecting power absorption.

label: Low Energy Electron Diffraction (LEED)
summary: Diffraction technique for surface structure analysis using electrons with energies ~20–500 eV.

label: Low-energy electron irradiation
summary: Generation of Z1/2 and EH6/7 centers via irradiation in SiC.

label: low-energy heavy-ion experiments
summary: Experiments involving the use of low-energy heavy ions for particle identification and spectroscopy.

label: Low-energy presputtering
summary: Presputtering with ions 100 eV to a few keV to clean substrates and enhance adhesion.

label: Low-frequency C–V measurement
summary: Quasi-static C–V measurement used in IT extraction.

label: low gas temperatures
summary: Conditions under which many discharge systems operate, allowing for collisionless sheath consideration.

label: Low growth temperature limit
summary: Thermally activated surface-diffusion-incorporation time implies a low temperature limit for good epitaxy.

label: Low-level injection
summary: Assumption enabling minority carrier diffusion equations in the analysis.

label: Low Pressure
summary: Conditions where temperatures of electrons and neutrals can differ, specifically at pressures less than or equal to 0.1 Torr.

label: Low-Pressure Capacitive RF Plasma
summary: A type of plasma where the electron distribution may not be Maxwellian.

label: Low-pressure chemical vapor deposition (LPCVD)
summary: Deposition process used to cap Al film with a SiOx layer.

label: Low-pressure continuous RF discharge
summary: A treatment method for high-density PE film and powder characterized by specific discharge power and voltage.

label: Low Pressure CVD
summary: CVD conducted below atmospheric pressure to improve deposition rates and uniformity.

label: Low-Pressure DC Glow Discharge
summary: A method for diamond film synthesis with high growth rates, utilizing a grounded anode and hydrogen with methane.

label: Low-Pressure Diamond Deposition (CVD)
summary: Diamond synthesis outside the carbon phase diagram, growth from gas phase via crystal growth in low-pressure conditions.

label: Low-Pressure Discharge
summary: A discharge condition characterized by low pressure, affecting the behavior of electrons and fields.

label: Low-pressure discharges
summary: Discharges characterized by electron temperatures of approximately 1–10 V and particle densities of 10^8–10^{13} cm⁻³.

label: Low-pressure environment
summary: Low-pressure synthesis environment for diamond production, as suggested by the chapter title.

label: Low-Pressure Glow Discharges
summary: Strongly non-equilibrium discharges characterized by room or slightly elevated temperatures.

label: low pressure materials processing discharges
summary: Conditions under which three-body reactions are discussed.

label: Low-Pressure Operation
summary: A condition required for high-density source development that leads to severe pumping requirements.

label: Low-pressure Plasma
summary: A state of plasma confinement characterized by low pressure, allowing for efficient confinement of energetic electrons.

label: Low-Pressure Plasma CVD
summary: Plasma CVD conducted at reduced pressure to enable easier substrate temperature control.

label: low-pressure RF-CCP discharge plasma
summary: A plasma treatment method used to modify polymer surfaces for controlling stem cell behavior.

label: Low-pressure RF discharges
summary: Discharges at pressures less than or equal to 0.1 Torr, commonly used in electronics.

label: Low substrate temperature
summary: Maintaining a low substrate temperature helps in the survival of diamondoid molecules during initial growth phases.

label: low temperature
summary: Conditions below room temperature affecting the Raman line width.

label: low temperature deposition
summary: A process condition that may compromise the properties of diamond when applied to silicon.

label: Low-Temperature Plasma
summary: A state of plasma characterized by low temperatures, significant in various chemical processes.

label: low temperatures
summary: Low temperature regime where ionized impurity scattering can dominate mobility.

label: Low Thermal Gradient Czochralski (LTG Cz) method
summary: LTG Cz method to achieve a low temperature gradient across seed–melt interface for high-quality BGO crystals.

label: Low-voltage planar device operation
summary: Planar CVD-diamond detector operates at low voltages around 20 V.

label: LPCVD
summary: Low-pressure CVD processes for thin film deposition.

label: LPE
summary: Liquid-phase epitaxy for SiC growth from a Si-based melt; includes micropipe closing and C/Si considerations.

label: LPE_growth_at_840C_for_2h
summary: Liquid phase epitaxy growth performed at 840°C for 2 hours using KC1 flux.

label: LPE (Liquid Phase Epitaxy)
summary: Epitaxial growth method used historically for III–V devices; cited with Alferov.

label: LTG Cz method
summary: Low temperature gradient Czochralski growth method used to grow nearly perfect bismuth germanate crystals.

label: τ_m
summary: Equi-biaxial stress due to mismatch ahead of the delamination front.

label: Magnetic Field
summary: Varies from 100 G for typical processing discharges up to 1000 G for fundamental plasma studies.

label: Magnetic field B_z
summary: Magnetic field oriented along the z-axis.

label: Magnetron Configuration
summary: A configuration using a dc magnetic field at the cathode to confine secondary electrons during sputtering discharge.

label: Magnetron sputtering
summary: Deposition process using magnetic confinement of the plasma to achieve high deposition rates.

label: marginally classical regime
summary: Regime between extreme quantum and extreme classical where most experiments operate.

label: Marrone-Treanor Model
summary: A model that assumes an exponential distribution of probabilities for dissociation from different vibrational levels.

label: Maskless surface formation
summary: Maskless approach to limit growth areas and form step-free diamond surfaces.

label: Mass deposition rate (LADS)
summary: Mass deposition rate achieved with LADS is about 30 mg/hr.

label: Mass deposition rate (NIRIM)
summary: Mass deposition rate possible with the NIRIM reactor is about 0.5 mg/hr.

label: Matrix Sheath
summary: A type of high-voltage sheath with a uniform ion density, characterized by a highly negative potential.

label: Maxwell boundary conditions at dielectric interfaces
summary: Continuity of tangential electric field across interfaces between dielectric media.

label: Maxwellian Distribution
summary: A statistical distribution of particle velocities in a plasma, often used in collision frequency calculations.

label: Maxwellian electron distribution
summary: A statistical distribution of electron energies used in calculating rate constants.

label: MBE
summary: Molecular beam epitaxy with atomic-level control; uses solid or gas sources; enables polytype engineering.

label: MBE (Molecular Beam Epitaxy)
summary: Selective epitaxy approach using shadow masks to pattern GaAs, AlGaAs, InAs; mask-related defects discussed.

label: Mean electron speed
summary: The average speed of electrons in a plasma.

label: Mean Free Path
summary: The average distance a particle travels between collisions, inversely related to the density and cross section.

label: Mechanical boundary conditions
summary: Atomic displacements of confined LO phonons vanish at interfaces (one approach to satisfy both mechanical and Maxwell boundary conditions).

label: Mechanical boundary conditions (omitted in initial analysis)
summary: Note that mechanical boundary conditions were neglected in the initial electrostatic treatment; later works include them.

label: Mechanical polishing
summary: Mechanical polishing to improve surface planarity before vacuum processing.

label: Mechanical renucleation (rubbing or brushing)
summary: Mechanical method to interrupt tungsten grain growth and renucleate the surface.

label: mechanochemical oxidation
summary: A mechanism important for coal conversion into carbon suboxides involving a sequence of mechanochemical reactions.

label: Melt growth
summary: General melt-growth methods discussed for borate crystals (e.g., BBO).

label: Melt temperature for Bi4Ge3O12 growth
summary: Bi4Ge3O12 melt stabilized at 1040°C during LTG growth sequence.

label: Membrane theory
summary: theory for bulge deflection emphasizing membrane behavior.

label: Mesa-Field-Plate Termination Structure
summary: Mesa-field-plate termination concept for GaN power devices.

label: Mesotaxy
summary: Ion-implantation process to realize buried disilicide layers in silicon; involves implantation of Co+ ions and annealing.

label: Metal-Organic Chemical Vapor Deposition
summary: CVD variant using metal-organic precursors (MOCVD).

label: Metal-Organic Chemical Vapor Deposition (MOCVD)
summary: Metal-organic CVD process used to deposit diffusion barriers (TiN) and metals (e.g., Cu, Al).

label: Metal–Organic MBE
summary: MBE variant using metal–organic precursors; also called MOMBE or CBE.

label: Metal-organic Vapor-Phase Epitaxy
summary: Film growth method used for epitaxial layer deposition.

label: metal vapor density
summary: Density of the metal vapor, typically copper, in the discharge process.

label: Metallization
summary: Metallization processing step in fabrication.

label: Metallo-organic chemical vapor deposition (MOCVD)
summary: CVD variant using metallo-organic precursors; cited as a major growth contributor.

label: Metallo-organic CVD (MOCVD)
summary: CVD process using metal-organic precursors; used for deposition of several metals and nitrides.

label: Metalorganic CVD
summary: MOCVD, a CVD variant using metalorganic precursors for epitaxial and oxide/metal films.

label: Metastable solvent epitaxy
summary: Modified LPE using a polycrystalline 3C-SiC source to grow 4H-SiC homoepitaxy.

label: Methane-based plasma
summary: Methane-based plasmas used for ICP etching; etching rates are much lower than chlorine-based plasmas for GaN.

label: Methane CO2 Reforming
summary: Endothermic process of methane oxidation by carbon dioxide to produce syngas.

label: Methane Concentration
summary: The concentration of methane in the gas source significantly impacts the growth rate of single-crystal diamonds.

label: MeV-energy implantation
summary: High-energy (MeV) implantation used to fabricate p-type diamond layers with reduced damage.

label: MeV ion implantation
summary: High-energy (MeV) ion implantation; associated with a damaged peak and suppression of displacements in the surface region.

label: MFZ-JTE (Multiple Floating Zone JTE)
summary: JTE termination using multiple floating rings to broaden dose tolerance; includes 36-zone and 72-zone variants with a total width of 450 μm.

label: MHD flow control
summary: Used for controlling shock positions in scramjet inlets.

label: micro-arc discharge
summary: A type of discharge that operates at high power and voltage, generating metal droplets and nanopowders.

label: Micro-Chipping
summary: A technique involving immersion of the substrate in a slurry of diamond particles agitated by ultrasound to embed residual diamond in the substrate.

label: micro-HCD
summary: Micro hollow cathode discharges that can operate at atmospheric pressure and generate high-density plasma.

label: Microarc Oxidation
summary: A sophisticated mechanism involving voltage pulses that induce current and electric field redistribution in an electrolyte, leading to the formation of a dielectric film and plasma bubbles.

label: Microchannel epitaxy (MCE)
summary: Dislocation-transfer control technique using mask-defined microchannels to reduce dislocations in overgrown layers.

label: Micropipe elimination strategies
summary: Major approaches: micropipe-free seed, stable growth under optimized conditions, and micropipe closing by dissociation.

label: Micropipe-free seed production
summary: Seed crystals to avoid micropipe replication; includes micropipe-free SiC platelet produced by the Lely method or micropipe-free wafer.

label: Microwave, 2.45 GGz
summary: Discharge type used for enhancement of adhesion properties of polyamide fibers.

label: Microwave Chemical Vapour Deposition (MWCVD)
summary: A technique for growing diamond films using microwave-produced plasma.

label: Microwave Discharge
summary: A plasma-chemical system for synthesizing diamond films characterized by high purity due to absence of electrodes.

label: Microwave Discharges
summary: Non-equilibrium plasma discharges used for coal conversion, characterized by high electron temperatures.

label: Microwave frequency
summary: Operating microwave frequency for plasma deposition: 2.45 GHz.

label: Microwave frequency 2.45 GHz
summary: Microwave excitation frequency used in typical low-energy-density discharges.

label: Microwave frequency 915 MHz
summary: Microwave excitation frequency used in high-power-density discharges.

label: microwave hydrogen plasma
summary: A process condition used for etching graphite to synthesize diamond thin films.

label: microwave hydrogen plasma treatment
summary: Treatment applied to diamond substrates for 30 minutes at a temperature of 600 °C.

label: microwave microdischarge
summary: A low-power micro-plasma source operating at microwave frequencies.

label: Microwave plasma-assisted chemical vapor deposition
summary: Process used to grow diamond films; referred to as MPCVD or PACVD.

label: Microwave plasma-assisted CVD
summary: Diamond films prepared using microwave plasma-assisted CVD.

label: Microwave Plasma-Assisted CVD (MPCVD)
summary: MPCVD is used for the synthesis of high-quality diamond films in methane-hydrogen reaction gas mixtures.

label: Microwave Plasma Chemical Vapor Deposition
summary: Technique used for H-termination on diamond surfaces.

label: Microwave plasma chemical vapor deposition (CVD)
summary: Growth method used for phosphorus-doped n-type diamond layers in pn junction fabrication.

label: Microwave Plasma Chemical Vapor Deposition (MPCVD)
summary: A refined method for producing synthetic diamonds using microwave energy to create plasma for diamond film growth.

label: Microwave Plasma Chemical Vapour Deposition (MPCVD)
summary: Deposition method used for diamond growth on copper.

label: microwave plasma CVD
summary: Synthesis method for low-resistivity freestanding substrates; suitable for thick film growth.

label: Microwave plasma CVD method
summary: A method used to prepare heteroepitaxial boron-doped diamond thick films on SiC substrates.

label: Microwave-plasma enhanced chemical vapor deposition (MPECVD)
summary: Widely used technique for diamond growth from the gas phase using microwave plasma to drive CVD.

label: Microwave plasma-enhanced CVD
summary: A method used for growing diamond structures in an ellipsoidal cavity reactor.

label: Microwave Plasma Enhanced CVD (MPE-CVD)
summary: A method used to grow diamond films on silicon substrates.

label: microwave plasma treatment
summary: A process that enhances the selectivity of gas-separating polymer membranes.

label: Microwave Power
summary: The power level used during the deposition process, ranging from 1800 W to 2400 W.

label: Mineralizer Concentration
summary: Concentration of mineralizers (KOH, KF, KBr, NaOH) affects particle size and morphology; higher concentrations often lead to larger or differently shaped particles.

label: Minimal Fab approach
summary: Minimal fab processing approach in device fabrication.

label: Minimum reaction temperature for SrTiO3/BaTiO3
summary: Minimum reaction temperature for SrTiO3 and BaTiO3 synthesis (90 C).

label: miscut_angle
summary: Vicinal Si substrate miscut angle to promote step-flow growth.

label: Mismatch strain
summary: Normalized mismatch strain (²_m) controlling bifurcation and curvature changes

label: Mismatch strain (2_m)
summary: Elastic strain due to lattice parameter difference between film and substrate; defined as 2_m = (a_s - a_f)/a_f or variations thereof.

label: Mismatch strain (ε_m)
summary: Elastic mismatch strain between film and substrate due to lattice parameter differences (or thermal expansion), used in curvature calculations.

label: mismatch strain tensor ε^m_{ij}
summary: spatially uniform mismatch strain in island and intervening layer; traction prevents relaxation

label: Mismatch stress
summary: Stress relative to interfacial strength driving buckling and delamination.

label: Mixed technique
summary: A combination of techniques for diamond film growth, involving carbon deposition and ultrasonic seeding.

label: Mixed techniques
summary: Processes that combine different methods to enhance nucleation sites.

label: MOCVD
summary: Metalorganic chemical vapor deposition; a low-temperature, near-irreversible epitaxial-growth process compared to earlier CVD methods.

label: MOCVD deposition
summary: Metal-Organic Chemical Vapor Deposition used to form mixed oxide SiO2-CeO2 on GaN diodes.

label: MOCVD HM2 (highly-mismatched) heteroepitaxy
summary: Growth of highly mismatched heteroepitaxy; challenges include high dislocation density and residual stress.

label: MOCVD (Metal-Organic Chemical Vapor Deposition)
summary: Selective epitaxy approach using organometallic precursors; GaCl adsorption behavior on masks influences selectivity.

label: MOCVD (Metalorganic Chemical Vapor Deposition)
summary: Metalorganic CVD processes using volatile organometallic precursors to deposit compound semiconductors.

label: MOCVD via pyrolysis of dialkylamide
summary: Lower deposition temperature (500–800°C) via MOCVD using a dialkylamide pyrolysis.

label: Moderate-Pressure Microwave Discharge
summary: A discharge that generates non-equilibrium plasma with optimal parameters at moderate pressures.

label: moderate-pressure RF-CCP discharges
summary: Discharges sustained at pressures between 1–100 Torr with specific characteristics.

label: Modified Lely method
summary: Growth method with higher supersaturation due to temperature gradient; more nucleation sites.

label: Modified Lely method (seeded sublimation)
summary: Seeded-sublmation technique for SiC growth (modified Lely method) enabling SiC layer growth.

label: Modified Lely method (seeded sublimation growth)
summary: Seeded sublimation growth technique to suppress spontaneous nucleation and enable controlled growth on a seed; operates in Argon with 1800–2600°C and high temperature gradients; yields high seed quality.

label: Modulation Spectroscopy
summary: Spectroscopy methods using modulation (e.g., electroreflectance, piezoreflectance) to study optical properties.

label: Molecular Beam Allotaxy (MBA)
summary: Process to form buried heteroepitaxial layers by controlled coevaporation and annealing.

label: Molecular Beam Epitaxy
summary: Large-scale technique for depositing semiconductor/multilayer structures using effusion and gas injector sources; includes GSMBE and MOMBE.

label: Molecular beam epitaxy at 550 C
summary: Deposition method and temperature for growth in relaxation studies (MBE at ~550 C).

label: Molecular Beam Epitaxy (MBE)
summary: Deposition technique used to grow SiGe/Si(001) films; provides in situ observation capabilities in LEEM studies.

label: Molecular dynamics simulations
summary: Theoretical technique that numerically integrates classical equations of motion for a set of particles with a specified force law; used to study surface melting.

label: Molten KOH etching
summary: Etching technique used prior to epitaxial growth to influence defects.

label: MOMBE
summary: Hybrid method combining molecular beam epitaxy with chemical deposition.

label: MOMBE (Molecular-Organic Molecular Beam Epitaxy)
summary: Molecular beam epitaxy growth method referenced for Si homoepitaxy; transition from reaction- to flux-limited growth around 600°C.

label: Momentum relaxation time (τ_m)
summary: Momentum relaxation time; inverse of the momentum relaxation rate: <dk/dt> = k(τ_m)^{-1} = ∑_{k'≠k} (k'−k) P(k,k').

label: Monochromatic light illumination
summary: Use of monochromatic light to generate free charge carriers for photoconductivity measurements.

label: Monte Carlo simulation
summary: Numerical technique to sample lattice configurations according to Boltzmann distribution.

label: Monte-Carlo simulations
summary: Monte Carlo simulations used with a lattice gas Hamiltonian to model adsorption/dissociation on surfaces.

label: MoO3 surface transfer doping of diamond
summary: Surface transfer doping of diamond using MoO3.

label: MOS channel length comparison (L_CH)
summary: Channel length in UMOSFET and DMOSFET; UMOSFET channel length is longer than DMOSFET channel length.

label: Mott transition
summary: Occurs in boron-doped diamond at an acceptor density of about 3 × 10^20 atoms cm^−3.

label: MOVPE
summary: Metalorganic vapor-phase epitaxy; alternative epitaxial deposition method for III-V/II-VI compounds.

label: MOVPE (Metalorganic vapor-phase epitaxy)
summary: Alternative growth method for GaN mentioned in the document.

label: MPCVD
summary: Microwave Plasma Chemical Vapor Deposition, a technique used for the synthesis of homoepitaxial single-crystal diamonds.

label: MPCVD experimental parameters for BEN nucleation
summary: Experimental conditions for the BEN nucleation process including microwave power, pressure, temperature, bias voltage, methane concentration, and duration.

label: MPCVD method
summary: A method for diamond film preparation that has reached maturity and stability.

label: MPCVD technique
summary: A method employed for the synthesis of homoepitaxial single-crystal diamonds.

label: MPECVD
summary: Microwave-plasma-enhanced chemical vapor deposition process used for diamond deposition.

label: Multiple-Floating-Zone Junction Termination Extension (MFZ-JTE)
summary: JTE termination realized by distributing dose across discrete implanted zones (zones).

label: Multiple Floating Zone (MFZ) / Space-modulated (SM) JTE
summary: Edge termination using multiple floating zones or space-modulated junction termination extension.

label: MW activated hydrocarbon/H2 gas mixtures
summary: Gas mixtures used in the diamond CVD process that reveal efficient conversion of hydrocarbons.

label: MWCVD
summary: Most widely used technique for ultrathin film deposition, utilizing microwaves to generate glow discharge in a reaction chamber.

label: MWPD
summary: Microwave power densities used during deposition.

label: MWPD (microwave power density)
summary: Ratio of injected microwave power to plasma volume; higher MWPD increases growth rate.

label: MWPECVD
summary: A method used for depositing boron-doped diamond coatings.

label: N*
summary: Equilibrium concentration of stable nuclei (alternate notation).

label: n_0
summary: Adsorption site density on the substrate.

label: n_em
summary: Exponent in Black's electromigration equation; varies with geometry (e.g., 2 for wide Al lines).

label: N_i
summary: Critical cluster size density (i).

label: n_s
summary: Number of surface sites.

label: N-type doping
summary: Doping process for (001)-oriented single-crystalline diamond using phosphorus.

label: N2 flow
summary: Flow of nitrogen gas during the growth of N-UNCD films, measured in sccm.

label: N2 injections
summary: Nitrogen gas injections used during the CVD growth process.

label: N2O nitridation
summary: Nitridation of oxide films grown on SiC to modify interface properties.

label: nano-second pulse DBD
summary: Achieved strong effects at high speeds due to ion wind and shock wave formation.

label: Nano-seeding
summary: A nucleation method involving the deposition of a layer of diamond nano-particles onto the substrate surface.

label: Nanoparticle suspension dip time
summary: A variable process parameter affecting the crystalline quality of diamond films.

label: Narrow base
summary: Base width much shorter than minority carrier diffusion length; used for simplifications.

label: NbN CVD reaction (NbCl4 + NH3 + 1/2 H2)
summary: NbN formed by NbCl4 + NH3 + 1/2 H2 → NbN + 4 HCl (excess H2).

label: Near-edge x-ray absorption fine structure spectroscopy
